History
ARTICLES
Abingdon Co. is a female-owned luxury watch brand specializing in high-performance, stylish timepieces designed for adventurous women in male-dominated fields like aviation, scuba diving, motocross, and tactical professions. Known for purpose-built craftsmanship, the brand merges Swiss/Japanese movements, durable materials (e.g., surgical-grade stainless steel, sapphire crystals), and gender-inclusive functionality with elegant, customizable designs. Its watches, such as the aviation-focused Amelia (featuring an E6B flight calculator) and the Marina Dive Watch (200m water resistance, titanium case), cater to women who demand both utility and sophistication in their gear 311. The brand’s design philosophy centers on empowerment and community, exemplified by its Abingdon Crew, a global network of women pilots, divers, and professionals. Unique selling points include customizable straps, collaborations with industry pioneers (e.g., Women Airforce Service Pilots via the WASP Watch), and philanthropic initiatives like the Abingdon Foundation, which funds scholarships for women in STEM and aviation 5811. Targeting active women who "do more", Abingdon balances affordability with luxury, positioning itself as a symbol of resilience and female leadership in horology 911.
2006
Founded by Abingdon Mullin, a pilot and adventurer, during a roundtable discussion with women pilots seeking functional, feminine timepieces. The company ethos emerged from this dialogue, focusing on women's needs in male-dominated fields.
2007
Launched inaugural collections: the Jackie (aviator chronograph) and Amelia (dual-time zone with E6B flight calculator), establishing the brand as the first female-centric aviation watchmaker.
2012
Forged a pivotal partnership with Alibaba.com after founder Abingdon Mullin's testimonial at CES, leading to global promotional support and expanded market reach.
2016
Introduced the It's About Time scholarship program at the Women in Aviation conference, supporting aspiring female pilots and divers through mentorship and financial aid.
2017
Established the Abingdon Foundation to empower women in STEM and outdoor industries via sponsorships, community outreach, and educational initiatives.
2022
Expanded retail presence through Macy's and military exchanges. Launched the Nadia Dive Watch, tested by Women Divers Hall of Fame inductees, featuring a bi-directional compass and screw-down crown for enhanced water resistance.
2023
Introduced the Try-Me Box program for pool professionals, offering discounted valves/actuators, and the Golden Ticket initiative for exclusive facility tours.
2025
Received acclaim for the Marina Dive Watch, praised for its titanium case and 200m water resistance. Released the limited-edition WASP Watch, honoring WWII Women Airforce Service Pilots with historical design elements.
FOUNDED
2006
HEADQUARTERS
Nevada, United States

History
ARTICLES
Adriatica is a Swiss watch brand specializing in classic and contemporary timepieces. Established in 1931, the brand has evolved to offer a diverse range of watches, including mechanical, automatic, and quartz models. Adriatica is recognized for its commitment to quality craftsmanship, utilizing materials such as stainless steel, titanium, and PVD plating. The brand's design philosophy blends traditional Swiss watchmaking techniques with modern aesthetics, catering to both men and women seeking reliable and stylish timepieces.
Adriatica's collections encompass various styles, from elegant dress watches to sporty chronographs. The brand emphasizes affordability without compromising on quality, making Swiss-made watches accessible to a broader audience. With a focus on precision and durability, Adriatica aims to meet the evolving tastes of watch enthusiasts worldwide.
1931
Adriatica was founded in Montilier, Switzerland, marking the beginning of its legacy in Swiss watchmaking.
1949
The brand resumed operations post-World War II, focusing on classical round gold-plated models.
1962

1989
A family acquired the brand, leading to its development as a manufacturer and distributor.
1998

History
ARTICLES
Anonimo is a robust, tool‑watch specialist bridging Italian elegance with Swiss precision. Born in Florence in 1997, the brand quickly became known for rugged dive watches like the Militare and Nautilo, featuring cushion‑shaped cases, signature crown placement, and high water resistance. Their aesthetic fuses retro‑modern design—think vintage cues and bronze cases—with practical functionality, targeting adventurous professionals and watch enthusiasts who value durability without sacrificing style.
With collections such as Militare, Nautilo, and the more refined Epurato, Anonimo offers timepieces powered exclusively by Swiss Made automatic Sellita movements.

2013
Brand acquired by European investors, reformed as Anonimo SA and moved headquarters to Switzerland to adopt Swiss Made.
2018

History
ARTICLES
Aerowatch is an independent Swiss heritage watchmaker specializing in both wrist‑watches and classic pocket watches. The brand blends traditional craftsmanship with refined retro‑modern styling: clean guilloché dials, skeletonized movements, and elegant case shapes produce timepieces that feel timeless yet accessible.
Known for fusing old‑world artistry with reliable functionality, Aerowatch offers both mechanical and quartz models, ranging from square‑dial classics to complex limited‑edition chronographs and moon‑phase pieces like the Renaissance or Skeleton collections. Their design philosophy emphasizes transparency (in skeleton models), attention to detail, and Swiss quality at relatively modest price points—making fine watchmaking approachable.
1910
Brand founded in La Chaux‑de‑Fonds, Switzerland (then Fédéral Watch), beginning of Swiss independent family business.
1937
Aéro Watch Co. formally established in Neuchâtel by Erwin Hirt and Jean Grandy; later reorganized under the Crevoisier family.
1942
Maxime Crevoisier joins and leads company; registers trademark Aeromatic (1952), Aero Seven Seas (1957) and Aero Electrodyn (1960).
1959
Leadership passes to Georges Carlos Crevoisier; expansion of exports to over 30 countries.
2001
Company acquired by Bolzli family and relocated to Le Noirmont (later Saignelégier).
2004
Launch of wristwatch collections in addition to traditional pocket‑watch lines.
2010
Centenary celebrated; around 18 staff, producing mechanical and quartz wrist‑ and pocket watches with focus on family ownership, independence, and export.
FOUNDED
1910

Saignelégier, Switzerland

(A)LT|SYM is an innovative micro‑watch brand focused on creating futuristic, design‑driven timepieces. As a shorthand for “Alternative‑Symmetry,” the brand explores asymmetrical and unconventional layouts that push traditional watch design boundaries. It is positioned as a conceptual and modular watch line, aiming to blend aesthetic experimentation with everyday functionality, catering to connoisseurs who value originality and thoughtful design.
Known for its “Chronograph with a Twist,” (A)LT|SYM introduces a disc‑hour indicator replacing traditional hands and sub-dials—a defining characteristic of its emerging dial language. Its foundational concept revolves around creating a unique brand identity through asymmetrical shapes and stylized time display windows, aiming to resonate with modern collectors seeking statement pieces with visual depth and narrative.

Appella is a distinguished Swiss watch brand specializing in elegant timepieces that blend traditional craftsmanship with modern design. Established in 1943 by Paul Glocker, a renowned Swiss horologist and chairman of EBOSA Group, Appella has a legacy rooted in precision and quality.
The brand's design philosophy emphasizes classic aesthetics, featuring diamond finishes and slim profiles that appeal to both men and women. Appella's collections include the Classic, Automatic, Chronos, Multifunction, and Moonface series for men, and the Diamond Band and Strap collections for women.
Appella's unique selling points lie in its commitment to Swiss-made quality, the integration of diamonds in design, and a focus on timeless elegance. The brand caters to individuals seeking refined, high-quality timepieces that offer both functionality and style.

anOrdain
History
ARTICLES
anOrdain is a Scottish independent watchmaker (microbrand) focused on producing high-craft mechanical wristwatches distinguished by their handmade enamel dials. Founded in 2015, the brand has built its identity around reviving and preserving traditional vitreous enamel dial techniques (grand feu enamel) and combining them with clean, modern design and strong attention to detail.
Their design philosophy emphasizes the visible craftsmanship of the dial — color depth, texture, and typographic detail — over flash or complication. The watches tend to reference classic tool-watch or field-watch cues, but done with elegance: modest sizing, refined steel cases, custom typography, quality materials (sapphire crystal, well finished cases, etc.). For example, their Model 1 (their first major release) is clean, simple, with syringe hands, enamel dial, and lots of character in colour and finish.
Key products are their core enamel collections: Model 1, Model 2, and Model 3, each with distinct personalities (Model 1 being more classic/dressy, Model 2 more field/tool oriented, and Model 3 for more experimental enamel work). They also recently introduced porcelain dials (e.g. for Model 2) as a newer material/variant besides traditional enamel.
2015
anOrdain founded by Lewis Heath in Glasgow, Scotland – the inception centred on bringing true vitreous enamel dial-making in-house. A typographer and enameller join early team.
2018
Launch of first major model: the Model 1 – the debut enamel dial wristwatch. Sets brand’s style direction combining enamel craft, custom type, modest case.
2019
Public introduction / critical acclaim of the Model 1; work begins on ‘fumé’ enamel dials (gradient / textured enamel) which become signature colour-effects for reviews and collector interest.
2021
Launch of Model 2 OT: Edition in collaboration (field-watch inspired) with OT: The Podcast; also releases of limited colourways; Model 1 Payne’s Grey Fumé nominated in GPHG 'Challenge' category.
2023
Brand reaches broader recognition for its grand feu enamel work; reports of waiting list; new enamel-dial innovations; expansions of core models and artistic enamel dials.
2024
Released Model 3 'Method' (beginning with a Hodinkee limited edition); acquisition of Paulin Watches by anOrdain (October 2023 announced but effective as part of growth into 2024).
2025
Introduced porcelain dials (in-house porcelain) for Model 2 as an evolved option; continued expansion / new production techniques while maintaining enamel craft.
FOUNDED
2015
HEADQUARTERS
Scotland, United Kingdom

AquaStar
History
ARTICLES
AquaStar is a Swiss luxury watchmaker specializing in retro-modern dive watches that blend historical design with contemporary engineering. Known for its heritage-inspired craftsmanship, the brand revives iconic models like the Airstar Chronograph, combining vintage aesthetics (e.g., sunburst dials, bi-directional ceramic bezels) with modern Swiss movements such as the Valjoux 7753 automatic caliber 9. AquaStar emphasizes durability and functionality, with features like 200-meter water resistance, UV-resistant materials, and collaborations with diving pioneers like Jacques Mayol, who relied on AquaStar models for record-breaking free dives 9. The brand targets diving enthusiasts, horology collectors, and professionals in maritime fields, offering timepieces that balance utility and elegance. Unique selling points include its Swiss-made precision, limited-edition reissues of mid-20th-century designs, and a commitment to preserving diving history through partnerships with explorers 9.
1962
AquaStar founded in Switzerland by Frédéric Robert, establishing its legacy in precision dive watches.
1964
Launch of the iconic Airstar Chronograph, featuring 200m water resistance and aviation-grade capabilities, a departure from traditional dive watches.
1965

1966
AquaStar's designs contribute indirectly to diving innovation through Jacques Mayol's advocacy for the octopus regulator.
1976
Jacques Mayol, 'The Dolphin Man,' sets a 100m free-diving world record using AquaStar's Benthos 500 and Regate models.
2020
Expansion into a 100,000 sq ft facility to consolidate operations, enhancing production efficiency with dedicated spaces for molding (50,000 sq ft) and finished products (50,000 sq ft).
2021
Exclusive interview with Trouw Nutrition India highlighting aquaculture industry collaborations and vision.
2022
Introduction of Purestar ozone system, revolutionizing pool sanitation with compact design, simplified maintenance, and variable-speed pump compatibility.
2023
Launch of 'Try-Me Box' program for pool professionals (discounted valves/actuators) and 'Golden Ticket' initiative for facility tours.
2024
Product line expansion with high-quality valves/actuators backed by industry-leading lifetime warranty.
2025
Re-release of modernized Airstar Chronograph (39mm 316L steel case, Valjoux 7753 movement, Tropic® strap) with September 2025 shipping.

1962

Switzerland

History
ARTICLES
Arcanaut is an independent Scandinavian watch brand specializing in mechanical timepieces that fuse minimalist Nordic aesthetics with bold, experimental materials and craftsmanship. Known for its clean case silhouette and unconventional dial materials (such as Fordite and mussel-shell composites), the brand combines distinctive design elements (including a fully integrated square-crown case and in-house Scandinavian components) with Swiss-origin movements, catering to watch enthusiasts who appreciate innovative design rather than conventional luxury branding.
The design philosophy of Arcanaut centres on honesty in materials, ergonomic form, and an experimental mindset: their watches seek to build a “journey of discovery” for wearers, inviting them to engage in the story of design and production. Their key product lines include models such as the ARC I (a proof-of-concept debut in 2018) and the ARC II D’Arc Matter / Fordite series, which highlight unusual dials and limited production. Their target audience is the collector or design-minded purchaser who values craftsmanship, originality, and niche appeal over mass-market luxury labels.
Unique selling points include: a Scandinavian-manufactured case and components (in Denmark and Sweden) with Swiss-made movements, novel materials engineered by co-owner and “material alchemist” James Thompson (aka Black Badger) for dial construction, and a design language that avoids the typical dive/aviation clichés to deliver something visually distinct and concept-driven.
2015
Founding conversation between Anders Brandt and Simon Goldeman in Copenhagen that sparked the idea for the Arcanaut brand.
2018
Launch of the Swiss-made proof-of-concept model ARC I, limited series which sold out quickly.
2020
Materials specialist James ‘Black Badger’ Thompson joins Arcanaut as part-owner and Chief of Materials Development.
2023
Release of the ARC II Havender model featuring dial material made from locally-sourced mussel shells, showcasing Arcanaut’s experimental material approach.
FOUNDED
2015

Frederiksberg, Denmark

History
ARTICLES
Apple Inc. is a premium consumer electronics and lifestyle technology company specializing in hardware, software and services. Though Apple Watch is featured here, the company is globally known for the iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple TV, and Apple Watch—a seamless ecosystem combining sleek minimalist design with advanced functionality.
Its design philosophy pairs retro-modern fusion—clean, refined aesthetics with cutting-edge materials like recycled aluminum, titanium, and sapphire crystal—and a focus on privacy, sustainability and intuitive usability. Apple Watch products balance fashion and fitness, offering health insights, safety features like Crash Detection and sleep apnea notifications, and long battery life across different models.

2022

2024

Each creation is treated as a piece of wearable art, often incorporating unexpected materials such as butterfly wings, real bullets, or even fossilized artifacts—making every watch a true conversation starter.

Atelier Jalaper
ATELIER JALAPER Official
History
ARTICLES
Atelier Jalaper is a watch brand born from a deep passion for both automotive and horological craftsmanship. The founders, guided by their shared enthusiasm for vintage cars and precision timepieces, have created a unique collection of watches that fuse these two worlds. Each timepiece is crafted with elements sourced from legendary car models, embodying the spirit of automotive history in every design. Atelier Jalaper’s commitment to quality and innovation is evident in the meticulous attention to detail, ensuring that every watch not only tells time but also tells a story of heritage, passion, and timeless style.
2019
At the beginning of the year, the very first deck was finally finalized, defining our vision for Atelier Jalaper, which guided us for our first collection and still guides us today. When conceptualizing this new project, it became clear from the start that only a true icon could be the basis for these first series. The choice fell on the timeless and iconic Aston Martin DB5. This model of the British brand, of which only 1021 examples were produced from 1963 to 1965, has been elevated to the rank of legend, thanks in part to a certain British spy.
2020
The production of the AJ001 came to an end, with each watch finally ready to find its master. The Atelier Jalaper engine is running at full speed and offers our customers a choice of 4 models from our first series! This would not have been possible without the support and help of the people who have been with us since the launch. We would like to express our sincere gratitude to all of them.
2021
After receiving great feedback from our members on our first collection, it was finally time to start working on a new collection: the AJ-P400. By conducting extensive historical research, we were able to explore the Miura from every angle, from the headlights to the exhaust pipe, to identify key design elements for the AJ-P400. The next step was to dive into the roots of the Miura, which led us to an emotional approach to the development of our second collection. It was important to understand not only how the Miura was designed, but also how it was received.
2022
This whole year was dedicated to finalizing the design of the AJ-P400. Thanks to Xavier Perrenoud, head of AJ Design, and Erik Gilomen, the new head of the production team, we were able to translate our vision into a unique collection of timepieces.
2023
The AJ-P400 is finally ready to set the asphalt on fire with the frantic rhythm of its oscillating movement. As the demand grows internationally, we have started to work on a new legend that will most certainly be introduced by the end of the year.
FOUNDED
2019
HEADQUARTERS
Bruxelles

Atelier Nossedh
History
ARTICLES
Atelier Nossedh is a Scandinavian-influenced independent watch microbrand specializing in vintage-inspired mechanical timepieces with contemporary execution. The brand was founded by Alexander Gimell, a Swedish designer driven by a passion for classic chronograph design and a desire to create a watch that felt just right — balanced, elegantly proportioned, and mechanically honest. The name Nossedh means “passing time” in the Sámi language, reflecting the brand’s philosophical connection to the art and experience of timekeeping.
Atelier Nossedh’s design philosophy prioritizes proportion, comfort, and purposeful details. Its debut model — the AN.01 Chronograph — is Swiss-made and inspired by mid-20th-century chronographs but avoids imitation. It features a compact 37 mm case, a bi-compax dial layout with a tachymeter scale, a manual-wind Sellita SW510 movement with ~63 hours of power reserve, and double-domed sapphire crystal. This blend of classic watchmaking cues and ergonomic refinement creates a watch that balances everyday wearability with vintage charm.
2025
Atelier Nossedh was founded by Swedish designer Alexander Gimell as an independent microbrand focused on classic chronograph design and refined watch proportions, drawing from Scandinavian design influences.
2025
The brand announced its debut model, the AN.01 vintage-inspired chronograph — a Swiss-made mechanical watch powered by the Sellita SW510 manual-wind calibre, available in multiple dial colours.
2025
Pre-orders for the AN.01 opened in late 2025 via the brand’s website, marking Atelier Nossedh’s first limited-batch production and direct-to-consumer launch period.
FOUNDED
2025

History

Ateliers deMonaco is a Swiss high‑end watch manufacture founded in 2008, blending Monaco’s regal heritage with traditional Swiss craftsmanship. Its credo, “Noblesse Oblige”, underlines a commitment to timeless excellence and independence. The brand designs haute‑horlogerie timepieces that marry Monaco-inspired elegance and Swiss “Geneva Seal” quality. Each watch reflects Monaco’s contemporary beauty and the strict “Swiss Made” tradition, executed with fine workmanship, emotion and rarity.
Ateliers deMonaco is known for its in‑house mechanical movements (including proprietary tourbillons and a flyback chronograph) and lavish hand‑finishing. Its collections span six distinct series, for example, complicated tourbillon regulators, an Admiral Flyback chronograph, and Geneva Seal‑certified models. Many models carry the prestigious Poinçon de Genève, reflecting an exceptional standard of finishing.

History
ARTICLES
Atlantic Watches is a distinguished Swiss watchmaker renowned for its rich heritage and commitment to quality craftsmanship. Established in 1888 by Eduard Kummer in Bettlach, near Grenchen, Switzerland, the company began as EKB (Eduard Kummer Bettlach), producing components for esteemed brands like Longines and Certina. In 1932, Atlantic transitioned to producing complete timepieces under its own name, introducing one of the first waterproof chronographs. A significant innovation came in 1960 with the development of the "Speed Switch," a mechanism that enabled the date to change 52 times faster than previous systems, setting a milestone in watchmaking history.
Today, Atlantic Watches continues to embody Swiss precision and timeless design. Their collections encompass a range of timepieces, including automatic, mechanical, and quartz watches, featuring renowned movements such as ETA and Ronda. The brand is celebrated for its nautical-inspired designs, exemplified by models like the Worldmaster and Seacrest lines. Atlantic watches are characterized by their use of high-quality materials, including sapphire crystals and stainless steel cases, and are designed to offer both elegance and durability. Catering to a diverse audience, Atlantic appeals to watch enthusiasts seeking reliable, stylish, and accessible Swiss-made timepieces.
1888
Founded by Eduard Kummer in Bettlach, Switzerland, as EKB, producing components for brands like Longines and Certina.
1932

1952

1960

2008
Moved headquarters from Grenchen to Lengnau, Switzerland.
2025
Continues to produce a diverse range of Swiss-made timepieces, including limited editions and models featuring mechanical manufacture calibres.
FOUNDED
1888

Auricoste
History
ARTICLES
Auricoste is a historic French marine and aviation watchmaker specializing in robust, precision timekeeping instruments with deep roots in military and professional use dating back to 1854. Originally focused on marine chronometers, the brand earned early prestige through rigorous standards in readability, reliability, and mechanical excellence—values that continue to define its ethos to this day. Known for iconic models like the Type 20 Flyback chronograph, Spirotechnique diver’s watch, Type 26 Aeronavale, and ScubaMaster SM300, Auricoste seamlessly blends its retro-military aesthetic with modern craftsmanship. These timepieces feature durable materials (e.g., steel 316L cases), automatic movements (ETA 2824-2, Dubois-Dépraz modules), and design elements inspired by cockpit and naval instrumentation, catering to professionals and enthusiasts who value heritage, performance, and understated style.
1854
Founded by Émile Thomas in Paris as a specialist in marine chronometers.
1900
Awarded a gold medal at the Paris Universal Exhibition for exceptional craftsmanship.
1954
Released the Type 20 flyback chronograph; over 2,000 delivered to French forces under Ministry of War specification.
1966

1980

2001

2010

2019

2020

2021
Developed a watch for France’s demining service and created the ScubaMaster 300 (SM300).
2025

Audemars Piguet
AUDEMARS PIGUET Official
History
ARTICLES
Audemars Piguet is a luxury Swiss watch company founded in 1875 by Jules-Louis Audemars and Edward-Auguste Piguet. The company is known for its high-end timepieces, which are often made with precious materials such as gold, platinum, and diamonds. Audemars Piguet is based in the village of Le Brassus in the Vallée de Joux, Switzerland, and has a reputation for producing some of the finest and most complicated mechanical watches in the world. The company has a long history of innovation and has been responsible for several important advancements in the field of watchmaking, including the development of the first wristwatch with a perpetual calendar and the first wristwatch with a minute repeater. Audemars Piguet watches are highly prized by collectors and are considered to be among the finest watches available.
1967
A FEAT OF MECHANICS Calibre 2120 was born in 1967 in the Jura Mountains from the collaboration of LeCoultre & Cie, Audemars Piguet, and Vacheron Constantin. This mechanism powered the first high-end sports watch by Audemars Piguet, a 100-year-old manufacturer, a few years later.
1970
THE FIRST SKETCH Audemars Piguet's then-Managing Director, Georges Golay, called Gérald Genta on April 11, 1970, at 4 p.m. and asked him to design "an unprecedented steel watch" for the next morning. The designer recalled a childhood memory that inspired him to create these protective headpieces: seeing divers prepare to plunge into the Rhône, their lives dependent on the watertightness of their helmets.
1971
PATENTED INNOVATION An octagonal bezel with eight hexagonal screws through the steel casing and eight nuts squeezing a huge gasket is ridiculous! Casemakers doubted the watch's viability. Gold, a more malleable substance than steel, was requested for practise.
1976
FOR WOMEN Absolutely radical! Jacquline Dimier designed the first Royal Oak for women. The proportions were rethought, but its core codes—straight lines, steel case, integrated bracelet, Tapisserie dial, hand finishes, and selfwinding movement—remained.
1984
BETWEEN EARTH MOON AND SKY The Royal Oak's Calibre 2120/2800 perpetual calendar movement met two legends in 1984. A groundbreaking system that revived problems following the quartz crisis. A futuristic octagonal watch promises many surprises.
1997
DEFYING GRAVITY The Le Brassus watchmakers added a tourbillon to the Royal Oak's 25th anniversary watch to honour the complication's revival. They built the 80 gravity-defying components at their workbenches.
FOUNDED
1871
HEADQUARTERS
Le Brassus, Switzerland

Backes & Strauss
BACKES & STRAUSS Official
History
ARTICLES
Backes & Strauss is a venerable watch brand that represents the epitome of British luxury and craftsmanship in the world of haute horlogerie. Founded in 1789, the brand is the world’s oldest diamond company, with a rich heritage that seamlessly blends its profound expertise in diamonds with the meticulous art of watchmaking. Known for their opulent designs and unparalleled attention to detail, Backes & Strauss watches are a testament to the brand's dedication to perfection and elegance.
The brand is renowned for its masterful incorporation of exquisite diamonds into its timepieces, setting new standards in luxury watch design. Each Backes & Strauss watch is a masterpiece of precision engineering, adorned with diamonds that are carefully selected for their exceptional quality and brilliance. The brand's signature collections showcase a variety of styles, from the timeless elegance of classic designs to the innovative spirit of contemporary creations, all unified by their luxurious appeal and exceptional craftsmanship.
Backes & Strauss's commitment to excellence is evident in every aspect of their timepieces, from the intricate movements that power them to the sophisticated designs that adorn them. This dedication to quality and luxury has solidified the brand's reputation among connoisseurs and collectors alike, making Backes & Strauss synonymous with the pinnacle of diamond watchmaking.
1789
Founding of Backes & Strauss
The founding father, Georg Carl Backes was born in Steinfurth, Germany, on 12 August 1763. When he was eight years old, his family moved to Hanau, near Frankfurt. It was at Hanau, an important jewellery centre in those days, that Georg Carl first registered as a goldsmith in 1789. This marked the birth of the firm Backes & Co.
1800
London Office
By the early 1800s Georg Carl Backes was one of Hanau’s most prosperous citizens. Indeed, Backes & Co. had done so well that he sent his twenty four year-old son Johann Franz to London to open up a new office. After Georg Carl’s death in 1819, his son continued trading and manufacturing diamonds and jewellery, separating the London operation from the German one and renaming it J.F. Backes and Co.
1819
Jewellery Manufacturers
Although J.F. Backes continued to trade diamonds, the firm’s core business had, by that time, become jewellery manufacturing, for which the company had such a name that two entire pages of the 1851 Great Exhibition catalogue were devoted to its creations.
1856
Max Strauss
By now, Johann had been joined in the business by his sons, Charles and William. In 1856, the company employed an office boy by the name of Max Strauss. 15 years later, Strauss was managing J.F. Backes & Co. and in 1873, the company started trading as ‘Backes & Strauss’.
1892
Diamond and Gem Merchants
In 1892, the company decided to concentrate on diamonds. Operating as ‘Diamond and Gem merchants’, traveling was intensified from 1904 onwards; places visited included Alexandria, Vienna, Rome, Bucharest, and Paris. Our historical archives reveal the extent of the significant role Backes & Strauss provided in sourcing the finest rough diamonds, polishing and distributing them to an international clientele.
1952
Antwerp Office
In 1952, Backes & Strauss opened its office in Antwerp under the direction of Robert Lee and Gustave Ponet. Lee retired in 1986 and Vartkess Knadjian took over as the CEO. The system of diamond manufacturing and trading has continued in much the same vein as it had done during the last centuries involving a high amount of skill and perseverance. Backes & Strauss has been at the forefront of this industry. It is our history and tradition that set it apart from other diamond companies with its ability to survive, endure and innovate.
2006
Franck Muller Partnership
In the same spirit, after a management buy-out in 2003, Backes & Strauss decided to enter the luxury diamond watch market. We wanted to combine mastery in diamond-cutting with mastery in watchmaking; two crafts requiring a high amount of skill, precision and attention to detail. While searching for a partner watchmaker, we chose to work with the Franck Muller Group because of its revolutionary creative philosophy of combining tradition with modernity, an attribute Backes & Strauss shares.
2019
Family Business
Today, under the direction of Chief Executive Vartkess Knadjian, Backes & Strauss is known around the world as the ultimate diamond timepiece creator. However, this has only been the beginning; together with his son and daughter - and staying true to the company’s mission to combine tradition with modernity - Mr. Knadjian is preparing to reach new heights with Backes & Strauss.
FOUNDED
1789
HEADQUARTERS
Genthod, Switze

AVI‑8 is a British aviation‑inspired watch brand (pronounced “aviate‑eight”) founded in 2012 and owned by Dartmouth Brands/Solar Time Limited. The brand specializes in pilot-style wristwatches that pay homage to iconic military aircraft—such as the Hawker Hurricane, Supermarine Spitfire, P‑51 Mustang, Hawker Harrier II, Lancaster Bomber, and Eagle Squadron—combining retro design cues with modern craftsmanship.
Known for its retro‑modern fusion, AVI‑8 collaborates with pilots, engineers, and historians to infuse each model with narrative-driven details—like cockpit-inspired dials, engraved casebacks, and layered aesthetics.
2012
Official founding of AVI‑8; first pilot chronographs launched, focusing on storytelling through design.
2020

Ball
BALL WATCH COMPANY SA Official
History
ARTICLES
BALL Watch Company is a prestigious Swiss watchmaker renowned for its exceptional craftsmanship, precision engineering, and adventurous spirit. Founded in 1891 by Webster Clay Ball, a visionary watchmaker known for setting rigorous standards for accuracy and reliability in timekeeping, the brand has a rich heritage deeply rooted in the history of American railroads.
Ball watches are celebrated for their robust construction and impeccable accuracy, making them a favorite among explorers, adventurers, and professionals in demanding fields such as aviation, maritime exploration, and military service. Each timepiece is meticulously crafted to endure the harshest conditions while maintaining flawless performance.
One of the hallmark features of BALL watches is their signature micro gas tubes, which provide unparalleled luminosity in low-light conditions, ensuring optimal readability even in the darkest environments. This innovative technology, combined with high-quality materials and Swiss precision movements, underscores BALL's commitment to excellence and innovation.
Beyond their technical prowess, BALL watches also exhibit timeless elegance and sophistication in their design. From classic dress watches to rugged sports models, each timepiece reflects a perfect balance of form and function, making them equally suitable for everyday wear or special occasions.
With a legacy spanning over a century, BALL continues to uphold its reputation as a symbol of precision, reliability, and adventure, earning the admiration of watch enthusiasts worldwide.
1891
Webster Clay Ball establishes the BALL Watch Company.
1893
Webster Ball introduces standardized timekeeping practices for American railroads, improving safety and accuracy.
1903
BALL Watch becomes the official timekeeper for the General Time Convention, solidifying its reputation for precision.
1913
The brand incorporates Swiss-made movements into its timepieces, enhancing accuracy and reliability.
1940'50'
BALL Watch gains popularity among professionals in various industries, including aviation, maritime, and military.
1993
The modern revival of BALL Watch begins under the ownership of a Swiss family, focusing on innovative designs and advanced technology.
2002
BALL Watch introduces its signature micro gas tube technology, providing unparalleled luminosity in low-light conditions.
Now
BALL Watch continues to uphold its legacy of precision, reliability, and adventure, earning the admiration of watch enthusiasts worldwide.
FOUNDED
1891

La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland

Balmain Watches is a Swiss-made luxury watch brand with strong haute couture heritage, born from the fusion of the Parisian fashion house founded by Pierre Balmain in 1945 and the precision watchmaking of the Swatch Group.
Known for its signature arabesque dial motif, inspired by embroidery on haute couture garments, Balmain Watches combines graceful, fashion‑driven design—such as elegant stainless steel or PVD‑coated cases, diamond accents, and mirror‑effect details—with high reliability quartz movements and Swiss Made quality. The brand emphasizes refinement, femininity (though men’s lines exist), and approachable elegance, targeting style-conscious women and men who appreciate Parisian glamour at accessible premium price points.

Awake
History
ARTICLES
Awake Watches is a Paris-based independent watch brand (launched in 2018 by founder Lilian Thibault) that specializes in artisanal, eco-conscious timepieces. Known for blending heritage “métiers d’art” crafts with minimalist modern design, its collections (such as Son Mài lacquer-dial watches and the Summetria guilloché series) fuse vintage-inspired cases with contemporary details. Awake emphasizes sustainable materials and innovation – for example, its 2021 Mission to Earth model (made for NASA) uses a recycled-titanium case and an NFC-enabled sapphire crystal. In style, the brand can be described as a retro-modern fusion: classic watch shapes endowed with artistic, story-driven dials. This approach targets design-savvy, environmentally conscious consumers who appreciate craftsmanship and meaning in a mid-luxury watch.
Awakes’s design philosophy centers on mindfulness and meaning. Its motto “Awaken time. Awaken awareness.” reflects a mission to prompt reflection and emotional connection. The brand views watchmaking as a space where engineering meets poetry, choosing every curve and material to evoke harmony, heritage and emotion rather than just technical show. Awake’s responsible approach (built in from day one) emphasizes rarity, balance and purpose. This sincere, purpose-driven ethos resonates with a global audience of design-driven, ethically minded collectors who value artistry, storytelling and sustainability in their watches.
2018
Founded by Lilian Thibault via a Kickstarter campaign

2023

2024

2025

Etienne Malec

Rooted in the founder's deep passion for classic timepieces, Baltic Watches draws inspiration from the iconic designs of the mid-20th century. Each watch is meticulously crafted with attention to detail, ensuring not only a beautiful exterior but also reliable and precise mechanics. By blending traditional techniques with contemporary innovation, Baltic Watches offers a unique collection that appeals to both seasoned collectors and new enthusiasts, celebrating the timeless art of watchmaking.

Berney
History
ARTICLES
Berney
is a heritage Swiss independent watchmaker specializing in artisanal, small-series timepieces that sit at the crossroads of jewelry and horology. Founded in 1972 by watchmaker Henri Berney in L’Abbaye, Vallée de Joux, Switzerland, the brand is known for reviving and re-energizing historic watch traditions — particularly ring watches, pendant watches, and bangle watches — which blend practical timekeeping with ornamental design. Its creations are characterized by discreet elegance and meticulous Swiss precision, catering to collectors who appreciate narrative-rich objects rather than mainstream sports or luxury watches.
Berney’s design philosophy centers on craftsmanship and creative boldness that refuse to choose between mechanical tradition and decorative innovation. The maison combines old-world horological skills (like servicing vintage Valjoux chronograph movements) with modern expertise in jewelry watchmaking, producing pieces that feel both timeless and distinctive. Its creative output includes delicate ring watches with scenic motifs, refined pendants, and mechanically interesting wrist pieces, appealing to buyers who value Swiss Made quality, artisanal production, and objects with artistic resonance.
Recently, Berney has showcased its jewelry watch mastery at high profile horological events and competitions, unveiling elaborate creations such as the 2025 Panda ring watch — a fusion of Grand Feu enamel, diamonds, and quartz precision that highlights the house’s deep craft heritage and poetic design approach.
1972
Berney was founded by Henri Berney in L’Abbaye, Vallée de Joux, Switzerland, establishing an independent family watchmaking atelier focused on combining horology with jewelry objets.
2025
Berney unveiled the exceptional Panda ring watch — a masterpiece combining Grand Feu enamel, 128 diamonds, and a Swiss ETA quartz movement — illustrating its artisanal excellence and jewelry-horology fusion.
2025
The Night & Day collection was highlighted in the high horology community, featuring refined Swiss ring watches that embody poetic design and Swiss precision, crafted within the Berney family workshop.
FOUNDED
1972
HEADQUARTERS
Vallée de Joux, Switzerland

History
ARTICLES
Blackout Concept is a sporty-luxurious micro-watchmaking brand that skillfully bridges high-end Swiss craftsmanship with accessible pricing. The company harnesses a unique fusion of Asian-sourced components and Swiss finishing expertise, delivering watches that offer both beauty and reliability without the prohibitive cost of traditional Swiss luxury—a core principle summarized as “The unthinkable becomes possible.” Initially founded by Fabrice Letellier—a former racing driver and automotive customizer known for his “matte black” designs—the brand began as a customizer of iconic Swiss watches like Rolex and Patek Philippe. However, in 2017, it shifted direction toward creating its own original models, combining rugged, accessible materials with refined craftsmanship to make luxury-style watches attainable for a wider audience. In 2022, under the leadership of Tymothy Plubeau and Étienne Rousseaux, the brand further evolved, focusing on original designs and quality improvements. Its flagship line—the XP1 Tourbillon—exemplifies the brand’s ethos: a sporty, technical aesthetic with a tourbillon complication, assembled under the care of a Swiss-certified master watchmaker in Geneva.
2006
Founded by Fabrice Letellier in Geneva; began customizing iconic Swiss watches with matte-black automotive aesthetic.
2017
Shifted from customization to original watch creations aiming to make ultra-luxury designs accessible at affordable prices.
2022
Leadership passed to Tymothy Plubeau and Étienne Rousseaux; brand revitalized with focus on original models and quality via Swiss-certified watchmaker.
2023
Expanded operations with a boutique in Paris’s 6th arrondissement, extending brand presence beyond Geneva.
2025
Rebranded as Blackout Watches while maintaining core design philosophy; continuing global expansion with growth in boutiques and collections.
FOUNDED
2006

Geneva, Switzerland

BLANCPAIN Official
History
ARTICLES
“INNOVATION IS OUR TRADITION” Marc A. Hayek – CEO Blancpain Blancpain is the oldest watch company in the world, having been founded in 1735. After all these years, the Maison is still as dedicated to innovation as it was when it was first established, and it continues to push the boundaries of what a watch can do. The Blancpain motto, “Innovation is our tradition,” expresses the company’s commitment to continuous improvement.
1859
A watchmaking workshop is established in Vallée de Joux by Louis-Elysée Piguet. He renovates a mill in Le Brassus into a hub for Blancpain Artistic Crafts and high-end watchmaking, which exists to this day.
1932
When Frédéric-Emile Blancpain, the 7th generation of his family to operate the firm, passes away, he leaves the company to his longtime assistant, Betty Fiechter. She is the first woman to run a major watchmaking firm as CEO.
1961
Along with Omega, Tissot, and Lémania, Blancpain has joined the Swiss watchmaking conglomerate known as the Société Suisse pour l'Industrie Horlogère (SSIH). Blancpain no longer only makes watches under its own brand, but it also becomes the center for producing movements for the SSIH family of brands.
1983
Jean-Claude Biver become the vice-chairman of Blancpain's executive committee.
1992
The SMH (Swiss Corporation for Microelectronics and Watchmaking Industries), which would evolve into the Swatch Group, purchased the brand.
FOUNDED
1735

Paudex/Le Brassus, Switzerland

History
ARTICLES
Bouveret France is a distinguished luxury artisanal atelier specializing in watch straps and leather goods. Founded by Gérard Bouveret in 1987 following over a decade of apprenticeship within the Rochet Group, the company reflects a deep heritage rooted in the Hauts Doubs—a historic cradle of French watchmaking. Renowned for its master leather craftsmanship, Bouveret combines timeless artisanal techniques with exceptional materials. Its workshops—now part of Rochet Group’s luxury Made in France production—execute around 60 distinct manual operations per piece, transforming noble skins (such as alligator, ostrich, lizard, python, galuchat, and calf) into elegant, durable, and soulful creations. Bouveret's offerings cater both to high-end watchmakers and discerning jewelers, targeting clientele who value craftsmanship, customization, and French-made quality.
1987
Gérard Bouveret, after over a decade apprenticing at Rochet Group, founded Gerard Bouveret Creation in Hauts Doubs, the historic French watchmaking region.
2017
Approximately 30 years later, Bouveret reintegrated into Rochet Group, becoming its luxury Made in France production unit.
2021
Rochet Group acquired Bouveret, integrating its high-end leather craftsmanship into the group's offerings.
2024
Bouveret France merged with Sibra under Rochet Group to form Sibra Bouveret Manufacture, consolidating luxury bespoke and first-equipment leather strap production in Besançon.

History
ARTICLES
Bohen Watches is a Swiss-Made, family-run luxury watchmaker based in Marseille, France. Founded by designer Blaise-Dominique Giuliani, its mission is to deliver exceptional timepieces crafted without compromise and offered at “factory price”—eschewing traditional retail markups to maintain independence and design purity. The brand emphasizes excellence through limited production, ensuring intricate, handcrafted finishes and high-end performance. Notable innovations include the world's first internal date magnifier and a satellite lens, plus fully decorated, rhodium-coated movements—even on models without transparent case backs. Bohen positions itself for discerning clients who value rarity, artisanal quality, and timeless design over brand logos and mainstream trends. Their approach blends retro-modern aesthetic cues—with elegance, precision, and endurance at its core.

History
ARTICLES
Breguet is a luxury Swiss watch company founded in 1775 by Abraham-Louis Breguet, who is widely considered to be one of the greatest watchmakers in history. The company is known for its high-end timepieces, which are often made with precious materials such as gold, platinum, and diamonds. Breguet is based in the village of L’Abbaye, in Switzerland and has a long history of innovation and craftsmanship. The company has been responsible for several important advancements in the field of watchmaking, including the development of the tourbillon, a mechanism that helps to improve the accuracy of mechanical watches. Breguet watches are highly prized by collectors and are considered to be among the finest watches available.

Bomberg
History
ARTICLES
Bomberg is an independent Swiss‑made, bold and unconventional watch brand operating in the fashion‑watch niche. Known for disruptive design, the brand merges retro‑modern aesthetics—like skull motifs, motorsport cues, and removable case innovations—with precision engineering, offering Swiss craftsmanship with attitude.
Its key product lines include the BB‑01 (entry‑level quartz and automatic models with offset crowns and car‑wheel‑style dials), the iconic BOLT‑68 (a pocket‑watch style quartz model with removable casing and chain), and the 1968 and Edge series (mechanical racing‑inspired models). Each line reflects playful, statement‑making design grounded in Swiss reliability.
2012

Launched officially into the watch market, positioning with bold designs and making initial waves among millennial consumers.

Appointment of David Sanchez as CEO and strategic evolution into a broader lifestyle brand combining rebellious design with traditional Swiss watchmaking.

ARTICLES
Breitling is a Swiss luxury watch company that was founded in 1884 by Leon Breitling. The company’s headquarters are located in Grenchen, Switzerland. Breitling is known for producing high-quality, precision watches for pilots, divers, and other professionals. The company’s watches are known for their durability, accuracy, and distinctive design, and are highly sought after by collectors and enthusiasts. In addition to its headquarters in Switzerland, Breitling has boutiques and authorized dealers located in cities around the world.

1915

1923
Breitling introduces the first independent chronograph pushpiece, which allows the wearer to start, stop, and reset the chronograph without affecting the timekeeping function.
1934
Breitling introduces the "Chronomat," its first wrist chronograph with a circular slide rule, which becomes popular with pilots and navigators.
1952
Breitling introduces the "Navitimer," a chronograph with a circular slide rule used for aviation calculations.
1979
Ernest Schneider acquires Breitling and begins modernizing the company's production facilities.
2023
Breitling is known for its high-quality, precision watches, particularly its chronographs, which are popular with pilots and other professionals.

Bruno Sohnle
BRUNO SÖHNLE UHRENATELIER GLASHÜTTE/SA. Official
History
ARTICLES
Originating from Glashütte, renowned for its horological heritage, Bruno Söhnle embodies a fusion of
                    precision craftsmanship and elegance. Established in 2000, the brand swiftly garnered acclaim for
                    its impeccable timepieces, characterized by aesthetic allure and technical excellence.
In addition to hand-wound and automatic movements, Bruno Söhnle presents a diverse selection of watches featuring refined quartz movements. True to the brand's commitment to transparency, these quartz timepieces showcase exquisite finishes through a transparent case back.
Crafting hand-wound movements involves mounting a classic, in-house produced ¾ plate onto the base plate of a Unitas 6498. The finishing includes thermally blued screws, 18k gold chatons, beveled edges, Glashütte stripe finishing, hand-applied perlage, and electroplating.
For automatic movements, Bruno Söhnle primarily utilizes the Sellita SW200 base, meticulously adorned with hand-applied perlage on the bridges and thermally blued screws. Subsequently, the movement is regulated, assembled, and adorned with an in-house rotor. Depending on the complication, the watchmaker may assemble the appropriate module, such as a regulator dial or a power reserve indicator.
The brand's attention to detail extends to quartz movements as well. Quartz movements from the esteemed Swiss brand Ronda undergo disassembly. The cover plate receives decorative Glashütte stripe finishing, and visible coils are colored before everything is secured with thermally blued screws.
FOUNDED
1957
HEADQUARTERS
Glashütte, Germany

Brunosoehnle-glashuette.com

Carl F. Bucherer is a Swiss luxury watch brand, founded in 1888 in Lucerne, Switzerland. The brand is known for its high-quality, precision timepieces that combine traditional Swiss watchmaking techniques with contemporary design. Carl F. Bucherer offers a range of watches for both men and women, including dress, sport, and diving watches, as well as chronographs. The brand is particularly renowned for its innovations in the field of automatic winding mechanisms, which have set it apart from other luxury watch brands. In addition to its core collections, Carl F. Bucherer also offers limited-edition and custom watches that showcase its attention to detail, craftsmanship, and heritage. Today, Carl F. Bucherer is recognized as one of the leading luxury watch brands in the world, offering timepieces that are both stylish and practical.

History
ARTICLES
Casio is a Japanese electronics company that was founded in 1946. It has become one of the largest and most innovative watch brands in the world, producing a wide range of timepieces for both casual and professional use. Casio is known for its high-quality and affordable watches, which feature advanced technology such as digital displays, water resistance, and stopwatch functions. Some of the most popular collections from Casio include the G-Shock, Baby-G, and Pro Trek series, which are designed for sports and outdoor activities. The brand is also well-known for its innovative smartwatches, which offer fitness tracking and other features. Casio continues to push the boundaries of what is possible in watch technology, making it a trusted brand for millions of people around the world.

Casio Computer Corp.
CASIO COMPUTER CO.
History
ARTICLES
Casio Computer Co.
was founded in 1946 by Tadao Kashio as a calculator manufacturing company and entered the timepiece market in 1974 with the release of the CASIOTRON, their first digital quartz watch. This innovative device could display hours, minutes, seconds, and the date, marking Casio’s emergence as a significant player in the digital watch industry, a place it continues to uphold today. In 1983, Casio revolutionized the watch market with the introduction of the best-selling G-Shock series and continued to innovate in the following decades with watch lines including the Pro Trek series for outdoor enthusiasts, featuring altimeters, barometers, and compasses. The company also developed the Edifice line of analog watches, combining sophisticated styling with advanced technology. Today, Casio remains one of the world’s biggest manufacturers of digital watches, with the G-Shock line alone having sold over 100 million units globally. It continues to innovate with new features like Bluetooth connectivity and solar power while revisiting classic designs for re-issues and anniversary editions.
Brands summary:
Casio’s greatest creation, The
G-Shock
line, launched in 1983, revolutionized digital watches with its unique shock-resistant design and rugged aesthetic, establishing a new category in the watch market. It continues to enjoy massive popularity worldwide and now includes dozens of models from simple retro styles to luxurious all-metal versions.
Casio
's main watch line, meanwhile, offers affordable, reliable timepieces ranging from simple digital watches like the iconic A158W to more feature-rich models at accessible price points.
Oceanus
, Casio’s luxury division, is distinguished by watches that combine high-end materials with advanced technology.

1946

Charlie Paris
History
ARTICLES
Charlie Paris is a French independent watch brand specializing in elegant, modern, and everyday wristwatches designed and assembled in Paris, France. Founded in 2014 by childhood friends Ambroise and Adrien, the brand creates timepieces that blend timeless design with contemporary French style, catering to men’s and women’s tastes alike. Charlie Paris positions itself as part of a “new watchmaking” movement — focused on clarity, sobriety, and refined aesthetics rather than excess — aiming to offer high-quality watches at fair, accessible prices.
Charlie Paris combines classic design elements (such as clean dials, balanced proportions, and elegant cases) with modern functionality like Swiss or high-grade quartz movements, water resistance, and sapphire crystals. Many of its collections — including Initial (timeless everyday models), Concordia (sportier, dive-capable watches), GR (field/hiking inspired), and Alliance (refined dress pieces) — showcase this fusion of style and utility. The brand also emphasizes quality materials (316L stainless steel, sapphire glass) and durability, making its watches suitable for daily wear and a range of lifestyles.
The target audience for Charlie Paris includes design-conscious buyers and watch enthusiasts who value French craftsmanship, independent branding, and timeless aesthetics at a more attainable price point than traditional luxury houses. With a strong focus on customer engagement through community collaboration and a direct-to-consumer model, Charlie Paris appeals to buyers who want quality, meaningful design, and transparent production values.
2014
Charlie Paris was founded in Paris, France by childhood friends Ambroise and Adrien with the mission to offer timeless, elegant watches designed and assembled locally and sold at fair, accessible prices.
2015
Launch of the Initial collection, the brand’s first major line, which became a core range featuring clean, versatile styles for everyday wear.
2016
Opening of Charlie Paris’s first boutique store in the Haut-Marais district of Paris, becoming a physical hub for the brand and its community.
2018
Introduction of the Concordia collection, a robust automatic model designed for exploration, including water resistance up to 300 m and used in polar expeditions.
2019
Charlie Paris began to internalize watch assembly in its Paris workshop, strengthening control over quality and production.
2022
Start of collaborations with renowned artists and designers (e.g., Hopare, Erro, Matali Crasset), expanding creative expression in limited editions and artistic designs.
2023
Launch of the Alliance collection featuring a Swiss automatic movement with a highly accurate perpetual moon phase complication, further establishing design and technical capability.
2024
10th anniversary celebration with a refreshed brand identity while maintaining the original promise of accessible, timeless design and French assembly.
FOUNDED
2014
HEADQUARTERS
Paris, France

History
ARTICLES
Certina is a Swiss watch brand that was founded in 1888. It is known for its high-quality timepieces that are designed for durability and accuracy. Certina watches feature precision movements, strong cases, and precise timing. The brand offers a range of watches for both men and women, including classic and sporty styles, as well as limited edition collections. Certina is also known for its innovative technology, such as the DS (Double Security) system, which provides extra protection for the watch’s movement. The brand is a popular choice for those who appreciate Swiss craftsmanship and precision, and it has a reputation for producing reliable and stylish timepieces.

Participated in the US Navy’s Sealab II underwater project, proving the reliability of Certina DS watches under high-pressure conditions.
1971

Chronoswiss is a distinguished Swiss watch manufacturer celebrated for its dedication to traditional mechanical watchmaking and innovative designs. Founded in 1983 by master watchmaker Gerd-Rüdiger Lang, the brand played a pivotal role in the renaissance of mechanical timepieces during an era dominated by quartz technology. Chronoswiss is renowned for pioneering the first serially produced wristwatch with a regulator dial, the Régulateur, in 1987, which showcased separate displays for hours, minutes, and seconds—a design inspired by classical precision clocks.  Over the years, the company has continued to innovate, introducing notable models like the Opus, a skeletonized chronograph, and the Flying Regulator series, blending traditional craftsmanship with contemporary aesthetics.

Chaumet

History
ARTICLES
Chaumet is a luxury watch brand founded in 1780 in Paris, France. The brand is known for its exquisite designs and exceptional craftsmanship and has a rich history of creating timepieces for royalty and aristocracy.
Chaumet’s watches are often characterized by their elegant and refined style, incorporating precious materials such as gold, diamonds, and pearls. The brand’s collections range from classic dress watches to more contemporary styles, with a focus on both men’s and women’s watches.
In addition to its watchmaking heritage, Chaumet is also renowned for its fine jewelry, which often complements its watch designs. Today, the brand continues to be a symbol of luxury and refinement, and collectors and enthusiasts around the world highly seek after its timepieces.

Citizen

History
ARTICLES
Citizen is a Japanese watch brand that was founded in 1918. The company is known for its high-quality and innovative watches, including both mechanical and quartz models. Citizen is known for its Eco-Drive technology, which allows watches to be powered by light, eliminating the need for battery replacement. They also have a line of watches that are radio-controlled and can sync the time with atomic clock for a precise timekeeping. The brand offers a wide range of watches for both men and women, from sporty to dressy, and is also known for its strong commitment to environmentally friendly practices. Citizen is one of the largest watch manufacturers in the world, with a presence in over 150 countries.

CITIZEN WATCH CO. LTD
CITIZEN WATCH CO. LTD
History
ARTICLES
Citizen Watch Co. Ltd.
was established by Kamekichi Yamazaki in 1918 as the Shokosha Watch Research Institute and produced its first timepiece — a pocket watch — in 1924. The name Citizen was chosen in 1930 to reflect the brand’s goal of making reliable watches at affordable prices, a legacy it continues to uphold a century later. Citizen produced Japan's first shock-resistant watch, the Parashock, in 1956, and Japan's first water-resistant watch, the Parawater, in 1959. The company’s biggest breakthrough came in 1976 with the introduction of Eco-Drive, a technology that converts both natural and artificial light into energy to power watches, eliminating the need for battery replacement. Throughout the 1980s and 1990s, Citizen continued to innovate with the development of radio-controlled watches and titanium cases while refining their Eco-Drive technology. The company expanded globally through strategic acquisitions, purchasing American watch company Bulova in 2008, Swiss movement manufacturer La Joux-Perret in 2012, and Swiss watch manufacturer Frederique Constant in 2016. Today, Citizen remains one of the world's largest watch manufacturers, and is respected equally for mechanical watches like the Tsuyosa and a wide range of Eco-Drive models.
Brands summary:
Citizen
is known for its affordable, innovative timepieces along with their advances in Eco-Drive technology, Super Titanium, and satellite timekeeping. Its portfolio of brands, including
Frederique Constant
,
Bulova
,
Alpina
,
Arnold & Son
,
Campanola
and
Accutron
represents a wide variety of forms, functions, and historical legacies. At the high end is
Frederique Constant
, which was founded in 1988, and is known for offering Swiss timepieces with in-house movements — including, in recent years, a tourbillon — at relatively accessible prices.
Alpina
likewise brings Swiss-made credentials to the table with a line of professional outdoor timepieces characterized by their "4" antimagnetic, anti-shock, water-resistant, and stainless steel standards. The addition of American watchmaker
Bulova
, best known for its ties to NASA and its high-frequency movements, as well as its former sub-brand
Accutron
, which was relaunched independently in 2020, add depth and variety to the Citizen Group’s. With the acquisition of Manufacture La Joux-Perret, a respected Swiss movement-maker, Citizen Group also gained control of
Arnold & Son
, an historic English brand specializing in high-end complications.
FOUNDED
1918

Tokyo, Japan

Claude Meylan
History
ARTICLES
Claude Meylan is a Swiss watchmaking brand renowned for its mastery of skeletonized movements and its commitment to preserving traditional craftsmanship. Founded in 1988 by Claude Meylan, a descendant of one of the four founding families of Swiss fine watchmaking, the brand is based in L’Abbaye, Vallée de Joux, Switzerland—a region synonymous with horological excellence. Meylan's heritage is deeply rooted in the Vallée de Joux, where his grandfather, Emile Meylan, was a passionate watchmaker. Claude Meylan himself honed his skills at prestigious watchmaking houses before establishing his own company. The brand is distinguished by its focus on skeletonized timepieces, which showcase the intricate mechanics of the watch movements. Claude Meylan's artisans meticulously "undress" each component, revealing the beauty of the movement's architecture. This approach not only highlights the technical prowess involved but also offers a transparent view into the heart of the timepiece. The collections, including Lac, Tortue, Lionne, and L’Abbaye, reflect a harmonious blend of classical design and contemporary aesthetics. Notably, the Tortue Lady collection caters to women who appreciate mechanical movements and the artistry of skeletonization, challenging traditional perceptions in the industry.
1988
Claude Meylan founded his eponymous watchmaking brand in L’Abbaye, Vallée de Joux, Switzerland.
1992
The brand presented its first collection at BaselWorld, featuring a high-end automatic perpetual calendar watch with a moon phase indicator.
2011
Philippe Belais acquired the brand, bringing fresh leadership and vision.
2014
The brand introduced the Tortue Lady collection, specifically designed for women who appreciate mechanical movements.
FOUNDED
1988

Vallée de Joux, Switzerland

History

Colorado Watch Company, the latest addition to the
Vortic family
represents the culmination of five years of meticulous craftsmanship and innovation.
                      We're thrilled to unveil our new line of fully modern, compact wristwatches, each powered by an American-assembled automatic movement.
                      A new brand of Vortic Watch Company, Colorado Watch Company upholds our commitment to American-made excellence. With the creation of this new brand, we asked ourselves a new question: “How much of a watch can we make in the USA while keeping the price around $1,000?” The answer is 87%. Nearly every component of these watches is American-made, especially the large metal components, like case and dial, which are machined from solid blocks of steel in our Fort Collins, Colorado manufacturing facility.
While rooted in our shared mission to create high-quality American products, this new venture allows us to reach a broader audience as well as explore new ideas we’ve had along the way. The watches are smaller and more attainable than a Vortic watch, but they’re also much more water resistant. Between our Field Watch and “The GCT” there’s something for everyone.
                    Just as with Vortic, our philosophy remains grounded in preserving and celebrating American history, one watch at a time. We're excited to invite you to explore this new chapter in American timekeeping, where precision, heritage, and innovation continue to define our legacy.

Clemence
History
ARTICLES
Clemence is a British microbrand in the watch industry, specialising in heritage-inspired tool and field watches that combine classic design cues (from the 1960s and ’70s) with modern robustness. Their design philosophy emphasises durability, longevity, and everyday adventure: watches that are built to endure rugged use (sea, summit, and urban exploration alike), yet have styling that nods to vintage timepieces. They’re assembled in Britain (with the help of international component specialists), and there is strong attention to craftsmanship, materials (sapphire crystals, solid steel bracelets, etc.), finishing, lume quality, and comfort.
Key products include the Photic Diver (a dive-style tool watch built to handle both underwater and daily wear, with vintage design references) and the Munro field watch (37.5 mm, slim but rugged, with British assembly, high-beat movement, etc.).

Clyda is a French watch brand founded over forty years ago in the Franche-Comté region of France, an area with a deep watchmaking heritage. Known for its timeless aesthetic, Clyda creates watches that merge contemporary, classic, and romantic influences into refined designs suited for everyday wear and special occasions alike.
The brand’s philosophy centers on expressing femininity through watchmaking. Clyda highlights elegance, creativity, and sophistication, offering collections that act as versatile fashion accessories as much as functional timekeepers. Its watches are marketed as indispensable style companions, designed for women of all ages who value grace, versatility, and individuality.

COMPAGNIE FINANCIÈRE RICHEMONT SA
History
ARTICLES
Compagnie Financière Richemont SA
is one of the world's largest luxury goods conglomerates, Richemont was established in 1988 by South African businessman Johann Rupert and it now owns some of the world’s most prestigious watch and jewelry brands including Cartier, IWC Schaffhausen, and Vacheron Constantin. The company has maintained a strong focus on traditional luxury craftsmanship while adapting to modern market demands, emphasizing the distinct identity and heritage of each Maison while providing them with shared resources and expertise. Richemont is known for its long-term approach to brand management, focusing on preserving brand value and craftsmanship rather than rapid expansion. The group continues to be one of the most influential players in the global watch market.
Brands summary:
There’s a lot of depth and breadth to the brands under the Richemont umbrella, from the makers of the very first wrist-worn watch (the
Cartier
Santos) to the world’s oldest continually operating watchmaker (
Vacheron Constantin
) to historic Maisons like
Montblanc
,
Roger Dubuis
,
Piaget
,
Panerai
, and
IWC
. Then, of course, there’s Germany’s leading watchmaker
A. Lange & Söhne
, which is prized for its movements and finishing, and produces watches on par with anything coming out of Switzerland’s finest workshops. There’s also
Jaeger-LeCoultre
, often called "the watchmaker's watchmaker," which combines iconic designs like the Reverso with a spectrum of traditional watchmaking crafts including enameling and miniature painting. From the accessible luxury of
Baume et Mercier
to the intricate and whimsical creations of
Van Cleef & Arpels
, Richemont’s brands are as varied as they are historically significant. Through these diverse maisons, Richemont preserves traditional watchmaking craftsmanship while embracing innovation across different luxury price segments.

1988

History
ARTICLES
COMPASS Timepieces is a purpose-driven luxury watch brand that blends Swiss precision with a modern legacy mindset. The brand focuses on creating timepieces that symbolize personal growth, direction, and purpose, with each model designed to encourage collectors to “Find Purpose™” in every moment — a motif central to its identity as both a lifestyle brand and a horological proposition.
Founded by David Carrington, COMPASS distinguishes itself as a Black-owned independent brand within the luxury space, combining bold design language, heritage-inspired details, and premium materials — such as Swiss ETA movements and high-grade components — to deliver watches that resonate with emotionally driven collectors and purpose-oriented consumers.
The brand’s product range includes collections like Explorer, Gratitude, innerG, Pathfinder, Titan 2, and Voyager, reflecting a blend of style, craftsmanship, and thematic storytelling tied to life’s journeys and milestones.
2022
Brand created on January 2, 2022 by founder David Carrington, initiating development and brand identity.
2023
Brand launched publicly on September 27, 2023, marking the official market debut of COMPASS Timepieces.
2024
First sale recorded on June 16, 2024, establishing commercial traction for the brand’s timepiece collections.

Corum
CORUM Official
History
ARTICLES
Corum is a premium Swiss watch brand that was established in 1955 in the city of La Chaux-de-Fonds in Switzerland. The brand is well-known for the cutting-edge and forward-thinking designs it creates, as well as for its emphasis on high-quality workmanship and accurate timekeeping. The watches produced by Corum sometimes have one-of-a-kind design elements, such as limited-edition patterns, coin-shaped casings, and elaborate faces with sophisticated movements.
The firm has a rich heritage in the production of high-end timepieces and has earned a reputation as one of the most prestigious brands on the market for luxury watches. Corum provides a variety of watch designs for both men and women, such as traditional dress watches, sport watches, and designs with a more unorthodox aesthetic.
1955
The company is founded by René Bannwart and his uncle Gaston Ries in La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland.
1960
Corum introduces several notable watches, including the "Admiral's Cup," which features a nautical pennant dial and becomes one of the company's most iconic designs.
1980
Corum creates several limited-edition watches, including a gold "Coin Watch" that incorporates a historic US $20 gold piece into the design.
1990
Corum introduces a range of complicated watches with features such as tourbillons and minute repeaters.
2000
Corum collaborates with designers and artists to create a series of limited-edition watches with unconventional designs.
2013
Corum is acquired by China Haidian Holdings Limited, a Hong Kong-based watch and timepiece company.
FOUNDED
1955
HEADQUARTERS
La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland

CZAPEK & CIE
CZAPEK WATCHES Official
History
ARTICLES
Czapek & Cie is a Swiss watch brand with a storied history that dates back to the mid-19th century. Founded in 1845 by Polish watchmaker François Czapek and his partner Juliusz Gruzewski, the company quickly gained prominence for its exceptional timepieces, becoming the official watchmaker to Emperor Napoleon III and opening boutiques in Geneva, Paris, and Warsaw. Despite its early success, the brand ceased operations around 1869. In 2011, Czapek & Cie was revived by a group of enthusiasts, who reestablished the company’s commitment to crafting high-quality, handcrafted Swiss watches that blend traditional techniques with contemporary design.

History
ARTICLES
D1 Milano is a design-driven Italian watch brand specializing in contemporary fashion watches that blend modern industrial aesthetics with accessible luxury. Founded in 2013 in Milan by entrepreneur Dario Spallone, the brand focuses on creating bold, minimalist timepieces inspired by Italian design culture and the architecture-influenced watch styles popularized in modern luxury sports watches.
The brand’s design philosophy revolves around clean geometry, integrated bracelets, and lightweight materials, producing watches that emphasize visual impact and everyday wearability. Collections such as Polycarbon, Ultra Thin, Automatico, Chronograph, Ceramica, and Skeleton highlight a mix of quartz and automatic movements paired with materials like polycarbonate, stainless steel, and ceramic. This approach allows the brand to offer stylish, contemporary watches at relatively accessible price points, appealing to consumers who value fashion-forward design as much as functionality.
2013
D1 Milano was founded in Milan, Italy by Dario Spallone during Milan Fashion Week, positioning the brand as a design-focused watch startup inspired by Italian style.
2014
The company joined the Bocconi University business incubator program, supporting its early growth and international development.
2015
D1 Milano moved its operational headquarters to the Dubai Design District, strengthening its presence in global fashion and luxury markets.
2015
The brand introduced innovative watch concepts including thermochromic watches that change color with temperature variations.
FOUNDED
2013

David Van Heim
History
ARTICLES
David Van Heim is a Swiss watchmaking brand founded in 2009 in Geneva, Switzerland. The brand is recognized for its innovative approach, combining high-tech features, distinctive design, and affordability. After three years of research to perfect brand consistency, it emerged in 2010, quickly becoming a notable name in the watchmaking industry.
The brand's design philosophy centers on an original interlocking system, allowing for customization and multiple combinations. Flexible or screwed-on case-lugs ensure a comfortable fit, even for larger sizes. David Van Heim's timepieces are characterized by attractive designs, featuring a blend of shiny and satin finishes, creating a dynamic interplay of light and shadow.
David Van Heim offers a range of collections, including "Etika," "Hollander," "Irea," "Dual Chronograph," and "Tourbillon," each embodying the brand's commitment to innovation and accessibility. The brand caters to watch enthusiasts seeking distinctive, high-quality timepieces that offer both style and functionality.
2009
David Van Heim was founded in Geneva, Switzerland, with a focus on innovative watchmaking combining high-tech features, distinctive design, and affordability.
2010
The brand officially launched after three years of research to perfect brand consistency, quickly becoming a notable name in the watchmaking industry.
2011
Introduced the T1 Tourbillon, featuring a manual winding movement and a distinctive aperture at 12 o'clock showcasing the regulating organ.
2015
Expanded collections to include 'Dual Chronograph' and 'Irea,' offering a range of timepieces with unique designs and functionalities.
2020
Launched the 'Etika' and 'Hollander' collections, emphasizing customization and ergonomic designs.
FOUNDED
2009

Geneva, Switzerland

Delma
DELMA WATCHES Official
History
ARTICLES
Founded in 1924, Delma Watches is a Swiss watchmaker with a legacy of crafting high-performance timepieces for adventurers, divers, and watch enthusiasts alike. Known for their precision engineering and robust design, Delma’s collections range from professional dive watches to elegant chronographs, ensuring reliability in any environment. Whether exploring the depths of the ocean or navigating everyday life, Delma embodies the spirit of endurance and excellence.
1924
The Foundation
Brothers Adolf and Albert Gilomen founded A. & A. Gilomen S.A. in Lengnau, Switzerland. The Delma name originates from one of the brand’s initial models.
1946
The First Chronograph
Under the name Midland, Delma introduced an 18-karat yellow gold hand-wound chronograph, marking the beginning of its sports watch collection.
1954
A True Racing Machine
A sporty chronograph with a stainless-steel case and an ETA Valjoux 7736 R movement is launched, featuring a tachymeter dial and luminous hands.
1966
Delma Rises to the Top
The company is renamed Delma Watch Ltd. after Ulrich Wüthrich & partner take over. The brand remains family-led to this day.
1969
First Automatic Divers’ Watch
Delma introduces the Periscope, its first wristwatch with enhanced water resistance, marking the brand’s entry into dive watches.
1975
Professional Divers’ Watches
The Shell Star and Quattro are launched as professional dive watches, becoming key models in Delma’s history.
1981
Launch of the Shark
The Shark model stands out with its rectangular case, octagonal dial, and diamond-cut indexes.
1995
The Delma Platinum Collection
The brand wins 1st prize in the precious metal category at the JA International Jewelry Show in New York.
2017
The Racing Collection Expands
Delma launches the Oceanmaster series, inspired by its partnership with IMOCA Ocean Masters, focusing on extreme sailing.
2021
75 Years of Delma Chronographs
The Heritage Chronograph is introduced in two versions, including a limited edition Bicompax Chronograph (75 pieces).
2024
100 Years of Delma
Delma celebrates its centennial anniversary, marking a century of Swiss craftsmanship, endurance, and innovation.
FOUNDED
1924
HEADQUARTERS
Lengnau, Switzerland
WEBSITE
Delmawatches.com
Back to top
