History
ARTICLES
Daniel Wellington is a minimalist Scandinavian-inspired watch and accessories brand, specializing in affordable timepieces and jewelry. Designed in Stockholm and launched in 2011 by founder Filip Tysander, the brand became known for its ultra-thin, elegant cases paired with interchangeable NATO, leather, or mesh straps.
With a design philosophy rooted in modern-retro fusion, Daniel Wellington blends classic timelessness—like clean round dials and slim profiles—with functional versatility via interchangeable straps. The watches use Japanese quartz movements (Miyota) and offer quality materials (e.g. Italian leather, stainless steel plating) while remaining accessible in price.
2006
Filip Tysander meets a British gentleman named Daniel Wellington on travel, inspiring vintage watch + NATO strap concept.
2011
Brand founded by Filip Tysander in Sweden; first minimalist watches with interchangeable straps launched.
2014
Surpassed 1 million watches sold and ~$70 million revenue within ~3 years of launch.
2016
Generated approximately US $230 million revenue and US $111.5 million in profit, one of fastest‑growing private companies in Europe.
2017
Named fastest‑growing private company in Europe (Inc./Dezeen rankings).
2018
Most‑mentioned brand on Instagram globally, surpassing Nike, Sephora, Amazon per SocialBakers.
2021
Reported operating losses for third consecutive year (~387 million SEK); begin turnaround under CEO Tianhao Liu.
2025
Company returned to profit and leadership expresses optimism around reaching billion‑SEK revenue again.
FOUNDED
2011

Stockholm, Sweden

Delhi Watch Company
DELHI WATCH COMPANY Official
Loading videos...
0/0
History
ARTICLES
Delhi Watch Company is an Indian heritage-inspired watch brand specializing in affordable, handcrafted timepieces that celebrate Delhi’s cultural legacy through design. Known for its "city storytelling" philosophy, the brand combines retro architectural motifs (from landmarks like India Gate, Connaught Place, and Chandni Chowk) with modern Japanese movements, creating accessible luxury watches that resonate with urban nostalgia. Key collections include the Dilli Series (featuring Mughal-era patterns), Janpath Automatics (named after Delhi’s iconic flea market), and Mehrauli Quartz (inspired by Qutub Minai’s latticework), targeting young professionals, design enthusiasts, and heritage-conscious millennials. The brand’s design ethos centers on democratizing craftsmanship – each watch incorporates locally sourced materials (stainless steel, vegan leather) and pays tribute to Delhi’s layered history through engraved casebacks and dial textures. Unique selling points include modular strap systems, collaborations with Delhi artisans, and ultra-affordable pricing without compromising on Miyota/Citeta movements. By blending historical narratives with contemporary wearability, Delhi Watch Company positions itself as "horological postcards from the capital."
2019
Founded by Mohit Jain and Sagar Gahlot, launching with the 'Dilli 36' collection featuring 36 iconic Delhi neighborhood designs.
2020
Introduced 'Chandni Chowk Bazaar' series with dials mimicking spice market colors and jali (lattice) patterns, selling 500+ units in pre-orders.
2021
Launched 'Janpath Automatic' collection using Japanese Miyota movements, becoming India's most affordable automatic watch line under ₹12,000.
2022
Collaborated with Delhi street artists for limited 'Hauz Khas Art District' edition, featuring graffiti-inspired dials and recycled stainless steel cases.
2023
Expanded to 'Mehrauli Heritage' quartz series with Qutub Minar-inspired hands and sandstone-textured dials, praised for architectural fidelity.
2024
Partnered with Dastkari Haat Samiti for hand-embroidered strap collection, supporting local karigars (craftsmen) and introducing modular strap system.
FOUNDED
2019
HEADQUARTERS
New Delhi, India
WEBSITE
Delhiwatchcompany.com

Dior
CHRISTIAN DIOR Official
History
ARTICLES
Dior is a world-renowned watch brand that has been synonymous with luxury and elegance since its establishment in 1947. Founded by Christian Dior, a pioneer of haute couture, the brand quickly became known for its exquisite designs and impeccable craftsmanship, setting a new standard in the fashion industry.
With its extensive range of watches, Dior offers a wide variety of styles and designs that cater to the tastes and preferences of its discerning clientele. Whether you’re looking for a classic, timeless piece or a modern, avant-garde style, Dior has something to suit every occasion and personality.
What sets Dior watches apart is their unwavering commitment to quality and attention to detail. Each timepiece is crafted to perfection by master watchmakers using the finest materials, and features intricate movements and stunning dial and case designs that showcase the brand’s creativity and innovation.
Dior watches are not just fashion accessories, but true works of art that embody the brand’s values of excellence, sophistication, and timelessness. With a Dior watch on your wrist, you are not just keeping time, but making a statement about your style and taste. A symbol of luxury and elegance, a Dior watch is the perfect accessory for those who appreciate the finer things in life.
1947
Christian Dior launches his eponymous fashion house in Paris, France.
1967
The first Dior watches are produced by the company's licensing partner, Benedom.
1975
Dior forms a partnership with the French crystal company Saint-Louis to produce a line of crystal watches.
1978
Dior establishes its own watch division, and the first collection of Dior watches is introduced.
2005
Dior introduces the "Christal" watch, which features a sapphire crystal bracelet and a unidirectional rotating bezel.
2011
Dior introduces the "Dior VIII" collection, which features a range of luxury watches for women, including models with diamond-encrusted cases.
FOUNDED
1947

Paris, France

DKNY (Donna Karan New York) is a renowned watch brand that embodies the spirit and energy of New York City. Known for its sophisticated and contemporary designs, DKNY watches combine urban aesthetics with high functionality. The brand caters to fashion-forward individuals seeking versatile timepieces that seamlessly transition from day to night. With a commitment to quality and style, DKNY watches are crafted to complement modern lifestyles, offering a range of styles from minimalist and classic to bold and trendy, ensuring there is a perfect watch for every taste and occasion.

1989

History
ARTICLES
Depancel is a French independent watch brand specializing in motorsport-inspired timepieces. Drawing its name from the iconic French automotive manufacturers Delage, Panhard, and Facel Vega, Depancel merges the elegance of classic French watchmaking with the dynamic spirit of racing culture. The brand's design philosophy emphasizes retro-modern aesthetics, evident in collections like the Série R01 and Allure, which feature bold square cases, vintage-inspired dials, and automotive-themed details. With a commitment to craftsmanship, each watch is designed and assembled in France, reflecting the brand's dedication to quality and heritage. Depancel's target audience comprises motorsport enthusiasts and watch aficionados who appreciate the fusion of vintage racing aesthetics with contemporary watchmaking. The brand offers a range of timepieces, from the mechanical hand-wound Legend 60s chronograph to the accessible Allure Mono Eye Mint Green with its meca-quartz movement.
2018
Depancel was founded by Clément, an enthusiast driven by a passion for motorsport and French watchmaking heritage. The brand's name is a tribute to the French automotive manufacturers Delage, Panhard, and Facel Vega.
2020
The brand launched its first collection, the Série R01, featuring a distinctive square design inspired by classic sports car grilles. The collection garnered attention for its bold aesthetics and automotive influences.
2021
Depancel introduced the Allure collection, blending neo-vintage design elements with modern watchmaking techniques. The Allure Mono Eye Mint Green model was particularly noted for its unique dial and accessible meca-quartz movement.
2022
The Legend 60s chronograph was released, equipped with the Seagull ST1901 hand-wound movement. The model pays homage to 1960s racing chronographs with its 'Panda' and 'Reverse Panda' dial configurations.
2023
Depancel expanded its offerings with the Série R01 Ivory, featuring a square case design inspired by vintage racing car air vents. The watch includes a Miyota 9120 automatic movement and showcases the brand's commitment to automotive-inspired aesthetics.
FOUNDED
2018

Direnzo
History
ARTICLES
Direnzo Watches is a boutique micro-watch brand crafting Swiss-made timepieces with a distinctive retro-modern flair. Driven by founder Sergio (G.) Di Renzo, the label specializes in elegant yet adventurous designs—often inspired by vintage automotive and aeronautical aesthetics—that fuse nostalgic cues with contemporary craftsmanship and accessible pricing. The brand is known for its “bowl-shaped” dials, first introduced with the DRZ 02 in 2018, creating depth and seamless case-dial integration—an example of its retro-futuristic signature design ethos. Direnzo consistently uses quality materials—316L steel with scratch-resistant HV 800 coating, sapphire crystals with AR coating, Sellita movements with Incabloc protection, and robust 10 ATM water resistance—to deliver both reliability and visual sophistication.
2018
Introduction of the signature bowl-shaped dial with the DRZ 02 design.
2021
Launch of DRZ 04 “Mondial” pre-sale on March 20, deliveries June 2021; offered a meteorite limited edition dial (Muonionalusta).
2023
Launch of DRZ 04 Mondial 2023 relaunch in February 2023, deliveries April 2023, including a meteorite limited edition.
2023
Introduction of DRZ 02R Aerolite original version in December 2023.
2024
Relaunch of DRZ 02R Aerolite 2024 announced June 25 2024; pre-sale June 29 and delivery late August-early September.
2024

Duckworth Prestex
DUCKWORTH PRESTEX Official
History
ARTICLES
For many years, Prestex watches were part of everyday life: an essential accessory that equipped
                    generations of families and kept them elegantly on time, thanks to their classic British time-pieces
                    that combined handsome looks with reliable accuracy.
Right up to the 1960s, when the brand was discontinued, Prestex showcased the quality and dedication of a family business that always put thier customers first.
in 2020. the Prestex name was born again. Nearly 100 years after the company was originally founded, Neil Duckworth resurrected his grandfather's watch brand and matched it with his own family name to create Duckworth Prestex.
These take the original Prestex designs from days gone by and update them with modern watchmaking technology.
The chronographs echo the pocket watches that began the Prestex story in the 1920s, while the automatic is a tribute to the later Prestex watches. The Bolton Pays homage to the town where the company was originally born, and the Belmont is a diving watch named after a nearby village, which was home to a sailing club.
All these watches, and those that follow them, tell a very personal story of how the brand came to be and what it stands for Tradition reinvented.
FOUNDED
2021
HEADQUARTERS
Duckworth Prestex,  United Kingdom

Duckworthprestex.com

History
ARTICLES
DOXA is a heritage Swiss watch brand specializing in robust dive and adventure timepieces. Founded in 1889 by Georges Ducommun
, the company is best known for its SUB series of diving watches that blend vintage style with practical innovation. Its flagship model, the SUB 300 (launched 1967), featured a patented rotating bezel and a signature bright orange, design cues that remain iconic for the brand. Over the decades DOXA has gained a reputation as a benchmark for diving watches
, combining mid-century aesthetics with technical reliability for professional and sports divers.
The latest DOXA models exemplify this retro-modern mix of style and utility. For example, the SUB 300T “Clive Cussler” edition features a handmade “vintage compass” dial and a distressed aged-steel case
, underscoring DOXA’s nostalgic design approach.

History
ARTICLES
DuFrane is an independent, American-assembled watch microbrand founded by Steven Lee in 2016. The brand blends retro / vintage design cues (especially pilot, dive, field styles) with modern reliability and craftsmanship, aiming for watches that are stylish, durable, and full of character. The design philosophy is rooted in storytelling & place. Many of the model names and aesthetic touches are inspired by Austin, Texas (and its landmarks or history), and Lee’s own journey as a collector, designer, and former pilot. Each piece is hand-assembled in Austin (with the exception of some Swiss-made components, e.g. in the Littlefield GMT).
Target audience: watch enthusiasts and collectors who appreciate microbrands; people who like vintage and pilot/dive motifs but want modern build, honest pricing, artisanal touches, and a sense of place & personality in their timepiece. Also those who value that the founder is directly involved in assembly and service.
2016
Brand founded by Steven Lee in Austin, Texas; first model released (‘Bergstrom’), incorporating pilot-watch heritage and hand-wound Swiss movement. DuFrane is named after Lee's mother’s maiden name.
2017
Production pieces of the Bergstrom began shipping (initial sketches earlier, but production delayed until late 2016 / early 2017).
2021
Founder Steven Lee discussed the brand’s philosophy in interviews, emphasizing its Austin roots, design inspirations, personal assembly, customer experience.
2022
Release and reviews of new models such as the Travis, earning praise for design, case, lume, and finishing in microbrand watch circles.
2025
DuFrane continues a core product lineup including models like Waterloo, Mabry, Littlefield GMT etc., with both automatic and Swiss movement models; maintains assembly in Austin, and participates in watch shows such as Toronto Timepiece Show.
FOUNDED
2016

DWISS

History
ARTICLES
DWISS (short for Design Watch Independent Switzerland) is an innovative Swiss microbrand specializing in distinctive design‑led mechanical and automatic wristwatches. Founded in 2011 by industrial designer Rafael Simoes Miranda, the brand’s core mission is summed up in its motto: “Any watch can tell time; DWISS tells it differently.” — reflecting its focus on unconventional time displays, architectural case designs, and artistic expression through horology.
DWISS combines Swiss precision engineering with bold, creative aesthetics, often using limited production runs and award‑winning design concepts to stand apart from mainstream Swiss brands. The brand is particularly recognized for innovative time display systems (e.g., Signature Hours, Wandering Hours), pioneering use of materials such as niobium, and multiple international design awards (including iF Design Awards and Good Design Awards).
Key products include collections such as M3S (Signature Hours) and the A1 PowerLume — a recent sports‑inspired Swiss automatic featuring a La Joux‑Perret G100 Soigné movement with a 68‑hour power reserve and bright Super‑LumiNova® indexes. Each collection is produced in strictly limited numbers to preserve exclusivity and collectible appeal.
2011
DWISS (Design Watch Independent Switzerland) was founded by industrial designer Rafael Simoes Miranda in Lugano, Switzerland, with the ambition to create innovative Swiss watch designs that redefine time telling. (Official brand history)
2012
DWISS produced the world’s first niobium watch — the CLASSIQUE CBMM — in collaboration with Brazilian mining company CBMM, earning its first International Design Award and pioneering the use of niobium in horology. (Historic milestone)
2019
Launch of the *Watch Design Club*, a community initiative inviting enthusiasts to co‑create watches alongside DWISS, blending collector involvement with design innovation. (Community engagement)
2021
DWISS celebrated its 10th anniversary with special editions including the *M3W* and *R2*, recognized with multiple international design awards and showcasing its design evolution. (Anniversary milestone)
2024
DWISS was named one of the *Top 100 Luxury Jewelry & Watch Brands* globally in the 2024 Luxury Lifestyle Awards, affirming its creative influence and design leadership among international brands. (Industry recognition)
2025
The *A1 PowerLume* collection was released featuring a La Joux‑Perret G100 Soigné movement with 68‑hour power reserve and advanced luminescence, showcasing modern sports design and technical refinement. (Recent product launch)
FOUNDED
2011
HEADQUARTERS
Lugano, Switzerland

Edox
EDOX SWISS WATCHES Official
History
ARTICLES
EDOX is a Swiss luxury watch brand that embodies the spirit of precision, innovation, and elegance. Established in 1884 by Christian Ruefli-Flury in the watchmaking region of Les Genevez, Switzerland, EDOX has remained at the forefront of horological excellence for over a century.
Renowned for its exceptional craftsmanship and attention to detail, EDOX timepieces combine traditional Swiss watchmaking techniques with contemporary design elements, appealing to both watch enthusiasts and connoisseurs of fine craftsmanship.
Each EDOX watch is a testament to the brand's commitment to precision and performance. From diving watches equipped with advanced water-resistant technology to chronographs designed for precision timing, EDOX offers a diverse range of timepieces tailored to meet the needs of various lifestyles and preferences.
The brand's dedication to innovation is evident in its collaborations with professionals in sports and exploration. EDOX has partnered with renowned athletes, racing drivers, and adventurers, pushing the boundaries of what is possible in watch design and functionality.
EDOX watches exude a timeless elegance that transcends trends, making them suitable for both formal occasions and everyday wear. With a blend of heritage, innovation, and Swiss excellence, EDOX continues to captivate watch enthusiasts worldwide, earning its place as a prestigious name in the world of luxury timepieces.
1884
Christian Ruefli-Flury establishes EDOX in the watchmaking region of Les Genevez, Switzerland.
1921
EDOX launches its first wristwatches, expanding beyond pocket watches to meet the growing demand for wearable timepieces.
1950'
EDOX gains recognition for its innovative water-resistant watches, particularly the EDOX Hydro-Sub, designed for diving enthusiasts.
1960
EDOX establishes itself as a leader in marine timekeeping with the introduction of the Geoscope, a watch capable of displaying time zones around the world.
1998
EDOX becomes the official timekeeper of the Dakar Rally, solidifying its reputation for precision and reliability in extreme conditions.
2004
EDOX celebrates its 120th anniversary with the release of limited edition commemorative timepieces, showcasing the brand's heritage and craftsmanship.
Now
EDOX continues to innovate and expand its collection, offering a diverse range of timepieces that blend Swiss precision with contemporary design.
FOUNDED
1884

Biel/Bienne, Switzerland

Electra is a vintage-inspired diver watch brand that masterfully fuses retro charm with modern functionality. History
ARTICLES
Epos is a niche Swiss mechanical watchmaker specializing in expertly crafted timepieces that blend traditional watchmaking heritage with refined, often retro‑modern styling. Known for offering “Artistry in Watchmaking”, the brand modifies and decorates Swiss base movements to deliver sophisticated complications—such as moon‑phase, big‑date, regulator, power‑reserve, and skeleton dials—at relatively accessible price points.
Their design philosophy emphasizes hand finishing, visible case‑back decoration, and in‑house modules built on vintage movements (from ETA, Sellita, Peseux, Landeron) reinterpreted into modern complications. The Artistry, Timeless, Sportive, and Pocket Watch collections showcase a fusion of technical refinement, aesthetic elegance, and mechanical authenticity.
1925
James Aubert founded James Aubert SA in the Vallée de Joux, pioneering complications like moon‑phase, power‑reserve, regulator and big‑date modules.
1983
Peter Hofer founded Montres EPOS SA (later Epos Uhren AG) in Biel/Lengnau, reviving mechanical watchmaking using vintage movements and traditional craftsmanship.
2002
Ursula Forster and Tamdi Chonge (Chonge‑Forster family) took over ownership and management, refocusing the brand on Epos‑badged mechanical collections and international expansion.
2010
Launch of the Epos 3400 model with reversed‑24‑hour second time‑zone using two independent mechanical movements.
2017
Introduction of the Epos 3435 “Verso”, the brand’s first inverted Unitas 6497 mechanical movement visible through the dial side.
2024
Debut of the stainless‑steel sports watch (Epos 3505) and its skeletonised variant (3505 SK), water‑resistant to 100 m, offering modern sporty design in the CHF ~1,600 range.
FOUNDED
1983

Lengnau, Switzerland

History
ARTICLES
Elgé is a heritage-revival watch brand rooted in the tradition of Savoyard French watchmaking. Originally founded in Annecy in the late 1940s, the brand earned recognition for its robust—and elegantly adventurous—tool watches powered by French and Swiss mechanical movements. After ceasing operations during the quartz crisis in 1973, Elgé has made a modern comeback with renewed respect for its artisanal origins and regional craftsmanship. The brand specializes in the Chamonix, a “French-style tool watch” that melds retro design cues—like bold “Magnum” hands, generous indices, and custom serif typography—with contemporary performance features. These include a 38 mm 316L stainless-steel case, 150 m water resistance, screw-down crown and caseback, domed sapphire crystal with internal anti-reflective coating, and finishes combining brushed and polished surfaces.

Eterna

History
ARTICLES
The origins of the Eterna watch brand may be traced all the way back to 1856 in Switzerland. The business has established a reputation for producing clocks of exceptional quality and accuracy, as well as for its pioneering attitude, having introduced a number of significant watchmaking advancements throughout the course of its history. The designs of Eterna watches are often recognized for their timelessness and refinement, as well as their use of luxurious components and cutting-edge technology in their construction. The business has a long history of making watches for several industries, including aviation, diving, and sports, and it is well-known for the durability and dependability of its timepieces.
1856
The company is founded by Josef Girard and Urs Schild in Grenchen, Switzerland.
1870
Eterna becomes one of the first Swiss watch brands to produce watch movements with ball bearings, leading to improved accuracy and durability.
1914
Eterna develops the first wristwatch with an alarm function, which becomes a popular design feature in subsequent years.
1932
Eterna introduces the "Eterna-matic" automatic movement, which features a rotor system that winds the watch using the motion of the wearer's wrist.
1848
Eterna introduces the "KonTiki" watch, named after the famous Kon-Tiki raft expedition, which features a robust and water-resistant design for use in sports and diving.
1978
Eterna introduces the "Golden Heart" watch, which features a transparent caseback that showcases the watch's movement.
1995
Eterna introduces the "Spherodrive" movement, which replaces traditional gears with ball bearings, leading to improved accuracy and durability.
2011
Eterna is acquired by Chinese watch company Citychamp Watch & Jewellery Group.
FOUNDED
1856

History
ARTICLES
Escudo Watches is a British-Portuguese microbrand specializing in contemporary wristwatches inspired by the spirit of early explorers and navigators. The brand’s design philosophy draws on the history, courage, and adventurous ethos of Portuguese and British voyagers who charted unknown seas, aiming to reflect that heritage in its timepieces. Escudo pairs classic watch design elements with modern craftsmanship and Swiss-made movements, creating stylish yet functional watches for everyday wear.
The brand emphasizes quality and attention to detail, offering watches that blend timeless aesthetics with reliable performance — for example, robust stainless steel cases, sapphire crystals, and high water resistance suited to an active lifestyle. Its watches often feature vintage-inspired design cues and versatile styling that works for both casual and dress occasions. Escudo’s key collections (such as the Ocean Seacrest) showcase this balance of heritage and utility, catering to consumers who value adventure-driven design with practical functionality. Escudo’s target audience includes enthusiasts of modern-vintage watches and design-driven consumers looking for something distinctive yet wearable. With features like Swiss movements and design cues linked to exploration history, the brand appeals to buyers who want narrative, craftsmanship, and everyday performance in a watch.

New Ocean Seacrest variants (e.g., Black Ice, Deep Azul) continued to expand the brand’s lineup.
2025
Escudo engaged the community in shaping new releases via polls and announced partnerships with the Federação Portuguesa de FootGolf and Oracle Time magazine.

Eska
Eska Watches Official
Loading videos...
0/0
History
ARTICLES
Eska Watches is a historic Swiss-niche watchmaker revived as a neo-vintage brand. Originally founded in 1918 in Selzach, Switzerland, by Sylvan Kocher (S. Kocher & Co, abbreviated to “Eska”), the brand became known for its elegant three-hand watches and artistic complications—such as cloisonné enamel dials—that resonated particularly in North and South American markets in its golden mid-20th-century era. Discontinued in 1987 due to the quartz crisis, Eska was resurrected in 2024 by watchmaking enthusiasts Christophe Chevreton and Sinicha Knezevic. Their vision: to honor the brand’s historical identity while reinterpreting it through a contemporary aesthetic. Their standout launch—the Amphibian 250 diver—reimagines the rare Amphibian 600 from 1959 in a modern yet heritage-driven form, available in permanent and limited editions like the White Shark and Red Viper.
1918
Founded by Sylvan Kocher (S. Kocher & Co, abbreviated to 'Eska') in Selzach, Switzerland, focusing on elegant three-hand watches and artistic dials.
1937
Relocated operations from Selzach to Grenchen, a center of Swiss watchmaking, enabling international expansion.
1948-1959
Expanded into chronographs and complications—including calendars, moon phases, and introduced the iconic Amphibian 600 diver, used by the French Navy.
1987
Ceased operations during the quartz crisis after nearly 70 years and over 250 models in its catalogue.
2024
Revival of Eska Watches by Christophe Chevreton and Sinicha Knezevic; launch of the Amphibian 250 via Kickstarter as a modern reinterpretation of the Amphibian 600.
2025
Release of the Heritage SK25 hand-wound bi-compax chronograph assembled in Besançon, and growing attention via press coverage and limited-edition diver variations.
FOUNDED
1918
HEADQUARTERS
Saint-Fons, France
WEBSITE
Eska-watches.fr

History
ARTICLES
Etien was founded by Tien, inspired by a transformative experience at the Fabergé Egg Exhibition in China in 2016. Immersed in the intricate details and timeless allure of Fabergé's masterpieces, Tien found the inspiration to create something equally enchanting. The exhibition, showcasing exquisite craftsmanship and opulent design, left a lasting impression on Tien. Each piece's meticulous artistry and historical significance stirred a deep appreciation for traditional craftsmanship and artistry.
This pivotal moment ignited a creative spark within Tien, driving an ambition to create something that could similarly captivate and endure through time. Tien embarked on a journey to marry the best of traditional craftsmanship with cutting-edge technology, aiming to forge a unique path in the world of horology. The vision was clear: to design and produce watches that are not just functional timepieces but true embodiments of refined taste and enduring beauty.
Each ETIEN watch is crafted with meticulous attention to detail, ensuring that every piece reflects the blend of classic elegance and modern innovation. Through this harmonious fusion, Tien aspires to bring forth creations that honor traditional craftsmanship while embracing the advancements of contemporary technology. The goal is to offer timepieces that stand as symbols of sophistication, appealing to those who appreciate the finer things in life.

History
ARTICLES
Fathers® Watches is an Italian-designed, Swiss-manufactured watch brand that creates high-quality mechanical timepieces grounded in emotional storytelling and symbolic heritage. The company was founded in 2021 by two friends, Luigi Generali and Matteo Franchina, with a mission to craft watches that celebrate deep personal connections — especially those between fathers and their children — and serve as heirlooms to be passed across generations.
Known for their Swiss Made automatic movements (including calibers from reputable makers such as Sellita, ETA, and Landeron) and careful proprietary design, Fathers® blends classic watch design with contemporary expression. Their collections — including models like the Horizon diver lineup (e.g., Eternal Legacy, Seafarer, Procida) and Small Seconds pieces like Nexus and Aeterna — combine robust mechanical performance with refined aesthetics that suit both everyday wear and meaningful occasions.
2021
Fathers® Watches was founded by friends Luigi Generali and Matteo Franchina, driven by a shared passion for watches and family legacy, with the intention to build meaningful Swiss Made mechanical timepieces rooted in emotion and tradition.
2022
Launch of the first Fathers® collection including the Horizon diver watches — designed with robust automatic movements and classic diver aesthetics, inspired by the horizon and nautical themes.
2023
Expansion of Fathers® offerings with GMT and small seconds models (e.g., Globetrotter GMT and Nexus), broadening design expression while keeping a focus on narrative and sentiment in watch design.
2024
Fathers® strengthened its brand identity with limited production pieces and emphasis on symbolic value, promoting watches as heirlooms and emotional mementos alongside technical refinement.
FOUNDED
2021

Naples, Campania, Italy

Festina Group
FESTINA GROUP
History
ARTICLES
The Festina Group
is a major Spanish-based watch conglomerate founded in 1984 when Miguel Rodríguez acquired the Swiss watch brand Festina, which itself dates back to 1902. The group has grown through strategic acquisitions to become one of Europe's significant watch industry players. The Festina Group currently produces five different brands of watches for the global market and maintains manufacturing facilities in Switzerland and Spain, with an estimated 15,000 points of sale across more than 90 countries. They are particularly well-known for their sports marketing initiatives, especially in cycling, where Festina served as the official timekeeper of the Tour de France for many years. The group continues to operate as a family-owned business under the leadership of the Rodríguez family, focusing on offering watches at various price points while maintaining Swiss watchmaking traditions.
Brands summary:
Festina
, the group's flagship brand, is known for sport-oriented timepieces and chronographs. Famous for its Tour de France sponsorship and cycling heritage, the brand offers quartz and automatic watches at mid-range prices, combining Swiss traditions with contemporary design and sports functionality.
Jaguar
, a premium brand within the group, focuses on elegant Swiss-made watches including dress watches and chronographs, blending traditional designs with modern reliability.
Candino
, represents traditional Swiss watchmaking within the group and produces classic timepieces with Swiss movements at entry-level prices.
Lotus
, s the group's fashion-oriented brand. Based in Spain, it offers trendy, affordable watches targeting a younger demographic. 
                          Launched as the group's entry-level brand,
Calypso
specializes in colorful, youth-oriented watches. The collection features sporty designs, digital watches, and fashion-forward models at budget-friendly prices.
FOUNDED
1984
HEADQUARTERS
Madrid, Spain

Festinagroup.com

Flik Flak
FLIK FLAK Official
History
ARTICLES
The Swatch Group established the watch brand Flik Flak in 1987. The firm specializes in the production of timepieces for children that are both of excellent quality and long-lasting. The objective behind the creation of the colourful and informative Flik Flak watches was to provide a means by which young children might acquire the skill of telling time in a manner that was both entertaining and instructive. The manufacturer provides a diverse selection of patterns for children, including those that include well-known characters, animals, and a variety of other amusing topics. The timekeeping at Flik Flak is renowned for its dependability and precision, and the company is also noted for its dedication to quality and safety. The timepieces produced by this manufacturer are made from long-lasting materials and are built to resist the wear and tear that comes with regular, daily activities.
1987
The Flik Flak brand is founded by the Swatch Group with the aim of creating high-quality and durable watches for children.
1988
Flik Flak launches its first collection of watches, featuring colorful designs and easy-to-read dials.
1990
Flik Flak expands its product range to include watches with popular children's characters, as well as watches with additional features such as alarms and timers.
2000
Flik Flak continues to expand its product range, introducing new collections with themes such as sports, nature, and music.
2010
Flik Flak partners with well-known children's brands, such as Disney and Hello Kitty, to create popular and engaging watch designs.
2015
Flik Flak launches a new range of watches with interactive stories that can be accessed through a mobile app, adding an educational and storytelling element to the watches.

The Fossil Group
, founded in 1984 by Tom Kartsotis, has evolved from a fashion watch importer to one of the world's largest watch manufacturers and lifestyle brands. Initially focused on retro-styled watches, the company expanded significantly through both brand creation and licensing agreements to produce an impressive variety of designs including the Blue line of sports watches and the Big Tic digital watch. The company would expand in the 21st century by acquiring
Zodiac
(2001),
Michele
(2004), and
Skagen
(2012), which they now produce in addition to licensed timepieces for
Michael Kors
,
Armani Exchange
,
DKNY
, and
Diesel
.
Fossil
is known for operating a vertically integrated business model that includes in-house design, manufacturing, and distribution. They maintain manufacturing facilities in China and assembly operations in various locations worldwide, and in addition to watches, are known for leather goods, jewelry, and accessories.

History
ARTICLES
Fossil is a renowned American lifestyle brand, recognized for its vintage-inspired designs that blend classic charm with contemporary style. Established in 1984, Fossil is celebrated for its high-quality timepieces, which often feature a mix of traditional craftsmanship and modern technology. The brand offers a diverse range of watches, from elegant analog designs to innovative smartwatches, catering to various tastes and preferences. Fossil's commitment to creativity and innovation has solidified its reputation as a leading global brand in the watch industry, appealing to both fashion enthusiasts and tech-savvy individuals.
1984
Fossil is founded by Tom Kartsotis in Richardson, Texas.
1989
Fossil introduces its iconic tin packaging, which becomes a signature element of the brand.
1993
Fossil goes public on the NASDAQ stock exchange.
2001
Fossil acquires the watch brand Zodiac, expanding its portfolio.
2007
Fossil opens its first retail store in Dallas, Texas.
2012
Fossil Group, Inc. acquires Skagen Designs, a Danish watch and lifestyle brand.
2015
Fossil acquires Misfit, a company specializing in wearable technology.
2016
Fossil launches its first line of smartwatches under the Fossil Q series.
2019

Franck Muller
FRANCK MULLER GENÈVE Official
History
ARTICLES
Franck Muller is a premium Swiss watch company that was established in 1991 by Franck Muller and Vartan Sirmakes. Franck Muller is the brand’s namesake. This firm is famous for its forward-thinking designs, intricate motions, and high quality of handicrafting in its products. The one-of-a-kind and sometimes surreal designs of Franck Muller watches are easily recognizable by the brand’s signature elements, which include elaborate complexities and vivid colour palettes. Collectors and watch lovers place a high premium on the watch collections produced by the company. These collections include a diverse array of watch designs, ranging from traditional dress watches to sports and diving watches. In addition to its own in-house movements, Franck Muller partners with a number of other watch brands and manufacturers to produce high-end limited edition timepieces. These watches are often produced in small quantities. Because of the company’s dedication to quality, innovation, and creative expression, it has established itself as a market leader in the luxury watch sector.
1991
Franck Muller and Vartan Sirmakes launch the Franck Muller brand in Geneva, Switzerland, with the aim of creating unique and innovative luxury watches.
1992
The brand introduces its first collection of watches, featuring a distinctive tonneau-shaped case and complicated movements.
1994
Franck Muller introduces the Crazy Hours complication, which features a dial with the hour markers arranged in a seemingly random order, adding an element of whimsy and playfulness to the design.
2000
The brand continues to innovate, introducing new complications and designs, including the Aeternitas Mega 4, which at the time was one of the most complicated watches ever created.
2010
Franck Muller collaborates with other luxury brands, including Rolls-Royce and Roberto Cavalli, to create limited edition watches with unique designs.
FOUNDED
1991

Geneva , Switzerland

FRANCK MULLER GROUP
History
ARTICLES
The Franck Muller Group
, co-founded by prestigious Swiss watchmaker Franck Muller and Armenian-born gem-setter Vartan Sirmakes, is among the smallest groups in the luxury watch world in terms of its overall holdings, but a significant player nonetheless. With three highly respected names in watchmaking under its roof, the
Franck Muller
Group commands more than its share of respect among watch aficionados.
Brands summary:
Muller
was a rising star in the watch world, having built a reputation for crafting high-end tourbillon watches, when he founded his namesake brand in 1992. The brand subsequently became known for its distinctive barrel-shaped cases, colourful dials, and unusual complications including the triple-axis Tourbillon and Crazy Hours. A decade later, Muller helped his employee,
Pierre Kunz
, open a watch brand within the
Franck Muller
Group specializing in retrograde complications. The group would also subsequently acquire
Backes & Strauss
, which was founded in Hanau, Germany in 1789 and distinguishes itself as the world's oldest diamond company, a partnership that would help the historic diamond merchant to diversify into watches. While many of
Backes & Strauss
' timepieces feature diamonds, its creations have a unique visual identity thanks to their signature thick bezels and prominent numerals.
FOUNDED
1992

Frédérique Constant
FREDERIQUE CONSTANT Official
History
ARTICLES
Founded in 1988 by Aletta Bax and Peter Stas, Frederique Constant is a Swiss luxury watch brand offering high-quality timepieces at accessible prices. Known for their classic and elegant designs, the brand utilizes traditional Swiss watchmaking techniques to produce both quartz and automatic watches for men and women. Frederique Constant’s attention to detail includes polished bevels and côtes de Genève decoration on movements. In addition to their philanthropic “Heart Beat” line of watches, the brand is committed to supporting charitable causes.
1988
Aletta and Peter Stas begin designing their own collection of watches.
1988
The 18th Century Collection is released after four years of labour, with Swiss movements assembled by a Geneva watchmaker.
1994
Frederique Constant introduces the first Heart Beat timepiece with a window at 12 o'clock to display the mechanical movement's beating heart. The Heart Beat aperture will become the Brand's signature in the future.
1997
Aletta and Peter Stas relocate to the Geneva suburb of Carouge. First-time exhibitor at Baselworld is Frederique Constant.
2000
Frederique Constant relocates its Carouge offices to a larger production facility in Chêne-Bourg.
2001
The "Live your Passion" slogan represents success-driven individuals like Aletta and Peter Stas. The Brand takes a decisive turn in the same year that Pim Koeslag joins the development team.
2002
Aletta and Peter Stas decide to acquire and resurrect the Swiss watchmaking brand Alpina timepieces in order to expand their product line to include sports timepieces and meet a greater demand.
2004
Launch of the first Manufacture calibre of the brand, the FC-910, developed and constructed wholly in-house. This first Manufacture movement incorporates the iconic Heart Beat aperture at 6 o'clock to commemorate the occasion.
2006
The opening ceremony for the new Manufacture building in Plan-les-Ouates.
2007
Commencement of the partnership with the British automobile manufacturer Austin-Healey and launch of the Vintage Rally collection.
2008
Frederique Constant unveils its first Tourbillon Manufacture limited edition, equipped with a silicon escapement FC-980 Manufacture calibre. Positioned at six o'clock, the tourbillon recalls Frederique Constant's signature Heart Beat aperture on the dial.
2009
The Runabout collection by Frederique Constant is inspired by the eponymous vessels of the booming 1920s. In 2013, Frederique Constant collaborates with the Riva Historical Society, an organization devoted to preserving these renowned wooden vessels.
2012
The identity of a future bestseller by Frederique Constant is revealed. Displaying the time zones of the world's 24 reference cities, this watch is ideal for continually travelling executives. Only the crown can be used to modify the FC-718 Manufacture movement's functions.
2015
The world's first Swiss-made timepiece with connected functions and an analogue dial. The watch features an electronic module affixed on a quartz movement that is connected to the Frederique Constant Smartwatch App.
2016
Aletta and Peter Stas consider the Frederique Constant Group's future and decide to accept Citizen's acquisition offer.
2017
The conventional flyback mechanism is comprised of a series of column wheels that function as a clutching system. The FC-760 Manufacture calibre that powers the Flyback Chronograph Manufacture eliminates this complication by substituting a patented star-shaped wheel for the standard column wheel. This results in a movement with fewer components and operations that is thinner.
2018
Unveiled at the exclusive Carpenter's Gallery, the Classic Hybrid Manufacture timepiece combines a quartz calibre and a mechanical mechanism for the first time in the history of watchmaking.
2018
Prior to joining the Frederique Constant Group in 2012 as Adjunct Commercial Director, Niels Eggerding had spent eleven years in the watchmaking industry. He was elevated to the position of vice president of sales in 2014. In 2018, he was appointed managing director of the Frederique Constant Group due to his excellent business abilities.
2019
The Manufacture was expanded to a total of 6200 m2 in order to increase production capacity and accommodate the increasing demand and expansion of the Group. The Manufacture Experience, a public museum that chronicles the history of the Frederique Constant Group, was also established.
2020
Frederique Constant advances its connected watch offerings by introducing the Smartwatch Vitality line, which features a digital display and an integrated heart rate sensor.
2021
Redefining mechanical timepiece regulation principles • A monolithic silicon oscillator replacing 26 standard components • A novel

History
ARTICLES
Garmin Ltd. is a pioneering technology company in GPS‑based consumer electronics, specializing in navigation and wearable devices. The brand is renowned for combining practical innovation with robust design, targeting outdoor enthusiasts, athletes, pilots, mariners, and everyday users who value accuracy and durability.
Known for reliable GPS and sensor‑based products (like its iconic Forerunner running watches and Marine chartplotters), Garmin blends high‑precision engineering with user‑centered design. Its devices often feature rugged form factors, long battery life, and intuitive interfaces—all built for use in challenging environments. Garmin’s key product categories include fitness wearables, marine electronics, aviation instruments, outdoor handhelds, and automotive GPS units.
1989

2010
Legally incorporated as Garmin Ltd. in Schaffhausen, Switzerland.

History
ARTICLES
Furlan Marri is a neo-vintage micro-watchmaker specializing in emotionally driven, design-led timepieces that draw on mid-20th century watch aesthetics. Known for blending classical chronograph proportions (especially inspired by ‘40s/’50s designs) with modern affordability, the brand combines elegant case forms and refined dial layouts with quartz and mechanical movements, catering to collectors and enthusiasts who appreciate timeless style without ultra-premium pricing.
The brand's design philosophy centers on community, storytelling, and detail: the founders—Andrea Furlan, a Swiss designer, and Hamad Al Marri, a collector and artist—aim to evoke emotional connections, bridging cultures and eras through watches. Their approach emphasizes transparency (about production), craftsmanship in finishing, and a deep respect for horological heritage, while using a business model that remains accessible.
Some of their key products include mecha-quartz chronographs (with a hybrid movement that delivers a mechanical feel), mechanical three-hand sector-dial watches, and more ambitious technical pieces like a perpetual calendar developed in collaboration for Only Watch. The target audience is modern watch lovers—especially younger or design-conscious collectors—who are drawn to vintage motifs but want well-finished, thoughtfully made watches at a relatively modest price point.
2021
Furlan Marri was founded in Geneva by Andrea Furlan and Hamad Al Marri, launching via a Kickstarter campaign that quickly achieved its funding goal in 35 seconds.
2021
Awarded the Grand Prix d’Horlogerie de Genève (GPHG) ‘Horological Revelation’ prize just seven months after founding.
2023
Opened their Geneva office and showroom at Rue du Nant 25, hosting a small team and offering in-person or virtual appointments.
2023
Collaborated with Dominique Renaud and Julien Tixier to create a one-off Secular Perpetual Calendar for Only Watch, signaling the start of a more technical line.
FOUNDED
2021

Geneva, Switzerland

Gallet
History
ARTICLES
Founded in 1826 in La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland, Gallet is one of the oldest and most storied names in Swiss watchmaking, renowned for its pioneering role in precision chronographs and professional timekeeping. From timing the Wright brothers’ first powered flight in 1903 to equipping pilots, motorsport racers, and adventurers with robust, high-performance timepieces, Gallet has been synonymous with innovation, durability, and exploration. With a deep-rooted legacy in aviation, motorsport, and military-grade chronographs, the brand became a staple in the U.S. market and a favorite among professionals who demanded reliability in extreme conditions. Now, as it prepares for a long-awaited revival under Breitling’s stewardship, Gallet is set to reclaim its place as a trailblazer in Swiss watchmaking, blending history with modern craftsmanship.
1826
Gallet was founded by Julien Gallet in La Chaux-de-Fonds
1856
Gallet expanded into the U.S. market.
1890
Jules Racine & Co. became the sole distributor of Gallet watches in North America.
1903
Gallet’s stopwatch timed the first powered flight by the Wright brothers’ Kitty Hawk Flyer.
1907
Gallet acquired Société d’Horlogerie Electa, forming “Fabrique d’Horlogerie Electa, Gallet & Co.”
1926
Marked the end of a key period of technical innovation and international recognition for Gallet.
1938
Gallet introduced the MultiChron Clamshell, one of the first waterproof chronographs.
1939
Gallet launched the Flying Officer, a pioneering waterproof chronograph for multi-timezone tracking.
1940
Rex Mays received the MultiChron Regulator after winning the Springfield Mile auto racing championship.
2025
Breitling announced the acquisition of Gallet.
2026
Breitling plans to relaunch Gallet, marking the brand’s 200th anniversary.
FOUNDED
1826

La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland

History
ARTICLES
The Girard-Perregaux watch brand was established in 1791, making it one of the oldest luxury watch manufacturers in Switzerland. The brand is well-known for its sophisticated watches as well as the technological improvements that it has made during its long history of invention and excellence in the watchmaking industry. During the course of its history, the brand is responsible for a number of significant advancements in horology, including the invention of the wristwatch and numerous other significant timekeeping advances. The timepieces produced by Girard-Perregaux are renowned for their unmatched elegance, outstanding craftsmanship, and meticulous attention to detail. Collectors and watch aficionados all around the globe have an insatiable appetite for the brand’s collections, which include everything from traditional dress watches to timepieces designed for sports and diving.

Geylang Watch Co
History
ARTICLES
Geylang Watch Co is a Singapore-based microbrand specializing in mechanical watches that fuse playful design with heartfelt cultural inspiration. Born from “a lifelong fascination with mechanical watches” and the “peculiarities off the beaten track in a crowded horological world,” the brand channels the vibrant, gritty spirit of Singapore’s Geylang district—especially the 'Lorongs' and back lanes—into its watches’ aesthetic and storytelling.
Design philosophy-wise, Geylang Watch Co emphasizes handcrafted quality and individuality: each watch is hand-assembled, regulated, and tested individually against a backdrop of creativity and grit. Their creations present a cheeky, playful irreverence in an industry sometimes too self-serious—a selling point that sets them apart, appealing to collectors and enthusiasts looking for personality, affordable luxury, and cultural resonance.
2024

Glycine
History
ARTICLES
Glycine is a heritage Swiss tool-watch brand specializing in robust timepieces crafted for aviation, diving and active use. Known for its pioneering GMT and multi-time-zone models (most notably the Airman line), the brand combines bold functional design elements — such as 24-hour dials, rotating bezels, high water resistance — with Swiss mechanical craftsmanship, catering to pilots, divers and serious watch enthusiasts.
Since its founding in 1914, Glycine has sustained a design philosophy rooted in precision, practicality and innovation rather than decorative luxury. It embraces a retro-modern fusion: vintage pilot and diver watch cues (large cases, legible dials, GMT functions) with modern manufacturing and tool-watch durability. Its key products include the Airman series (GMT/aviation oriented) and the Combat / Sea Kraken series (diver-oriented). The target audience is the informed collector or active lifestyle buyer who values functional heritage, robust build quality and Swiss heritage without the ultra-luxury markup. Unique selling points for Glycine include its long and genuine Swiss origin (since 1914) with inventive modules such as the EMSA automatic winding system in the 1930s. Also, its iconic pilot’s watch (Airman) which has real aviation credentials and even ties to space missions.
1914
Founded by Eugène Meylan in Biel/Bienne, Switzerland, marking the beginning of Glycine’s Swiss-watchmaking heritage.
1931
Patent of the EMSA automatic winding module by Glycine, advancing the transition from hand-wound to automatic watches.
1953
Introduction of the Airman model – a pilot’s watch capable of tracking multiple time zones, establishing Glycine’s aviation credentials.
1959
Development of vacuum-sealed watch cases (patented CH 355742), enhancing durability & water-resistance for tool-watches.
2016
Acquired by the Invicta Watch Group, bringing Glycine under new ownership while preserving its Swiss identity and model lines.
FOUNDED
1914
HEADQUARTERS
Biel/Bienne, Switzerland

Glycine-watch.ch

History
ARTICLES
Google is a premium consumer electronics brand specializing in smartwatches, particularly the Pixel Watch series. Known for seamlessly combining Google’s software intelligence with Fitbit’s health‑tracking expertise, the brand integrates circular, domed-glass designs with advanced sensors and AI‑powered functionality. Their design philosophy emphasizes elegant, minimalist aesthetics—the stainless-steel, water-resistant (5 ATM) Pixel Watch features a bold, domed display and a proprietary band‑attach mechanism inspired by camera lens mounts. This allows easy customization with a variety of woven, stretch, active, leather, and metal bands.
Key products include the first‑generation Pixel Watch (launched October 2022), Pixel Watch 2 (October 2023), and Pixel Watch 3 (September 2024), each improving health‑tracking, display area, battery life, and Wear OS integration. The audience includes tech‑savvy Android users, fitness-conscious individuals, and existing Pixel ecosystem customers seeking stylish yet powerful smartwatches with deep Google services integration.
2021
Google acquires Fitbit for USD 2.1 billion and integrates Fitbit’s health platform and team into its hardware division.
2022
Launch of Google’s first‑party Pixel Watch announced October 6 and released October 13; marks Google’s debut as a smartwatch manufacturer.
2023
Release of Pixel Watch 2 on October 12 with recycled-aluminum housing, improved sensors (heart rate, skin temperature, stress) and Wear OS 4.
2024
Pixel Watch 3 officially announced on August 13 and released Sept 10; introduced two sizes (41 mm and 45 mm), larger displays, Wear OS 5, and ‘loss of pulse’ detection.
FOUNDED

Gérald Genta
History
ARTICLES
Gérald Genta is a prestigious Swiss haute horlogerie brand rooted in the visionary work of legendary watch designer Gérald Charles Genta. Known historically as one of the most influential figures in modern watchmaking, Genta designed some of the industry’s most iconic watches — including the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak and Patek Philippe Nautilus — before establishing his eponymous brand in 1969 to bring his own creative visions to life.
The brand combines bold aesthetic innovation with high watchmaking craftsmanship, emphasizing avant-garde design, complicated mechanical movements, and artistic expression. Early Gérald Genta creations included groundbreaking designs and horological innovations such as the ultra-thin automatic perpetual calendar and Grande Sonnerie — a world-class chiming wristwatch developed over several years.
1969
Gérald Genta founded his eponymous brand with a mission to create extraordinary watches that reflected his personal artistic vision, after years of designing legendary models for other major manufacturers.
2000
The original Gérald Genta company was sold (and ultimately integrated into Bulgari), marking the end of its initial independent era.
2011
Gérald Genta, the founder and visionary behind the brand and countless horological icons, passed away, leaving behind thousands of designs and an enduring legacy.
2023
Gérald Genta was relaunched under **LVMH’s La Fabrique du Temps Louis Vuitton** atelier with access to Genta’s archives and guidance from master watchmakers, focusing on high watchmaking and limited productions.

1969

Grand Seiko is a prestigious Japanese watch brand renowned for its precision, craftsmanship, and elegant design. Established in 1960 as a subsidiary of Seiko, Grand Seiko aims to produce high-end timepieces that rival the finest Swiss watches. Each watch is meticulously crafted, combining traditional techniques with cutting-edge technology. Notable for their Spring Drive and Hi-Beat movements, Grand Seiko watches are celebrated for their exceptional accuracy and distinctive aesthetic, embodying the Japanese principles of simplicity, beauty, and excellence. The brand has earned a dedicated following among watch enthusiasts for its commitment to quality and innovation.

History
ARTICLES
Graham is a heritage‑inspired luxury watch brand with roots dating back to 1695 in London and now firmly based in the Swiss watchmaking hub of La Chaux‑de‑Fonds. The brand blends British legacy and Swiss precision, reviving the name of the renowned watchmaker George Graham under Swiss‑British ownership beginning in 1995, exactly 300 years after its origins.
Known for its Chronofighter Vintage series and related collections like Chronofighter Superlight, Swordfish, and Fortress, Graham features watches with signature elements such as the thumb‑operated trigger chronograph and vintage aviation‑inspired styling in materials like bronze that develop patina over time. Crafted in limited series, Graham’s mechanical timepieces emphasize independence, craftsmanship, and distinctive design, targeted at connoisseurs who appreciate bold retro‑modern styling, mechanical sophistication, and exclusivity.
1695
Brand originally created by George Graham in London, renown watchmaker.
1995
Revival of Graham brand by Swiss and British enthusiasts, exactly 300 years after original foundation.
2020

History

Gruppo Gamma is a heritage-inspired microbrand specializing in rugged tool watches that draw from naval and military diving history. Known for their robust, vintage-inspired designs, the brand combines clean, legible dials and sturdy case construction with modern production techniques and reliable automatic or hand-wound movements. Their watches evoke the character of mid-20th century diver instruments while remaining practical for contemporary use.
The design philosophy centers on simplicity, function, and “no-fuss” usability: Gruppo Gamma aims to create watches that are “easy to read, fuss-free to operate and good enough for daily adventures.” Their brand name itself is a tribute to the elite Italian naval frogmen unit called Gruppo Gamma, known for its underwater reconnaissance missions — tying the brand’s identity to exploration, durability, and legacy.

History
ARTICLES
H. Moser & Cie. is a distinguished Swiss luxury watch manufacturer known for its exceptional craftsmanship and innovative designs. Founded in 1828 by Heinrich Moser in St. Petersburg, Russia, the company quickly gained recognition for producing high-quality timepieces favored by Russian aristocracy and members of the Imperial Court. In 1848, Moser returned to his hometown of Schaffhausen, Switzerland, where he established a factory that employed several hundred artisans, significantly contributing to the region’s industrialization. Today, H. Moser & Cie. operates as an independent, family-owned company, producing around 3,000 watches annually and maintaining its legacy of horological excellence.

Gustave & Cie
History
ARTICLES
Gustave & Cie is a contemporary French watchmaking house that specializes in mono-hand (single-hand) watches—where one hand elegantly indicates both hours and minutes in either a 12-hour or 24-hour format. Founded by designers Timothée Barbier and Guillaume Rémond, the brand draws inspiration from the innovative spirit of Gustave Eiffel and blends timeless minimalism with a modern twist. Their timepieces, envisioned as both urban and adventurous, feature thoughtful design, quality components, and meticulous craftsmanship. Watches are designed in Paris, with cases assembled in Besançon and final assembly—including bracelet fitting and packaging—handled in-house in Paris. They use Swiss or Japanese movements, aimed at delivering French-made quality with affordable sophistication.
2014
Founding of Gustave & Cie by Timothée Barbier and Guillaume Rémond—friends from the University of Burgundy in Dijon—drawing inspiration from Gustave Eiffel and launching with the mono-aiguille watch concept.

2025

HYT
HYT WATCHES Official
History
ARTICLES
HYT is a Swiss luxury watchmaker renowned for its groundbreaking integration of fluid mechanics with traditional horology. Established in 2012, HYT introduced a unique approach to timekeeping by utilizing fluids to display time, drawing inspiration from ancient water clocks known as clepsydras. This innovative design features two immiscible liquids—one colored and one transparent—that move within a glass capillary tube to indicate hours, driven by bellows that expand and contract in harmony with the mechanical movement. This seamless fusion of fluid dynamics and mechanical engineering exemplifies HYT’s commitment to challenging conventional watchmaking norms and redefining the art of time measurement.
FOUNDED
2012

Neuchâtel, Switzerland

ICE HOLDING GROUP
History
ARTICLES
Ice Holding Group
, founded in 2006 with the creation of
Ice-Watch
by Belgian entrepreneur Jean-Pierre Lutgen, has grown into a distinctive watchmaking group based in Geneva, Switzerland. 
        The group is recognized for combining affordability with expressive design, introducing vibrant and fashionable Swiss watches that appeal to a global audience. 
        Over the years, Ice Holding Group has diversified its portfolio to blend accessible fashion timepieces with innovative Swiss-Made collections. The company gained international visibility through its flagship brand
Ice-Watch
, known for colorful designs, celebrity collaborations, and accessible pricing. 
        In 2025, the group expanded with the launch of
WatchPeople
, a Swiss-Made brand positioned under the philosophy that "freedom is the only luxury," offering authentic and original watches starting from €99. 
        Together, these brands allow Ice Holding Group to reach both the fashion-oriented consumer and the Swiss-Made enthusiast seeking accessibility without compromising quality.
Brands summary:
From the expressive and fashion-driven
Ice-Watch
to the inclusive and value-driven
WatchPeople
,
Ice Holding Group
demonstrates its ability to adapt to evolving watchmaking trends. With Ice-Watch’s global reach and WatchPeople’s Swiss-Made authenticity at affordable prices, the group offers timepieces that embody color, originality, and accessibility for a wide range of audiences.

Hublot is also known for its collaborations with other luxury brands and personalities, such as Ferrari, and Pelé, and for its commitment to traditional watchmaking techniques. INVICTA WATCH GROUP
History
ARTICLES
Invicta Watch Group
traces its origins to 1837, 
                                when Raphael Picard founded the company in La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland. 
                                Originally dedicated to crafting high-quality mechanical watches at accessible prices, 
                                the brand’s name—
Invicta
, meaning “invincible” in Latin—has long represented resilience and innovation in watchmaking.
After being re-established in Hollywood, Florida, in 1991 under investor
Eyal Lalo
, Invicta evolved into a global powerhouse known for its bold designs and “affordable luxury” philosophy. 
                                The company’s watches combine Swiss precision with modern flair, offering robust stainless-steel cases, 
                                flame-fusion crystals, and distinctive oversized dials.
In 2016, Invicta Watch Group expanded by acquiring the historic Swiss brand
Glycine
, 
                                reinforcing its commitment to preserving traditional horology while pursuing modern innovation. 
                                The Group also owns
TechnoMarine
and
S. Coifman
, 
                                extending its reach across luxury, sport, and lifestyle markets.
With headquarters in Florida, USA, and a continued Swiss design heritage, 
                                Invicta Watch Group today represents a fusion of craftsmanship, accessibility, 
                                and bold expression—offering over 30 collections that blend technical mastery 
                                with distinctive visual identity for collectors and enthusiasts worldwide.

Invicta
Invicta Watch Official
Loading videos...
0/0
History
ARTICLES
Invicta is a Swiss-founded watchmaker (est. 1837) now headquartered in Hollywood, Florida. The brand specializes in bold, sporty timepieces and “affordable luxury” watches. Known for expertly built timepieces at accessible prices, Invicta offers large stainless-steel cases, eye-catching dials, and robust features (e.g. high water resistance and flame-fusion crystals) that combine classic horological elements (like Swiss movements and sapphire crystal) with modern technology. Its key collections – such as the diver-inspired Pro Diver, the premium Reserve series, and the racing-themed Invicta Racing models – embody a fusion of vintage-inspired design and contemporary innovation. The brand’s styling often “blends timeless elegance with modern design”, catering to fashion-conscious buyers and watch collectors who want statement-making watches on a budget.
Invicta’s design philosophy emphasizes meticulous craftsmanship and original concepts. The company prides itself on pushing “creative and technical revelations” in case construction and styling, which is evident in its over 30 distinct collections spanning diving, sport, and ornamental themes. Privately owned and still true to its founding Swiss ideology, Invicta offers features like automatic/chronograph movements and custom materials that appeal to both enthusiasts and general consumers. Its target audience ranges from younger, style-oriented buyers drawn to bold, oversized watches, to seasoned collectors seeking quality at value prices. In essence, Invicta’s unique selling points are its retro-modern aesthetic (rooted in Swiss heritage), its commitment to robust build quality (“meticulous art of case construction”), and its broad accessibility through affordable pricing.
1837
Founded by watchmaker Raphael Picard in La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland (Latin: Invicta means "invincible"), with a mission to offer high-quality mechanical timepieces at accessible prices.
1991
Acquired by investor Eyal Lalo; re-established in Hollywood, Florida, as Invicta Watch Company of America.  Lalo refocused the brand on modern automatic and quartz watches that blend Swiss movements and design with affordable pricing.
2000
Early 2000s – Introduced the Lupah and Pro Diver collections, featuring oversized cases (up to ~50 mm) and bold designs. These popular lines helped establish Invicta’s identity as a maker of dramatic, sporty watches.
2007
Launch of the Russian Diver series, based on a vintage Soviet naval prototype; originally a limited-edition release (2007) that became one of Invicta’s staple collections.
2016
Acquired historic Swiss brand Glycine Watch SA (founded 1914), expanding its portfolio while pledging to preserve Glycine’s identity.
2020
Partnered with NBA legend Shaquille O’Neal to create the Shaq Signature Series, a line of oversized specialty watches co-designed with the athlete.
FOUNDED
1837
HEADQUARTERS
Florida, United States
WEBSITE
Invictawatch.com
THE HEARTBEAT OF TORONTO: WHEN FOREVER LASTED EIGHTEEN INNINGS

Jakob Eitan
History
ARTICLES
Jakob Eitan is a luxury watch brand based in Milton and is owned by Colin Potts a Milton resident.
The name Jakob Eitan is derived from those of my twin boys.  When they were born, my wife Samantha, celebrated by designing the stylized “JE” and committing to it as a tattoo for her wrist.  When the brand was formed, the “JE” symbol seemed a fitting choice, and became the Jakob Eitan logo.
February 2021 saw the release of the first Jakob Eitan watch with a blue dial, and recently in December of 2021 a second release with black dial.
When creating these watches, materials and quality were of the utmost importance.  As a result, the watches are made of solid 316 Stainless Steel housing a Sapphire crystal, the standard in luxury watch making, and has a high quality Japanese quartz movement. The 1970s were a tumultuous, but exciting time for watchmaking around the world.  It was the dawn of the quartz watch when released to market in 1969.  This release put the Swiss watch industry under heavy pressure to catch up to quartz technology, which was changing the nature and market for mechanical watches.  The crisis was a catalyst for creativity, and the watch industry responded with bold and angular designs.  These 1970s watches have become modern classics, and it is from this design language that the Jakob Eitan sports watch draws.
I am honoured to have the opportunity to share with you my passion for watches and watchmaking.
FOUNDED
2021
HEADQUARTERS
Milton, Canada

History

Jaeger-LeCoultre is a luxury watch brand based in Switzerland. The company was founded in 1833 by Antoine LeCoultre, and it has a long history of producing high-quality watches and movements. Jaeger-LeCoultre is known for its wide range of watch styles, including dress watches, sports watches, and complications watches, as well as its use of advanced technology. The brand is particularly well-known for its complications watches, such as the Reverso and the Master series, and for its use of in-house movements. Jaeger-LeCoultre is also known for its commitment to preserving traditional watchmaking techniques and for its collaborations with other luxury brands. The brand is considered as one of the most prestigious and traditional watchmakers in the industry.

History
ARTICLES
Jaipur Watch Company is an Indian artisanal watchmaker specializing in handcrafted luxury timepieces that transform historical artifacts, antique coins, and cultural heritage into functional art. Known for its "storytelling through watches" philosophy, the brand combines reclaimed materials (pre-independence coins, vintage typewriter keys, heritage wood) with Swiss/Japanese movements, creating historically significant wearable pieces. Key collections include Coin Watches (British Raj-era coins), Typewriter Key Watches (repurposed keys), and Art Deco-inspired designs, targeting history enthusiasts, collectors, and conscious luxury consumers who value cultural preservation and uniqueness. The brand’s design ethos centers on reviving forgotten narratives – each piece documents India’s heritage through materials like wood from 200-year-old havelis (mansions) or Partition-era artifacts. Unique selling points include limited-edition craftsmanship (each watch numbered), sustainable luxury (upcycled materials), and personalized provenance tracking.
2013
Founded by Gaurav Mehta in Jaipur, launching India's first micro-luxury watch brand with a focus on transforming antique coins into wearable history.
2015
Introduced the signature 'Coin Watch' collection using authentic British India-era silver coins, gaining recognition for cultural preservation.
2017
Launched the 'Typewriter Series', repurposing vintage typewriter keys into watch dials. Featured at India Design ID, establishing art-meets-horology niche.
2018
Collaborated with UNESCO for a limited edition 'Heritage Watch' supporting monument conservation, using stone from historical sites.
2019
Expanded to 'Wood Collection' with dials crafted from reclaimed heritage wood (e.g., 150-year-old haveli doors).
2020
Received international acclaim when Prime Minister Narendra Modi gifted a custom 'Pride of India' coin watch to Denmark's PM.
2021
Launched 'Partition Series' using artifacts from 1947 India-Pakistan Partition, documenting refugee heritage through horology.
2023
Debuted 'Astrolabe Collection' featuring astronomical instrument designs and collaborations with Indian astronomers.
2024
Opened experiential atelier in Jaipur, offering watchmaking workshops and material provenance tours.
FOUNDED
2013

Rajasthan, India

Junghans
JUNGHANSGERMANY Official
Loading videos...

History
ARTICLES
Junghans is a German watch brand that has been creating timepieces for over 150 years. Founded in 1861 by Erhard Junghans and his brother-in-law Jakob Zeller-Tobler, the brand began as a small clock factory in Schramberg, Germany. Over the years, Junghans has become known for its innovative designs and high-quality craftsmanship.
One of Junghans’ most iconic watches is the Max Bill, which was designed by the Swiss architect and artist of the same name. The Max Bill watch is characterized by its minimalist design, clean lines, and understated elegance. It has become a classic timepiece and is still in production today.
In addition to the Max Bill, Junghans has created a wide range of other watches, including chronographs, dress watches, and sports watches. The brand is known for its use of high-quality materials, such as stainless steel, sapphire crystal, and leather.
Today, Junghans continues to produce watches in Germany and has a reputation for precision and reliability. The brand has won numerous awards for its designs and has collaborated with artists and designers to create unique and innovative timepieces. Overall, Junghans is a respected and admired brand in the world of watchmaking.
1861
On 14 April 1861, Erhard Junghans and his brother-in-law founded the watch component factory 'Zeller & Junghans' in Lauterbachtal valley. Initially producing parts for large Black Forest clocks, Junghans created its first complete clocks in 1866.
1888
Junghans registers a new trademark featuring a five-pointed star with the letter 'J'. Two years later, the star is stylised into eight points and the full brand name is added.
1903
With 3,000 employees producing over three million timepieces per year, Junghans becomes the largest watch and clock factory in the world, expanding its factories and subsidiaries across Europe.
1918
Arthur Junghans completes a terrace-style factory structure to optimize daylight in workspaces, allowing high-precision manufacturing in the steep Black Forest valley.
1936
Junghans fits its own designed movements into wristwatches, introducing the J80 calibre. The legendary '16 weckermädle' alarm girls boost advertising worldwide.
1949
Junghans develops the first wristwatch chronographs with the in-house J88 calibre, becoming the image of a typical pilot watch and securing contracts with the newly-formed Bundeswehr.
1956
Junghans becomes the third largest chronometer manufacturer in the world. The J83 automatic chronometer calibre is introduced, becoming highly sought-after by collectors.
1961
Max Bill designs purist wristwatches for Junghans, establishing design classics that combine aesthetic purity with the latest technical advancements.
1971
Junghans develops the first German quartz wristwatch in a pre-production series, the Astro-Quartz, marking a new chapter in watchmaking history.
1972
At the Munich Olympic Games, Junghans serves as official timekeeper, setting new standards in electronic time measurement and accurate sports timing.
1990
Junghans engineers miniaturise radio control technology for wristwatches, launching the Junghans Mega 1, the world's first radio-controlled wristwatch.
1993
The Mega Solar Ceramic is introduced, a solar-powered radio-controlled wristwatch with integrated antenna, representing Junghans' pinnacle in accuracy and design.
2004
Junghans develops a multi-frequency radio-controlled movement, enabling watches to receive time signals from Europe, North America, and Asia for automatic adjustment.
2008
The Erhard Junghans 1 mechanical watch is launched, combining historic inspiration with modern technical processes, limited to 12 pieces worldwide.
2009
Following insolvency, Junghans regains independence under new owners Dr. Hans-Jochem Steim and Hannes Steim, setting a new course for the future.
2011
Junghans celebrates its 150th anniversary with special anniversary watches, events at the company site, and the opening of the new Junghans company museum.
2013
The Steim family expands the Schramberg site, adding an assembly line for radio-controlled movements and investing in the development of a new generation of wristwatch movements.
2015
Junghans introduces a smaller multi-frequency radio-controlled solar movement 615.84 for the ladies' timepiece range, symbolizing continued innovation.
2016
The Meister Pilot series is introduced, blending the classic 1955 Junghans pilot watch design with the modern Meister range aesthetics.
2017
The Meister Driver watch evokes the era of early motoring, combining classic car design inspiration with mechanical watch craftsmanship.
2018
Junghans celebrates the 100th birthday of the terrace building with limited-edition Meister Chronoscope Terrassenbau models and dev

Kelton

History
ARTICLES
Kelton is a nostalgic yet vibrant French watch brand rooted in everyday affordability and playful design. Originally launched in 1955 through a partnership between TIMEX and French watchmaker Stéphane Boullier, Kelton quickly became beloved for its accessible pricing and unconventional distribution—selling timepieces through tobacco shops, press stands, and stationery stores, rather than traditional jewelers—making it a pop-cultural icon in mid-20th-century France.
Today, Kelton lives on with a modern, retro-inspired aesthetic deeply infused with pop, racing, and vintage sensibilities. The brand’s current collections—such as the Colorama line, the sporty RC models, and commemorative 1955 diving editions—highlight bold colors, oval or angular cases, quartz and automatic movements, and French design paired with assembly in France. Kelton’s target audience spans style-conscious trendsetters and watch enthusiasts seeking affordably priced, whimsical, and well-made timepieces that celebrate French heritage and craftsmanship.
1955
Kelton is founded through a collaboration between TIMEX and Stéphane Boullier (of VIXA), creating a new French brand assembled initially in Besançon.
1962
TIMEX opens the first Kelton production plant in Besançon, enabling rapid scale-up in annual sales.
1969
