History
ARTICLES
Kate Spade is a renowned lifestyle brand known for its playful sophistication and crisp, colorful aesthetic. Founded in 1993 by Kate Brosnahan Spade, the brand initially focused on handbags before expanding its range to include clothing, accessories, and home decor. Kate Spade watches epitomize the brand's signature style, featuring whimsical details, vibrant colors, and classic yet contemporary designs. Each timepiece combines functionality with fashion, appealing to those who appreciate both quality craftsmanship and a fun, spirited approach to accessorizing. Kate Spade watches often include unique elements like quirky motifs and elegant touches, making them a delightful addition to any wardrobe.
1993
Kate Spade New York was founded by Katherine Noel Brosnahan (Kate Spade) and her husband Andy Spade. The brand initially focused on handbags and quickly gained popularity.
2000

2007
Kate Spade at Home was launched, extending the brand’s offerings to home decor items.
2015
The brand started producing watches, which were manufactured by the Fossil Group. This collaboration brought in a range of fashionable and versatile designs, including both analog and smartwatch options.
2017
Tapestry Inc. (formerly Coach Inc.) acquired Kate Spade & Company for $2.4 billion, further expanding the brand’s presence in the global market.

History
ARTICLES
Jowissa is a Swiss watch brand specializing in elegant, Swiss-made timepieces that blend traditional craftsmanship with contemporary design. Founded in 1951 by Josef Wyss in Bettlach, Switzerland, Jowissa has evolved into a third-generation family-run business. The brand is renowned for its signature faceted crystal designs, which add a touch of brilliance to each watch, and its commitment to offering high-quality Swiss watches at accessible prices. Jowissa's collections cater to both men and women, featuring a range of styles from classic to modern, ensuring there's a timepiece for every taste.
The design philosophy of Jowissa emphasizes bold aesthetics and vibrant colors, aiming to make a statement on the wrist. Each watch is assembled by hand in Switzerland, ensuring meticulous attention to detail and adherence to Swiss quality standards. The brand's dedication to innovation is evident in its use of unique materials and design techniques, such as the incorporation of cut glass reminiscent of gemstones, which has been a hallmark since the 1960s. Jowissa's watches are not just timekeepers but also fashion accessories that reflect personal style and sophistication.
1951
Founded in Bettlach, Switzerland, by Josef Wyss, marking the beginning of Jowissa's legacy in Swiss watchmaking.
2025
Celebrated 70 years of Swiss craftsmanship, continuing to produce high-quality, stylish watches that blend tradition with modernity.

History
ARTICLES
Laco is a renowned German watch company with a proud heritage spanning decades of crafting
                    high-quality timepieces. Emerging onto the scene in 1925, Laco quickly established itself as a leader in precision watchmaking. Evolving
                    over the years, Laco has become synonymous with aviation-inspired designs, capturing the essence of
                    adventure and exploration.
At the heart of each Laco timepiece lies the hallmark of "Made in Germany" craftsmanship, a testament to the brand's unwavering commitment to quality and excellence. Crafted with precision and attention to detail, every watch embodies the fusion of traditional craftmanship with modern innovation.
From classic pilot watches reminiscent of the golden age of aviation to contemporary interpretations that combine style with functionality, Laco offers a diverse range of designs to suit every taste and preference. Whether it's the enduring elegance of a navy watch or the rugged durability of a squad watch, each timepiece exudes timeless sophistication and charm.
Laco watches are more than just accessories; they are symbols of craftsmanship, passion, and the relentless pursuit of horological perfection. With a Laco watch on your wrist, you carry with you a piece of history and a testament to the enduring legacy of German watchmaking excellence.

Link2Care
History
ARTICLES
Link2Care is a forward-thinking health technology company specializing in smart wearable devices that integrate Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) with Western medical practices. Their flagship product, the Watch2Care, is a 4G-enabled hybrid smartwatch that acts as an independent hub, connecting various IoT devices to provide comprehensive health monitoring. This integration allows for the collection of both Western biometric data and TCM pulse information, offering a holistic view of the user's health in real-time.
The accompanying Link2Care app processes this data to generate a daily health score, delivering personalized, actionable insights to users. Additionally, the platform connects users to the Link2Care Alliance, a curated marketplace of validated supplements, ensuring that users have access to effective wellness products. Endorsed by the Federation of Hong Kong Watch Trade and Industries Ltd., Link2Care aims to set new standards in health monitoring through interoperability and strategic partnerships.
2025
Launch of the Watch2Care series, including the Watch2Care Vital and Watch2Care Vital Pro, integrating Western and TCM health monitoring.

Introduction of the Link2Care app, providing users with daily health scores and personalized insights.

2025

The company emphasizes a diverse range, from dive and pilot to field, dress, and solar watches, with both automatic and Ameriquartz models assembled in the USA

Locke & King was founded in 2020 by Hamilton, Ontario entrepreneur Ryan Moran. Following the loss of
                    his job due to the impacts of the pandemic, he chose to view this challenge as a turning point in
                    his career, a time to do his own thing. Having always loved watches, Moran dreamt of creating a
                    Canadian timepiece company, one that reflected the “roll up your sleeves,” underdog character of his
                    hometown, and created classically designed, enduring automatic watches for everyday heroes to “make
                    their own time.”
                    Since then, Locke & King - named for an intersection in Ryan’s Hamilton neighbourhood - has already
                    proven popular among collectors and casual buyers alike, selling out its first line to customers
                    both across Canada and the world.

LIP is a historic French watch brand celebrated for its fusion of timeless design, exceptional craftsmanship, and cutting-edge innovation. Founded in 1867 in Besançon, the heart of France’s watchmaking heritage, LIP has been a pioneer in the industry for over 150 years. The brand is synonymous with iconic creations that have adorned the wrists of notable figures, including General Charles de Gaulle and Sir Winston Churchill.
LIP’s collections reflect a perfect balance of tradition and modernity, offering a wide range of designs from vintage-inspired models like the classic Henriette and Nautic-Ski to bold, contemporary pieces like the Mach 2000. Known for its quality movements and meticulous attention to detail, LIP watches cater to discerning enthusiasts who value both history and style.
With a commitment to sustainability and a proud “Made in France” ethos, LIP continues to innovate while staying true to its roots, making it a symbol of enduring elegance and French savoir-faire in the watchmaking world.

History
ARTICLES
Louis Vuitton is a French luxury maison specializing in leather goods, fashion, accessories, and high-end watchmaking. Renowned for its artisanal heritage and iconic retro-contemporary design (including the emblematic monogram and hardware inspired by its historic trunks), the brand blends timeless elegance with modern innovation. It appeals to a global clientele who value exclusivity, exceptional craftsmanship, and the ability to express personal style through luxury.
In the watchmaking field, Louis Vuitton embodies a unique fusion of travel spirit, creativity, and horological savoir-faire. Since the launch of its emblematic Tambour collection in 2002—with its distinctive drum-shaped case—the brand has expanded into fine watchmaking, combining bold aesthetics with technical mastery. Highlights include Escale (with refined proportions, trunk-inspired details, and in-house movements), Spin Time (using rotating cubes instead of hands), Voyager, and Horizon (smartwatches tailored to modern travel). These timepieces target both luxury connoisseurs and contemporary travelers seeking exceptional design with functional innovation.
1854
Founded by Louis Vuitton in Paris; introduced lightweight, airtight flat-topped trunks.
2002
Launch of the Tambour collection, an emblematic watch line with a distinctive drum-shaped case.
2003
Won the GPHG Best Ladies’ Watch Award for the Clipper model.
2011
Acquired La Fabrique du Temps, enabling the development of in-house high-watchmaking movements.
2017
Introduced the Tambour Horizon smartwatch, merging digital connectivity with Louis Vuitton aesthetics.
2024
Presented the Escale Cabinet of Wonders high-watchmaking series (Koi’s Garden, Snake’s Jungle, Dragon’s Cloud) showcasing exceptional artisanal techniques.

History
ARTICLES
Louis Erard is an independent Swiss mechanical watchmaker positioned in the affordable‑luxury niche. Known for its specialization in regulator‑layout and complication watches, the brand blends traditional Swiss craftsmanship with creative, contemporary collaborations. Rooted in the Jura watchmaking region, it emphasizes accessibility without compromising mechanical excellence.
Under the leadership of Manuel Emch and Alain Spinedi, Louis Erard has made a name for itself through retro‑modern fusion: classic regulator movements and hand‑finished details meet playful design twists via partnerships with designers like Alain Silberstein, Vianney Halter, Konstantin Chaykin and others. The brand stands out for offering limited‑edition artistic timepieces at competitive price points, appealing to mechanically minded collectors who seek originality and Swiss horological integrity.
1929

History
ARTICLES
Luminox is a renowned watch brand known for its durable and reliable timepieces designed for demanding conditions. Established in 1989, Luminox has carved a niche in the market by providing watches that offer unparalleled visibility in low-light and no-light situations. Their proprietary Luminox Light Technology (LLT) ensures that the watches remain luminous for up to 25 years without requiring an external light source or battery power. This feature makes Luminox watches a favorite among military personnel, law enforcement, and outdoor enthusiasts. The brand is also recognized for its rugged design, Swiss craftsmanship, and commitment to precision and performance.

LVMH Group
LVMH | Louis Vuitton Moët Hennessy
History
ARTICLES
LVMH
, this global luxury conglomerate may be named for fashion (Louis Vuitton), champagne (Moët), and spirits (Hennessy), but its growing portfolio of respected watch brands makes it one of the most important players in the industry. Created in 1987 through the merger of Moët Hennessy and Louis Vuitton, and led by Bernard Arnault since 1989, LVMH helped to usher in the current era of global luxury brands. These days LVMH is probably most recognized for helping to supercharge
TAG Heuer
's brand identity through bold redesigns, partnerships with Formula 1 and Porsche, and high-profile ambassadors including Ryan Gosling and Max Verstappen. Before that, however, the group helped to turn
Hublot
from a relatively minor player in Swiss watchmaking to one of the most popular watch brands in the world. And that's to say nothing of Louis Vuitton, which has in recent years, through massive investment in its watchmaking infrastructure, become an increasingly relevant player in the high-end watch market.
Brands summary:
LVMH oversees a diverse portfolio of watch brands representing some of the most iconic models and respected movement-makers in the game. Among these is
TAG Heuer
, whose archive of highly collectible models includes the Monaco, Carrera, and Autavia.
Hublot
, meanwhile, continues to explore its "Art of Fusion" philosophy through industry-first creations like the colourful "Sapphire Aluminium oXide and rare Earth Mineral" (SAXEM) used for its Big Bang Tourbillons, and limited-editions in ceramic and carbon. There's also
Zenith
, makers of the famous El Primero chronograph,
Bulgari
, whose Octo Finissimo timepieces have broken several records for thinness, and
Chaumet
, the former jeweler to Napoleon's court. The recent acquisition and reanimation of shuttered indie watch brand Daniel Roth demonstrates LVMH's continued ambitions for expansion and diversification.
FOUNDED
1987
HEADQUARTERS
Paris, France

Maison Boanton
History
ARTICLES
Maison Boanton is a French independent luxury watchmaker specializing in elegantly crafted mechanical wristwatches that blend timeless design heritage with contemporary refinement. The brand draws deep inspiration from classic design movements — notably the geometric precision of Art Deco and the elegant simplicity of the 1950s — to produce timepieces with balanced proportions, thoughtful details, and enduring aesthetic appeal rather than fleeting trends.
Boanton’s design philosophy centers on heritage-rooted aesthetics, artisanal craftsmanship, and technical quality. Each watch is conceived in a collaborative design studio featuring experienced designers and watchmakers, and brought to life through meticulous development, machining, finishing, and hand-assembly processes that can take up to two years per model. The brand uses quality Swiss movements — for example, La Joux-Perret automatic calibres — and durable materials such as 316L stainless steel with double-domed sapphire crystals, delivering both refined beauty and wearable performance.
Key products include the Emblème collection — a signature line with geometric cases and lacquered dials celebrating Art Deco heritage — the Héritage (a limited edition automatic with racing-inspired cues), and special pieces like the Emblème Pink Edition, created in support of the IMAGYN association for women’s cancer awareness. Maison Boanton’s target audience consists of collectors and discerning buyers who appreciate timeless design, craftsmanship, and symbolic meaning in their mechanical watches.
2024
Maison Boanton was founded by passionate watch enthusiasts and experts led by founder Hassan Sefrioui, bringing together designers with experience from Cartier and luxury watchmaking to revive heritage design in contemporary watches.
2025
Launch of the Emblème collection — Maison Boanton’s signature range blending Art Deco inspired geometry, lacquered dials, and contemporary watchmaking, reflecting timeless design and Swiss mechanical precision.
2025
Introduction of the Héritage limited edition watch — a 40 mm automatic mechanical piece powered by the La Joux-Perret G100 calibre with 68-hour power reserve, paying homage to classic automotive design and limited to 252 numbered examples.
2025
Release of the Emblème Pink Edition — a special 24-piece limited series with a pink lacquer dial in support of the IMAGYN association for breast cancer awareness, highlighting the brand’s commitment to meaningful collaborations.
FOUNDED
2024
HEADQUARTERS
Saint-Germain-en-Laye, France

Maison Montignac
History
ARTICLES
Maison Montignac is a premium French watchmaking brand that expertly blends urban, sporty, and classic styles into its creations. Rooted in an aesthetic that’s both elegant and creative, each timepiece embodies a distinctive DNA marked by bold design and uncompromising quality—positioning the brand as “unrivalled in the French watchmaking landscape”.
The brand draws inspiration from a centuries-old fortress—Montignac in Périgord Noir—symbolizing strength, resistance, and timeless endurance. This heritage influences watches “designed to last and be passed down through generations,” with all pieces conceived, assembled, and tested in France, particularly in the Doubs valley near Besançon. Maison Montignac emphasizes transparency, marrying high-grade materials (like 316L stainless steel, bronze, and durable PVD treatments) with carefully selected reliable movements.
Targeted at individuals who seek more than just a timepiece, the brand caters to discerning watch enthusiasts who value design audacity, craftsmanship, and accessibility. Whether you gravitate toward automatic or quartz movements, skeleton or open-heart dials, Montignac offers watches with “disruptive designs and attention to detail” that make a distinct style statement.

Maserati
History
ARTICLES
Maserati Watches is an Italian‑heritage watch & jewellery brand born from the Trident legacy of Maserati cars, produced under Morellato S.p.A. The brand occupies a niche at the intersection of automotive‑inspired design and accessible luxury.
With a design philosophy rooted in retro‑modern fusion, Maserati Watches emphasizes stylistic cues drawn directly from iconic cars—tachymeter bezels, counters evoking dashboard instruments, polished and satin‑finished cases, and dynamic 3D dials. Collections boast automatic MIYOTA movements in sporty Velocità models, classic leather‑strapped editions, and sporty jewellery pieces that combine steel, ceramic, and braided leather.

Debut of the GranTurismo grand tourer, re‑establishing Maserati’s design excellence and performance.
2020
Introduction of the MC20 super‑sports car, ushering in a new era of Maserati performance.
2022

MARATHON WATCH Official

History
ARTICLES
Marathon Watch Company’s roots were laid well over a century ago in 1904 when Hersh Wein founded Weinstrum Watch, later to be renamed Wein Brothers. The second generation of the Wein family was then deployed worldwide to begin their own watch businesses, with Morris Wein, son of Hersh Wein, founding Marathon in Montreal, Canada in 1939. The head office being located in just above a popular cafe frequented by members of the dept of defense. By 1941, Marathon was officially supplying wristwatches and timing instruments to Allied forces.
Today, Marathon is headquartered in Vaughan, Canada with the brand’s wristwatch assembly based in their own factory in heart of Swiss watchmaking country, the historical town of La Chaux-de-Fonds. Marathon has continued its purpose-built legacy for over 80 years, still actively developing and supplying timepieces and other equipment under contract to the Government of Canadian, U.S Government., British Government and other Allied forces.
1904
The company is founded as Weinsturm Watch by the Wein family, later renamed Wein Brothers.
1939
Morris Wein formally establishes Marathon Watch, supplying fine precision timepieces throughout North America.
1941
Marathon begins manufacturing timing instruments for the Allied Forces — a military heritage that defines the brand to this day.
2000s
Fourth generation of the Wein family takes leadership, with Mitchell Wein as President, continuing the tradition of military-grade Swiss-engineered timepieces.

Mathey Tissot
History
ARTICLES
Mathey Tissot is an independent Swiss watchmaker specializing in affordable luxury timepieces. Established in 1886 by Edmond Mathey-Tissot in Les Ponts-de-Martel, Switzerland, the brand initially gained recognition for its precision in crafting repeater pocket watches and chronographs. Over the years, Mathey Tissot has built a reputation for producing Swiss-made watches that combine classic design elements with modern functionality, offering enthusiasts a blend of heritage and contemporary appeal.
The brand's design philosophy emphasizes timeless aesthetics, often drawing inspiration from vintage styles while incorporating modern materials and movements. Key products include chronographs, moonphase models, and military-inspired designs, catering to both men and women. Mathey Tissot's target audience comprises watch enthusiasts and collectors seeking quality Swiss craftsmanship at accessible price points, distinguishing itself from more mainstream luxury brands.
1886

Elvis Presley purchased several dozen customized Mathey-Tissot automatic watches, gifting them to family, friends, and staff.

Alberto Frigerio acquired Mathey-Tissot, bringing renewed focus on quality and personalized service.

History
ARTICLES
MB&F is a Swiss high-end horological concept laboratory that specializes in designing and crafting small-series, avant-garde watches and mechanical art devices. Rather than following traditional watchmaking design norms, the brand’s philosophy is to fuse micro-engineering with artistic vision — producing timepieces that are more akin to three-dimensional kinetic art than conventional wristwatches.
Known for its bold, sculptural cases and technically complex movements, MB&F blends futuristic design elements (inspired by sci-fi, supercars, aviation, and architecture) with traditional Swiss watchmaking craftsmanship. Their creations appeal to collectors and connoisseurs who value originality, artistry, and mechanical innovation — essentially, watch lovers seeking wearable art rather than just time-telling instruments. MB&F’s niche lies in positioning itself not as a conventional watch brand, but as a creative “laboratory” for radical horological ideas, catering to an audience that appreciates exclusivity, engineering excellence and artistic boldness.
2005
Founding of MB&F (Maximilian Büsser & Friends) as the world’s first horological concept laboratory.
2007
Release of the first Horological Machine, HM1 — a sculptured 3-D case and engine that set the standard for future MB&F Machines.
2009
Introduction of HM3, reinforcing MB&F’s commitment to three-dimensional, unconventional watchmaking design.
2011
Launch of the Legacy Machine collection, starting with LM1 — a more classical aesthetic reinterpretation, expanding MB&F’s appeal.
2012
LM1 wins both the Public Prize and Best Men’s Watch Prize at the Grand Prix d’Horlogerie de Genève, gaining major critical acclaim.
2015
Launch of M.A.D.Editions, MB&F’s more accessible line aiming at a wider audience while preserving creative spirit.
2015
Celebration of MB&F’s 10th anniversary; over a decade of hyper-creativity and over 11 calibres formed the base of its Horological and Legacy Machines.
2016
LM Perpetual wins the Grand Prix d’Horlogerie de Genève Best Calendar Watch Prize — recognition for technical excellence and innovation.
2022
MB&F moves into the M.A.D.House — a century-old house transformed into a creative hub — marking a new chapter in its brand journey.
2025
20th Anniversary of MB&F, celebrating two decades since 2005 and more than 20 calibres developed over that period.
FOUNDED
2005
HEADQUARTERS
Carouge, Switzerland

Mbandf.com

Marvin
History
ARTICLES
Marvin is a distinguished Swiss watchmaking brand established in 1850 by brothers Marc and Emmanuel Didisheim in Saint-Imier, Switzerland. Renowned for its rich heritage in fine horology, Marvin has consistently blended traditional craftsmanship with innovative design. The brand's ethos revolves around passion, daringness, strong ethics, and humor, aiming to create timepieces that are both elegant and original. Marvin's watches are powered by top-quality Swiss movements, ensuring reliability and precision. Over the years, the company has expanded its presence in over sixty countries, solidifying its reputation in the global watchmaking industry.
The Marvin design philosophy emphasizes unique aesthetics, daring designs, and meticulous attention to detail. The brand has a history of introducing innovative concepts, such as the "Tyre Watch" in the 1930s, which was housed in a rubber tire and targeted at automotive enthusiasts. Collaborations with renowned designers like Sébastien Perret and Jean-François Ruchonnet have led to the revival of iconic lines, including the Malton collection, which pays homage to the brand's origins. Marvin's commitment to quality is evident in features like the "M&E D" engraving on the left side of each case, symbolizing the founders' legacy.
1850
Founded by Marc and Emmanuel Didisheim in Saint-Imier, Switzerland, establishing a clock movement factory.
1893

1912
Transitioned to a full Manufacture status, enhancing in-house production capabilities.
1930s
Introduced the 'Tyre Watch,' a unique timepiece housed in a rubber tire, targeting automotive enthusiasts.
2009
Awarded the 'Price of the Jury' for the 'New Time, New Codes' advertising campaign by Montres Passion magazine.
2010
Unveiled the Malton 160 at the GTE show, commemorating the brand's 160th anniversary.
2019
Reintroduced the Malton line, collaborating with designers Sébastien Perret and Jean-François Ruchonnet.
FOUNDED
1850

Saint-Imier, Switzerland

Mezei Watch Company
History
ARTICLES
Mezei Watch Company is a modern micro-brand specializing in thoughtfully designed automatic wristwatches that emphasize minimalism, craftsmanship, and personal storytelling. Known for clean lines, refined proportions, and textured dials, the brand seeks to combine subtle, elegant design elements with functional benefits — namely reliable automatic movements and wearability — catering to watch lovers who appreciate understated style with character.
The brand’s philosophy centers on intentional design and personal connection: its founder spent over a decade in the watch industry before launching Mezei, motivated by a desire to create timepieces that feel unique, grounded, and built to last.
The design language emphasizes a “sweet spot” case size (e.g., 40 mm diameter, modest thickness) so the watches remain versatile — suitable under a shirt cuff, yet striking enough for casual wear.

Mondaine Group
MONDAINE GROUP
History
ARTICLES
The Mondaine Group
is a Swiss watch manufacturer known for its focus on iconic, functional timepieces with clean, distinctive designs. Founded by Erwin Bernheim in 1951, the family-owned company has built a reputation for Swiss-made watches that balance quality craftsmanship with accessible pricing. The group manages three distinct watch brands, each with its own unique identity and market position.
Brands summary:
Mondaine
is the flagship brand, and it's globally recognized for its Official Swiss Railways watch based on the iconic clock found in Swiss train stations.
Luminox
is the group's performance sports watch brand, known for its self-powered illumination technology that keeps the watch faces visible in any lighting conditions for up to 25 years and historic connections to the US Navy SEALs. On the less rugged end of the spectrum, Mondaine owns
Pierre Cardin
, a brand named for one of the most influential and prolific fashion designers of the 20th century. Inspired by Pierre Cardin's most iconic designs as well as by the energy and vibrancy of his Parisian home, the watches marry current fashion trends and traditional aesthetics for an aspirational audience. The group also operates
M-Watch
, a specialist in Swiss-made timepieces at entry-level price points.

1951

Mühle Glashütte
History
ARTICLES
Mühle Glashütte is a German precision watchmaking company specializing in marine chronometers, nautical instruments, and robust tool watches. Known for its maritime heritage and practical elegance, the brand combines traditional Glashütte craftsmanship with modern technical innovations, catering to sailors, professionals, and watch enthusiasts who value functionality and understated design. The brand's design philosophy focuses on readability, durability, and precision, featuring signature elements like wood-grain finished dials, ship's wheel logos, and robust cases. Key product lines include the Marine collection (inspired by nautical instruments), Terrasport outdoor watches, and Robert Mühle classic dress watches. Unique selling points include in-house modified movements, anti-magnetic protection, and water resistance up to 1,000 meters in professional models.
1869
Founded by Robert Mühle as a precision workshop producing measurement tools for Glashütte's watchmaking industry.
1920
Began manufacturing speedometers and dashboard instruments for automobiles and aircraft, expanding beyond watchmaking.
1945
Factory destroyed during WWII; company reestablished by Hans Mühle focusing on industrial measuring instruments.
1994
Fourth-generation family member Thilo Mühle relaunched the watch division, producing the first modern Mühle wristwatches.
1996

Movado Group Inc.
MOVADO GROUP INC.
History
ARTICLES
The Movado Group Inc.
is a renowned American watch company that designs, manufactures, and distributes watches globally. Founded in 1983 through the acquisition of the historic
Movado
brand, the group has expanded through strategic acquisitions to become one of the world's leading watch companies. The Movado Group Inc. targets different market segments through its diverse portfolio of owned and licensed brands, balancing heritage timepieces with fashion-forward designs.
Brands summary:
The Movado group Inc.'s namesake brand is best known for a minimalist aesthetic exemplified by the iconic Museum watch with its solitary dot at 12 o'clock, which is included in the permanent collection of NYC's Museum of Modern Art. The acquisition of
MVMT
in 2018 brought a catalog of trendy, affordable watches targeting younger consumers into the fold, along with an audience of young social media-savvy customers. Similarly,
Olivia Burton
was founded in 2012 and acquired by the Movado Group Inc. in 2017 and is known for offering style-conscious affordable luxury.
Concord
, the most prestigious member of the group, brings both history and technical achievement to the table, while
Ebel
is recognized for its distinctive wave bracelet design and the Sport Classic collection. The Movado Group Inc. also produces watches under various licensed fashion brands including
Coach
,
Tommy Hilfiger
,
Hugo Boss
, and
Lacoste
. These collections translate each fashion brand's distinctive design aesthetic into timepieces that complement their clothing and accessories, offering fashion-conscious consumers branded watches at mid-market price points.

New Jersey, USA

Nivada Grenchen is a Swiss watch brand with a legacy of innovation and adventure. Founded in 1926, it became renowned for rugged, precision timepieces like the Antarctic, worn during Operation Deep Freeze I, and the Chronomaster, a favorite among sports enthusiasts. Discontinued in the 1980s due to the quartz crisis, the brand was revived in 2018, bringing back its most iconic models with modern craftsmanship while staying true to its heritage. Today, Nivada Grenchen continues to blend vintage aesthetics with contemporary reliability, offering timeless watches for collectors and explorers alike.

Ocean Crawler
History
ARTICLES
Ocean Crawler is an American micro-watchmaker specializing in self-winding and automatic mechanical dive watches and adventure-oriented timepieces. Their design philosophy is a fusion of “nostalgic sophistication” (classic dive / exploration heritage) with modern durability and high performance. They strive to make watches that aren’t just display pieces but built to be worn in real situations—on land or sea. Key qualities they emphasize include water resistance (often very deep), shock resistance (including extreme tests), precision, reliability, and limited edition / rarity.
Some of their key products / models include the Champion Diver, the Core Diver, the Ocean Navigator, and special/limited editions (e.g. “Fordite” dials, full lume variants). These watches often come with dual straps, high-legibility features (e.g. luminous markers), tough materials (titanium, hardened steel), and special finishing.
2017
Ocean Crawler is founded by Christian Champion; initial model launched was the Champion Diver, which was recognized among the 10 best of 2017 by WatchUSeek.

Northern Star Watch

History
ARTICLES
Northern Star Watch is an independent American microbrand specializing in bold, celestial-inspired automatic wristwatches that blend striking visual design with meaningful storytelling. Launched in 2021 by founders Shaheen and Noraida Miller, the brand emphasizes watches as symbols of purpose, strength, and guidance — drawing inspiration from the night sky and medieval motifs such as knights, shields, and celestial bodies.
Known for its standout Sentinel Collection, Northern Star Watch combines unique geometry and artistic dial work (often featuring moissanite gems to evoke stars) with robust technical features like a Miyota 90S5 automatic movement, dual-coated anti-reflective sapphire crystal, and 316L surgical-grade stainless steel construction to deliver both style and performance. The design language takes cues from both cosmic themes — rich celestial colorways and layered dials — and medieval symbolism reflected in elements like shield-inspired crowns and angular cases.
The target audience is watch enthusiasts and lifestyle buyers who seek timepieces that make a statement, combining visual impact with craftsmanship and a meaningful narrative. With attention to comfort, everyday wearability, and a mission-driven ethos (including options to support charitable causes), the brand appeals to customers looking for purpose-driven design and individuality rather than mass-market trends.
2021
Northern Star Watch was founded by Shaheen and Noraida Miller, inspired by a vivid dream and a desire to create timepieces that blend celestial beauty, purpose-driven design, and craftsmanship. The brand was officially launched as Northern Star Watch LLC in Florida.
2023
Introduction of the Sentinel Collection featuring a unique hexagonal case, triple-tiered dials, moissanite gem accents, and premium Miyota 90S5 automatic movement, establishing the brand’s distinctive visual identity and technical foundation.
2024
Expansion of the Sentinel Collection with multiple dial variations (e.g., White, Black, Gunmetal, Icy) and limited editions with gold-rotor options, increasing model diversity and collectible appeal.
2025
Strengthened charitable mission and community engagement under the motto “Fashion Meets Purpose, Be The Light,” involving customer options to allocate a portion of purchases toward charitable causes and ongoing narrative development.
FOUNDED
2021

Florida, United States

OVD
History
ARTICLES
OVD — short for Origin, Vision & Design — is a niche watch brand rooted in minimalist principles, aiming to return to the foundational elements of design while maintaining creative innovation. The brand’s design philosophy centers around crafting aesthetically compelling, functional timepieces that spark curiosity and stand out—so much that wearers often hear, “Hey, what kind of watch are you wearing?”
OVD's collections demonstrate a compelling fusion of retro-modern themes, innovative storytelling, and superior craftsmanship. From space-inspired models like “NASA Full Moon” and “Saturnian” to heritage-rich designs such as the Ronin (inspired by samurai sword-making) and The Moon Walker (incorporating Super-LumiNova for radiant glow), the brand leans into creative narratives and functional excellence.

Omega is a Swiss luxury watch brand that has been synonymous with quality, accuracy and innovation for over a century. Founded in 1848, Omega has since become one of the world’s most respected and celebrated watchmakers, thanks in part to its rich heritage of technical excellence and its commitment to pushing the boundaries of watchmaking. Known for its iconic designs and legendary timepieces such as the Speedmaster and the Seamaster, Omega has earned a reputation as a watchmaker that combines classical style with cutting-edge technology to create timeless and enduring timepieces.

History
ARTICLES
Panerai is an Italian luxury watch brand that was founded in Florence, Italy in 1860. The brand is known for its large, bold watches with distinctive designs, and it has a strong association with the Italian navy. Panerai watches were originally made for the Italian Navy in the 1930s and 1940s, and the brand has always been associated with the maritime world, which is reflected in its design. The brand is also known for its use of high-quality materials, such as titanium and carbon, and its use of in-house movements. Panerai watches are highly sought after by collectors and enthusiasts for their unique design and craftsmanship. The brand is now part of the Richemont group.

History
ARTICLES
Patek Philippe & Co. is a Swiss watch brand that has been producing exceptional timepieces since its inception in 1839. This luxury brand is known for its precision, elegance, and durability, and has been a favorite of wealthy and sophisticated collectors for generations. Patek Philippe watches are not only renowned for their mechanical precision but also for their intricate designs that reflect years of craftsmanship and attention to detail. The company remains one of the last independent Geneva watch manufacturers and is widely recognized as one of the most prestigious watchmakers in the world.

History
ARTICLES
Paul Hewitt is a nautical‑inspired accessories brand born on Germany’s North Sea coast, specializing in watches and jewelry that merge maritime motifs with minimalist German design. Known for its anchor‑themed aesthetic and casual elegance, the brand blends clean lines, organic shapes, and talismanic ocean symbols with accessible pricing and quality craftsmanship.
The design philosophy emphasizes sustainability and responsible sourcing alongside style: many pieces use recycled materials such as Marinium® steel (derived from ship steel), rPET plastics, and solar‑powered watch movements, all framed in maritime themes such as waves, turtles, and anchors. Their watches often feature sapphire crystal, solar calibers, and clean Scandinavian‑Nordic minimalism, appealing to style‑conscious, eco-aware consumers who gravitate toward affordable luxury and ocean‑positive values.
2009
Paul Hewitt founded on the northern German (North Sea) coast by Philipp Gloth and team, launching nautical‑inspired watches and jewelry.
2021
Begins measuring CO₂ emissions company‑wide and starts offsetting all unavoidable emissions via certified climate projects.
2022
Introduces fully recycled Marinium® stainless steel in product lines and transitions to rPET bracelets; begins solar‑powered watch offerings.
2022
Launch of the Turtle Collection, with a portion of profits donated to ocean conservation NGOs like Turtle Foundation and Reefscapers.
FOUNDED
2009

Lower Saxony, Germany

Orlam
History
ARTICLES
Orlam is a Swiss heritage watchmaker specializing in classic, mechanical timepieces with a vintage-modern aesthetic. Founded in 1931 in Bienne (Biel), Switzerland, the brand prides itself on “craftsmanship first,” creating honest, Swiss‑made watches with authentic heritage and timeless design. Its design philosophy blends retro-inspired elements (like open-heart and skeleton dials) with modern simplicity, making high-quality Swiss movements accessible and meaningful to today’s consumers. Emphasizing sustainable innovation and durability, Orlam positions its watches as lifelong companions that honor every moment – combining clarity of design with eco‑conscious materials and manufacturing.
Orlam’s product lineup (though details are limited) appears to include minimalist three-hand models, skeletonized automatics, and possibly sport-styled divers, all reflecting the brand’s clean, timeless look. The key selling points are its authentic Swiss craftsmanship, accessible pricing (making “Swiss-made” attainable), and the fusion of classic style with modern sustainability. In short, Orlam caters to a discerning, younger audience that values origin, clarity and meaning in their accessories. The brand targets watch enthusiasts who appreciate heritage aesthetics and ethical production – effectively blending retro charm, reliable build quality, and conscious design into each timepiece.
1931
Orlam brand is established in Bienne (Biel), Switzerland by Marc Favre & Co.
2024
Orlam is relaunched as a modern Swiss watch brand emphasizing its heritage, craftsmanship and sustainable innovation.
FOUNDED
1931

Bienne (Biel), Switzerland

Piaget is a luxury watch and jewelry brand based in Switzerland. The company was founded in 1874 by Georges Piaget in the village of La Côte-aux-Fées. Piaget is known for its high-quality watches and jewelry, as well as for its use of advanced technology and craftsmanship. The brand is particularly well-known for its ultra-thin watches, such as the Altiplano and the Emperador, and for its use of precious stones and materials in its jewelry collections. Piaget is also known for its commitment to traditional watchmaking techniques and for its use of in-house movements. The brand’s watches are often considered status symbols, known for their elegance, refinement and exclusivity.

Pierre Lannier
History
ARTICLES
Pierre Lannier is a stylish mid-range watchmaker specializing in contemporary French timepieces for men and women. Known for blending “chic à la Française” elegance with accessible pricing, the brand offers quartz, automatic, and solar-powered watches crafted with meticulous attention to detail. Their designs often merge retro-modern aesthetics, with functional benefits such as 3 ATM water resistance and 2‑year warranties. Led by the Burgun family since its founding in 1977 in Alsace, Pierre Lannier positions itself as a faithful guardian of French watchmaking heritage, combining in-house design and assembly with an eye on emerging fashion trends. It appeals to style-conscious individuals, who seek craftsmanship, French-made prestige, and original yet wearable designs at a reasonable price.

Founded by Béatrice and Jean‑Paul Burgun in their Alsace home; first collection launched in Strasbourg.

2022

2023

History
ARTICLES
Born in Bern, Switzerland, in 1959, Pierre Kunz has been a prominent figure in the watchmaking industry for over two decades. He founded the prestigious Swiss watch brand, Pierre Kunz, which is now part of the Franck Muller Group. Known for its innovative designs and exceptional craftsmanship, the brand specializes in intricate complications such as retrograde displays and tourbillons. Kunz was given the unique opportunity by Franck Muller to design his own models at the renowned Watchland Park in Genthod, a hub celebrated for its creativity. Overlooking the shores of Lake Geneva with Mont Blanc in view, Watchland is home to master craftsmen and skilled artisans dedicated to preserving Switzerland’s rich watchmaking heritage. With bold and distinctive designs, each Pierre Kunz timepiece reflects a blend of traditional horology and modern aesthetics, ensuring its place at the forefront of luxury watchmaking.

History
ARTICLES
Redwood Watches is a Canadian watch brand that specializes in rugged, heritage-inspired timepieces. Founded in 2014 by a family of watch enthusiasts, the company is committed to delivering high-quality, military-style watches at accessible prices. Their designs draw heavily from vintage military and outdoor watches, with a focus on durability and functionality. Each watch is assembled in their workshop on the north shore of the St. Lawrence River near Montreal, Canada, ensuring meticulous craftsmanship and quality control.
Redwood's design philosophy centers on combining classic aesthetics with modern technology. Their collections include solar-powered watches like the Kilroy and Monsoon, as well as automatic models such as the Tactical v3 and Pilot A-13. These timepieces feature robust materials, scratch-resistant sapphire crystals, and luminous dials for enhanced readability in low-light conditions. The brand's commitment to fair pricing allows customers to own a well-crafted watch without the premium markup associated with many luxury brands.
2014

Roamer
History
ARTICLES
Roamer of Switzerland is a heritage‑driven Swiss watchmaker specializing in elegantly designed, Swiss‑made wristwatches that emphasize quality, craftsmanship, and affordability. Known for integrating traditional watchmaking standards with timeless aesthetic cues, the brand delivers collections ranging from classic dress watches to sporty chronographs and diver models.
Rooted in a retro‑modern fusion, Roamer often blends tonneau‑shaped cases or open‑worked skeleton dials (“Competence Skeleton”) with modern materials like scratch‑resistant sapphire glass and in‑house updated movements such as the MST 2.0 and Mechano. Its pieces such as the R‑Line Classic showcase minimalist vintage charm with contemporary flair.
1888
Founded in Solothurn by Fritz Meyer; initially produced cylinder escapements before launching full watches and calibre No. 38
1905
Partnership Meyer & Stüdeli (MST) formed; development of in‑house movements and the MST brand
1955
Patent and launch of the Anfibio watertight case, giving rise to durable diver models
1972
Introduction of Roamer’s first quartz movement (microquartz)
2003
Christian Frommherz becomes CEO; steers brand back toward mechanical watches and affordable luxury
2013
Celebrated 125th anniversary with limited editions such as Stingray Chrono Diver
2024
Launch of the Mechano, the first watch powered by the updated MST 2.0 in‑house calibre
2025
Introduction of the Helios Power: the first Swiss‑made solar‑powered automatic chronograph from Roamer
FOUNDED
1888

Canton of Aargau, Switzerland

History
ARTICLES
Samsung is a global electronics leader specializing in wearable devices—particularly the Samsung Galaxy Watch series, sold in Canada through its official channel. The brand emphasizes a sleek fusion of modern technology and classic watch aesthetics, combining Samsung’s hallmark circular bezel designs with cutting‑edge smart features like AI‑powered wellness tracking and smart home integration.
As a pioneer in health‑focused wearables, Samsung offers devices that blend performance with style. Its latest models—such as the Galaxy Watch8 Classic and Ultra—feature high‑performance 3 nm processors, rotating bezels, and seamless integration with Samsung Health and SmartThings for fitness, sleep coaching, and smart‑device control. Samsung targets tech‑savvy consumers who want the functionality of a smartwatch with the look and feel of a timeless timepiece.

History
ARTICLES
Selten is a contemporary watch company that is open
                    to different ideas, creative thinking, unique materials, and an overall fresh approach to making
                    timepieces.
Leonardo Tsai from Hong Kong started Selten in 2017. Coming from a watch dial making family, he has a huge crash in watches ever since he was young. The first launch of Selten’s meteorite series 00& 01 are truly stunning. Leonardo spent 4 years in R&D to perfect them. Later in 2023, the second model (Salvage bronze) diver was born with a hand patina’d dial, each one is one of the kind.
The name “SELTEN” is taken from the German word for “rare”—due to the utmost respect for German watchmaking. According to Leonardo, “The quality, craftsmanship, and beauty of German watches, as well as the resilience of the German watchmaking industry, serve strongly as inspiration to me.”

History
ARTICLES
Seagull 1963 is a niche, heritage-inspired watch brand specializing in reissues of the iconic Project D304 Chinese Air Force chronograph from 1963. Known for its retro-modern fusion, the brand combines mid‑century military aesthetics, vintage dials, blue hands, red‑star motifs, with robust hand‑wound mechanical ST19 (also called ST1901) movements derived from Swiss designs. This marriage of historical design and dependable functionality appeals to watch enthusiasts who appreciate authenticity without the luxury markup.
1955
Tianjin Watch Factory founded in Tianjin, China; builds first Chinese wristwatch in 100 days (Project WuXing/Dong Feng)
1961
Tasked with designing the official Chinese Air Force aviation chronograph (Project D304)
1963
Completion of over 30 prototypes of Project D304; now popularly called the '1963' among collectors
1974
Factory adopts the English brand name ‘Seagull’ as export operations begin
1990
Recognition as a national‑level enterprise by the Chinese government; expansion into complex movements
1992
Establishment of Tianjin Seagull Corporation to consolidate watchmaking efforts
2009
Collector-driven revival led by 'Thomas' in Hong Kong; reissue of the D304 chronograph under Red Star/Seagull using ST19 movements begins, reviving global interest in the Seagull 1963.
2023
Launch of the Bronze 60th Anniversary Limited Edition (750 pieces), featuring ST1901 movement, sapphire crystal, and vintage military design elements.
2025
Release of the ICON Moonphase Bronze Limited Edition (500 pieces), introducing a moonphase complication and green dial with patina-ready bronze case.
FOUNDED
1955

Antwerp, Belgium

Seiko is a Japanese watch brand that was founded in 1881. It is one of the oldest and most respected watch manufacturers in the world. Seiko is renowned for its innovation and advanced technology in the watch industry, including its development of quartz watches and kinetic watches. The brand offers a wide range of watches, from sporty and casual to elegant and sophisticated, catering to every watch enthusiast’s needs and styles. Seiko’s commitment to high-quality craftsmanship, precision, and durability has made it a popular choice among watch enthusiasts worldwide.

2016

SINN SPEZIALUHREN Official
Loading videos...

History
ARTICLES
Sinn Spezialuhren is a German precision engineering watchmaker specializing in high-performance tool watches for professional and extreme environments. Known for its innovative technologies and no-nonsense design, the brand combines scientific approach to watchmaking with rugged functionality, catering to pilots, divers, engineers, and military personnel. The brand's design philosophy emphasizes technical perfection and durability, featuring proprietary technologies like Tegimented hardened steel, Ar-Dehumidifying technology, and magnetic field protection. Key product lines include the EZM (Mission Timer) series for emergency responders, U-series diving watches, and chronographs tested in space. Unique selling points include certified tool watch functionality (DIN, ISO standards), visible engineering (skeletonized models), and extreme environment testing (from Arctic expeditions to space missions).
1961
Founded by flight instructor and pilot Helmut Sinn in Frankfurt, initially producing pilot's chronographs and navigation clocks.
1968
Launched the iconic Sinn 103 chronograph, establishing the brand's reputation for professional aviation timepieces.
1994
Lothar Schmidt (former IWC engineer) acquires the company, ushering in a new era of technical innovations.
1997
Introduced the U1 diving watch with submarine steel case, beginning the U-series of professional dive watches.
2001
Developed the patented Tegiment technology for ultra-hardened watch cases and bracelets.
2005
Launched the EZM 10 mission timer for German special forces (GSG9), featuring unique countdown function.
2011
Presented the Frankfurt Financial District watch with oil-filled case for superior readability, winning multiple design awards.
2015
Introduced the 903 St BE UTC, tested by ESA astronaut Reinhard Furrer during space missions.
2018
Released the 105 UTC with temperature-resistant movement (-45°C to +80°C) for extreme environments.
2021
Celebrated 60th anniversary with limited edition 103 Ti ARKTIS, tested in Arctic conditions.
2023
Launched the 140 S with fully skeletonized movement, showcasing technical craftsmanship.
FOUNDED
1961
HEADQUARTERS
Frankfurt am Main, Germany

History
ARTICLES
SOVRYGN is a boutique / microbrand watchmaker founded by Nigerian-Canadian designer-entrepreneur Rotimi, blending cultural storytelling with modern horological design. Their niche is in producing timepieces that go beyond mere function: they aim to imbue each watch with narrative, purpose, and aesthetic identity. Their flagship line, The Calendar, features multiple complications (day, date, month, 24-hour, power reserve) in a distinct case form that sets it apart from typical microbrand offerings. Design-wise, SOVRYGN leans toward a fusion of bold, modern geometry and layered dial architecture. They emphasize premium materials: anti-reflective sapphire crystal, exhibition case backs, BGW9 lume, and steel bracelets with micro-adjust clasps.
Their design philosophy reflects a balance of emotion and engineering — each element is intended to carry both symbolic and functional weight. Their target audience is likely watch enthusiasts who appreciate microbrands, independent storytelling, and mechanical complexity — people who don’t just see watches as tools, but as wearable expressions of identity.

Their collections often feature bold colors, striking dials, and reliable automatic or quartz movements, catering to watch enthusiasts who appreciate both form and function. With a focus on affordability without compromising on craftsmanship, Straton Watch Co. delivers eye-catching watches for everyday wear, motorsport-inspired models, and dive-ready designs—all built to make a statement.

Swarovski
SWAROVSKI Official
History
ARTICLES
The name Swarovski has long been associated with luxury, accuracy, and dazzling craftsmanship. The company is known for making beautiful crystal items like jewelry, home art, and accessories. The brand was started by Daniel Swarovski in Wattens, Austria, in 1895. They are experts at cutting crystal to make beautiful pieces that catch and reflect light in beautiful ways. Because Swarovski is dedicated to new ideas, high quality, and good design, it has become a major player in the world of high-end and glamorous items.
As an extension of its traditional crystal products, Swarovski started making watches that mix the brand's famous crystal craftsmanship with beautiful watch design. People who want to add a bit of class and luxury to their everyday life wear Swarovski watches. Every watch is a mix of new technology and old-fashioned artistry. They use precise Swiss movements inside designs that are often decorated with their signature crystals. These watches are more than just timepieces; they're fashion statements that look great at any event that calls for a touch of class. Since it started making watches, Swarovski has become even better known as a name that shows the beauty of crystal in all areas of life.
1892
Daniel Swarovski, the founder, invents an automatic cutting machine that facilitated the production of crystal glass, laying the groundwork for the company's future endeavors in crystal production, including watches​
1895
The Swarovski company is founded by Daniel Swarovski, along with financier Armand Kosmann and Franz Weis. They established a crystal cutting factory in Wattens, Austria, utilizing local hydroelectricity for the energy-intensive grinding processes. This marks the start of Swarovski's journey, which would eventually include the production of watches​
1899
Swarovski first used the edelweiss flower in its logo, a significant branding element that would evolve over time
1935
The introduction of customized optical instruments by Swarovski's son, Wilhelm, eventually led to the launch of Swarovski Optik and the company's diversification into various products, including eventually watches​
1977
Swarovski enters the United States' jewelry market, marking a significant expansion that includes its later ventures into watches
1988
Swarovski changes its logo from the edelweiss flower to the current swan logo, an important branding milestone that reflects on all its products, including watches
2020
A major company shake-up occurs as Swarovski appoints Robert Buchbauer, a direct descendant of the founder, as its new CEO. This period marks significant changes within the company, affecting all divisions including watches​
2021
Under the creative direction of Giovanna Battaglia Engelbert, Swarovski releases a new retail collection that includes watches, among other products. This collection draws on archival designs, including those created by founder Daniel Swarovski​
FOUNDED
1895
HEADQUARTERS
Wattens, Austria
WEBSITE
Swarovski.com

Stil Timepieces
History
ARTICLES
Stil Timepieces is a contemporary Swiss-inspired watch brand specializing in performance-oriented mechanical timepieces that draw heavy design inspiration from motorsports and high-performance engineering. The brand’s ethos is rooted in the precision, speed, and exhilaration of auto racing, with watches that seek to reflect the dynamic spirit of the track — combining Swiss movements, robust materials, and sporty styling to deliver both performance and visual impact.
Stil Timepieces blends classic horological craftsmanship (such as Swiss automatic movements and sapphire crystal) with innovative materials like titanium and advanced carbon fiber. Its product lineup includes models like the Megara Limited Edition — forged in lightweight titanium with a rotating ceramic bezel and rated to 300 m water resistance — and the S46LM, inspired by iconic performance machines of the early 2000s that emphasize precision and control. These watches combine technical functionality with bold aesthetics tailored to motorsport enthusiasts.
The brand’s target audience consists of sports and performance watch enthusiasts who appreciate Swiss movement reliability and design influenced by automotive culture. Stil’s unique selling points include motorsport-inspired styling, Swiss watchmaking standards, and the use of premium materials such as titanium and carbon fiber to balance durability with comfort and speed-driven aesthetics.

History
ARTICLES
Swatch is a widely known Swiss watch brand that was established in 1983 by the Swiss entrepreneur, Nicolas Hayek. It is popular for its trendy and affordable watches which come in a variety of colors, designs, and styles. The brand aims to bring together the traditional Swiss watchmaking craftsmanship with a modern and contemporary touch. Swatch has become a household name in the watch industry and has built a reputation for creating durable, functional, and fashionable timepieces. With a diverse collection ranging from sports watches to classic dress watches, Swatch caters to the needs of every watch enthusiast.

History
ARTICLES
Swiss Military Hanowa is a sporty Swiss‑Made watch brand rooted in precision and robust design. It was founded in 1963 in Bienne (Biel), Switzerland, by Hans Noll and later launched as the Swiss Military line in 1990. Known for combining retro military aesthetics with modern engineering, the brand emphasizes durability, luminous readability, and functional elegance.
The design philosophy centers on offering high-performance timepieces—quartz, chronograph, and diver watches—that perform under extreme conditions, reflecting values associated with the Swiss armed forces like endurance and robustness. With modern materials, a contemporary color palette, sapphire glass, strong water resistance, and competitive pricing, Swiss Military Hanowa targets active, quality-conscious individuals seeking utilitarian style without breaking the bank.

History
ARTICLES
TAG Heuer is a luxury watch brand based in Switzerland. The company was founded in 1860 by Edouard Heuer. The brand is known for its sporty, stylish watches, and its use of advanced technology, such as chronographs and quartz movements. TAG Heuer is particularly well-known for its Carrera and Monaco collections, which are inspired by the world of motor racing, and its Link collection, which is characterized by its iconic bracelet design. The brand is also known for its collaborations with other luxury brands and personalities, such as McLaren and Leonardo DiCaprio and for its commitment to precision and innovation. TAG Heuer watches are highly sought-after by collectors and enthusiasts for their sporty, stylish design and craftsmanship.

Thomas Sabo
THOMAS SABO Official
History
ARTICLES
THE HISTORY OF THOMAS SABO COMPANY  is one of the leading global brands designing, selling and distributing jewellery, watches and beauty products for women and men. Our driving forces are a love of fashion and a fascination for creating innovative, highly-expressive accessories.
Extraordinary attention to detail is paid in the creation of trend-oriented designs which range from classic and elegant, to flamboyant and edgy. This, together with the highest standards of product quality, are the key characteristics of the world of THOMAS SABO. Expressive and iconic designs from the ladies' and men's collections, such as the love knot, skulls, leather bracelets and the feminine infinity rings, have acquired cult status in the THOMAS SABO product world and are highly-coveted among customers across the world.
Our mission is to create a product that delights and fascinates people. Our aims are: to constantly inspire our customers with innovative and trend-setting creations to spread our unique love for style to define the industry standard with materials of the highest quality
1984
THOMAS SABO is founded by Thomas Sabo.
1990
By the end of the 1980s, THOMAS SABO has developed into one of the most successful brands for innovative silver jewellery in the German-speaking world. In the early 1990s the company repositions itself and now appears with an extended design team and a mature brand concept. THOMAS SABO is now renowned for its Sterling Silver Collection.
1992
Susanne Kölbli becomes Creative Director at THOMAS SABO. Ever since, the qualified graphic designer has been decisively shaping the aesthetic of the brand with her unique style, wealth of ideas and her sensitivity for trends.
1998
The first exclusive THOMAS SABO stand-alone-shop is opened in Frankfurt. In the years that follow many stand-alone-shops in prime locations, as well as shop-in-shops in leading department stores, are established.
2006
The introduction of the THOMAS SABO Charm Club jewellery collection is an overwhelming success. The passion among customers for collecting the ultra-detailed Charms remains unbroken to this very day.
2007
The Rebel at heart product range is launched: aimed at the male target group, it features expressive items of jewellery with an edgy, rock & roll vibe. The collection is characterised by designs crafted from blackened 925 Sterling silver, matched with masculine materials such as leather and black synthetic zirconia.
2009
The brand expands its portfolio with the launch of the Watches Collection, which perfectly reflects the DNA of THOMAS SABO and the existing jewellery collections.
2010
THOMAS SABO extends its product range with the launch of Charm Rose Eau de Parfum, the first perfume for the brand and steps into the beauty market.
2012
For the very first time, THOMAS SABO employs A-list personalities such as Katy Perry, Poppy Delevingne and David Garrett for its international advertising campaigns. The ambassadors represent the two product ranges in the Sterling Silver Collection: Glam & Soul (women's) and Rebel at heart (men's).
2013
THOMAS SABO breaks new ground in design and launches items of jewellery with 18kt yellow and rose gold plating for the very first time.
2014
The Karma Beads jewellery line, which consists of Filigree Beads and the Wheel of Karma as the centrepiece, is launched. Thanks to the diversity and combination possibilities of the new jewellery line, each jewellery creation radiates individual karma style. Top model Georgia May Jagger and Formula 1 driver Nico Rosberg represent the brand as the new campaign faces.
2015
THOMAS SABO Love Bridge is launched: the wide selection of bracelets and necklaces that can be personalised is the latest addition, presented by the testimonials Georgia May Jagger and Marlon Teixeira. From now on, with the motto “Make memories last”, special moments can be eternalised on THOMAS SABO jewellery, thanks to complimentary engraving.
2016
In March 2016 around 490 employees move into the new, international and highly modern company head office in Silberstraße 1 in Lauf an der Pegnitz, Germany. This marks a major milestone for the further long-term growth of the company.
FOUNDED
1984
HEADQUARTERS
Lauf an der Pegnitz, Germany

Thacker Merali Watch Company

History
ARTICLES
Thacker Merali is a contemporary boutique watch brand specializing in Swiss automatic mechanical wristwatches designed with intentional craftsmanship and compelling design inspiration. Founded by friends Bob Thacker and Raheem Merali after completing their MBAs, the company emerged from a desire to create a timepiece that carried meaning and personal connection — inspired by an heirloom watch and a shared passion for horology. Their flagship model, The Original, reflects this ethos by combining rugged elegance with reliable Swiss components, catering to enthusiasts who value both design and substance.
The brand’s design philosophy centers on premium mechanical performance and versatile aesthetics. Thacker Merali watches feature Swiss Sellita SW220 automatic movements with a day/date complication, robust 316L stainless steel cases, double-domed sapphire crystals with anti-reflective coating, and Swiss Super-LumiNova luminescence — balancing classic mechanical watch craft with everyday functionality. The Original is at the core of the lineup, supported by a variety of quick-release strap options that allow owners to tailor the watch’s look and performance to their lifestyle.
Thacker Merali’s target audience is collectors and style-conscious buyers who want an entry-level luxury mechanical watch with meaningful design and high-quality Swiss internals. Its unique selling points include a high-value Swiss movement at a competitive price, a bold and versatile aesthetic suited for sport and dress wear, and a brand narrative built on friendship, heritage, and intentional design.

Thacker Merali Watch Company was founded by friends Bob Thacker and Raheem Merali, inspired by a desire to create a meaningful, high-quality mechanical timepiece after completing their MBAs. (Official brand story)

2024

Timeless
History
ARTICLES
Timeless Swiss Watch is a contemporary Swiss watch brand specializing in distinctive neo-vintage mechanical timepieces. Founded in Switzerland with a design ethos that blends classical design codes with avant-garde touches, the brand creates watches that are recognizably modern yet rooted in traditional aesthetics. The company believes watch design should be “distinctive and recognizable at first glance,” combining architectural elements, textured dials, and thoughtful finishes with functional benefits such as Swiss automatic movements and quality materials like sapphire crystal and calf leather straps.
The brand’s philosophy is guided by Maël Oberkampf, a Swiss industrial designer and architect with experience designing for high-end brands. Timeless emphasizes meticulous dial construction, curved sapphire crystals, multi-part crowns, engraved serialized cases, and balanced proportions to offer a product that feels both timeless and innovative. The watches are assembled in the Swiss Jura using robust Swiss STP1-11 movements, underscoring the brand’s commitment to Swiss watchmaking heritage and quality finishes.
2018
Timeless Swiss Watch Sàrl was founded in Switzerland by designer Maël Oberkampf, with the goal of creating neo-vintage Swiss Made mechanical watches combining classical design with contemporary influences. (Swiss industry launch press release, November 2020)
2021
First watches (HMS 001 and HMS 002) were launched, featuring Swiss STP1-11 automatic movements, distinctive multi-part crowns, and architectural dial design.

History
ARTICLES
Tissot stands as a titan in the realm of watchmaking, boasting a heritage that stretches back to 1853 when it was founded in Le Locle, Switzerland, by Charles-Félicien Tissot and his son, Charles-Émile Tissot. Renowned for its pioneering spirit and innovation, Tissot has been at the forefront of many breakthroughs in the watch industry, including the creation of the first mass-produced pocket watch and the first watch with two time zones in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. As part of the Swatch Group since 1983, Tissot has continued to expand its reputation for quality craftsmanship at accessible prices, embracing modern technologies and materials. Its landmark introductions include the T-Touch, the first tactile watch, underscoring Tissot's commitment to innovation. Today, Tissot's legacy is evident in its diverse range of timepieces, from elegant traditional watches to high-tech sports models, making it a brand celebrated worldwide for its precision, reliability, and distinction.
1853
Tissot was founded in Le Locle, Switzerland by Charles-Félicien Tissot and his son Charles-Émile Tissot.
1910
Tissot gains reputation for pocket and pendant watches, and begins producing women's wristwatches with gold and diamonds.
1929
Tissot merges with Omega to form SSIH, a major milestone in Swiss watchmaking.
1930
Tissot introduces the first anti-magnetic watch.
1953
Tissot launches the Navigator, the first watch with 24 time zones, for its 100th anniversary.
1971
Tissot releases the Astrolon (IDEA 2001), the world’s first plastic mechanical watch.
1980
Tissot becomes Official Timekeeper of the Tour de France from 1988 to 1992.
1983
SSIH merges with ASUAG to form the Swatch Group, including Tissot.
1990
Tissot introduces the T-Touch, with a touch-sensitive crystal for controlling functions.
2000
Tissot innovates with solar-powered and high-tech material watches, and expands its role in sports timekeeping.
2008
Tissot begins partnership with FIBA as official timekeeper, later extended to 2027.
2015
Tissot partners with the NBA, its first major North American sports league collaboration.
2025
Tissot expands its global partnership with the NBA, WNBA, and NBA G League.
FOUNDED
1853

Le Locle, Switzerland

History
ARTICLES
Titoni is an independent, Swiss-made watchmaker in the mid‑price segment, offering mechanical wristwatches that combine timeless retro‑modern design with affordability and craftsmanship. Known for its proud family‑owned heritage since 1919, the brand seamlessly blends tradition with modern technology: classic aesthetics, precision engineering and attention to detail are woven into each timepiece. Its design philosophy emphasizes elegant yet robust styles—think understated dials, clean lines, and vintage-inspired elements like domed crystals and retro fonts—appealing to enthusiasts who value subtlety over flash.

1981
Third generation Daniel Schluep takes over leadership, consolidating global expansion.
2006

2019
100th anniversary; debut of the Line 1919 and introduction of Titoni’s first in‑house T10 movement.
2020

History
ARTICLES
Trauffer Watches is a Swiss‑heritage watch brand born in 2021, merging traditional Swiss folklore motifs with contemporary watch design. The company positions itself as an authentic Swiss folk‑inspired entry in watchmaking, serving a niche looking for approachable, stylish timepieces rooted in national visual identity.
The brand is built on a retro‑modern fusion aesthetic: watches showcase playful yet tasteful references to Swiss symbols—Edelweiss blossoms, cows (“Chüehli”), and Alpine charm—fixed into dials, straps, and metal embellishments.Targeted at young, culture‑rooted Swiss consumers and collectors abroad, the watches appeal to those who value folklore and heritage but also want quality, Swiss‑made craftsmanship at accessible prices. Brand ambassadors include Swiss athletes and traditional champions, reinforcing the tie to authentic Swiss identity and contemporary lifestyles.
1938
Founding of the Trauffer family’s original wooden‑toy business (Trauffer Holzspielwaren) in Hofstetten‑bei‑Brienz by Alfred and Rosa Trauffer.
2008
Marc A. Trauffer takes over the Trauffer family business in its third generation, transforming it into a modern Swiss brand.
2021
Trauffer Watches concept launched: Marc A. Trauffer teams with François Boegli and Central Watch Sàrl in Courrendlin to co‑develop watch line from sketch to final product.
FOUNDED
1938

Tudor is a luxury Swiss watch brand that was established in 1926 by Hans Wilsdorf, the founder of Rolex. With a rich history and timeless appeal, Tudor continues to push the boundaries of watchmaking excellence with every new collection.

URWERK is a Swiss watch brand that stands at the crossroads of art and engineering, crafting avant-garde timepieces that challenge conventional watch design. Renowned for their futuristic approach, URWERK's watches are distinguished by their unique satellite complication and orbiting hour modules, a hallmark of the brand’s innovation. Embodying a blend of high-quality craftsmanship and daring creativity, URWERK appeals to collectors and enthusiasts who seek out watches that make a bold statement.

Union Glashütte
History
ARTICLES
Union Glashütte is an affordable-luxury German watch brand rooted in the historic watchmaking town of Glashütte, Saxony. Since its founding in 1893 by Johannes Dürrstein, the brand has sought to make fine mechanical watches that bring Glashütte craftsmanship to a broader audience.
Known for blending classic, retro-modern styling with practical design, Union Glashütte combines traditionally finished mechanical movements with user-friendly aesthetics. Each timepiece is assembled, regulated and finished by hand on site in Glashütte. Though it uses Swiss-origin movements in many models, each is individually adjusted, decorated and inspected under strict German precision standards—and bears the legally protected “Made in Glashütte” designation.
1893
Founded by Johannes Dürrstein in Glashütte; first Grand Complication presented at Chicago World Fair.
1899
Relocated to a modern production facility at Hauptstraße 41 in Glashütte.
1900
Completed the ‘Universaluhr’ (Universal Watch) with alarm and complications; expanded into observation watches.
1936
Original Uhrenfabrik Union removed from commercial register amid economic upheaval.
1996
Union Uhrenfabrik GmbH re‑founded as a brand under Glashütte Original.
1997
Returned to market with Glashütte‑Original‑designed wristwatch collection and full vertical integration.
2000
Acquired by Swatch Group AG; Union placed under same group as Glashütte Original.
2008
Brand repositioned as independent with its own ateliers and dedicated mechanical collections.
2012
Opened new production facility at Frühlingsweg 5 in Glashütte.
2018
Celebrated 125‑year anniversary; special exhibition by German Watch Museum and release of limited ‘1893 Johannes Dürrstein’ edition.
FOUNDED
1893
HEADQUARTERS
Saxony, Germany

UNDONE
UNDONE Official
History
ARTICLES
Undone is an innovative watch brand that merges retro aesthetics, quality mechanics, and accessible customization to create unique timepieces that reflect the wearer’s individuality. Founded in 2014 in Hong Kong, Undone has gained a cult following for its direct-to-consumer approach, cutting out middlemen to offer well-crafted mechanical and quartz watches at prices far below traditional luxury brands. Undone was born from the idea that watches should be more than just accessories—they should be an extension of personal style. Unlike mass-produced brands, Undone allows customers to customize nearly every detail of their watch, from dials, hands, and colors to movement types and straps. This ensures each piece is one-of-a-kind, yet remains affordable compared to high-end bespoke watchmaking.

Universal
History
ARTICLES
Numa Emile Descombes and Ulysse Georges Perret founded Universal Genève in 1894 in Locle, Neuchâtel, Switzerland. Between the 1930s and 1960s, the company, operating under the name Universal Watch Co. Ltd., saw significant growth under the leadership of Raoul Perret, the founder’s son. The watches, signed “Universal Genève,” became renowned for their elegance and technical sophistication. This era gave rise to iconic models such as the Compax, Aero-Compax, and Tri-Compax (1930-40), the Polerouter (1954), and the Microtor UG 66 (1966). In the 2005 edition of Baselworld, Universal Genève introduced two new collections, Okeanos for men and Anthea for women, which cleverly reinterpreted the brand’s design while honoring its heritage. The 2006 models continued this tradition, and Universal Genève marked its resurgence with the introduction of its own manufactured movement, the MICROTOR UG 100.
1894
The talented Numa watchmakers Emile Descombes and Ulysse Georges Perret created a company specializing in the manufacture of complicated watches. Sales are growing quickly and each creation is received with enthusiasm.
1897
When Descombes died, Perret joined forces with Louis Edouard Berthoud to manufacture pocket watches. In 1898, they presented a chronograph with a 30-minute counter — the Universal Watch Extra — then created their first wrist chronograph in 1917.
1934
The company is renamed Universal Watch, moves to Geneva and experiences impressive international growth. A succession of elegant and complex models – Compax, Aero-Compax and Tri-Compax (1930-40), Polerouter (1954) and Microtor UG66 (1966) – forever mark their era.
1960
Faithful to its taste for innovation, in 1975 Universal Genève launched the thinnest quartz caliber in the world. During the 1980s and 90s, the brand enjoyed an impressive international reputation and began to be represented in Southeast Asia, the Middle East and Italy.
2005
While the 2005 and 2006 models reinterpret the design of the house in harmony with the Universal Genève spirit, the arrival of the Microtor UG100 and the mass production of the UG101 caliber launched in 2007 strongly confirm the renewal of the brand.
2008
On the occasion of Baseworld 2008 and 80 years after the launch of its first reversible watch — the Cabriolet — Universal Genève presents the Microtor Cabriolet, the first in the shape of a watch keeper (since the revival of the brand) distinguished by a spirit bold and contemporary.
2024
Partners Group, has announced its acquisition of Universal Genève
2026
The brand will be officially relaunched.
FOUNDED
1894
HEADQUARTERS
Geneva, Switzerland

Unison Watches is a design-forward Canadian watch brand that blends minimalist aesthetics with modern functionality. Founded by a product designer passionate about horology, Unison aims to offer fresh and original timepieces that stand out in the crowded watch market. Their design philosophy emphasizes bold, conversation-starting pieces that challenge conventional watch design, aiming to create their own legacy rather than relying on inherited heritage or inspiration from unrelated industries.
The brand's flagship model, the Model Orbital, exemplifies this approach. It features a slim, curved 316L stainless steel case, 100-meter water resistance, and a unique dial design illuminated by Swiss Super-LumiNova® BGW9 lume.
