Vacheron Constantin
VACHERON CONSTANTIN Official
History
ARTICLES
Vacheron Constantin is a Swiss luxury watch manufacturer founded in 1755, making it one of the oldest continuously operating watchmakers in the world. The company is known for its exceptional quality and craftsmanship, and it is considered one of the most prestigious and exclusive brands in the watch industry.
Vacheron Constantin watches are highly sought after by collectors and enthusiasts for their intricate mechanical movements, elegant designs, and rich heritage. The company’s watch collections include the Patrimony, Traditionnelle, Overseas, and Historiques, among others.
Vacheron Constantin has a long history of innovation and technical achievement in watchmaking, including developing the first watch with a precision rate of one second per day in 1955, and introducing the first watch with a digital display in 1972.
The brand has a reputation for producing highly complicated watches, including perpetual calendars, minute repeaters, and tourbillons, which require exceptional skill and expertise to create. Vacheron Constantin also produces limited edition and one-of-a-kind timepieces, often featuring intricate designs and rare materials.
Vacheron Constantin has a strong commitment to excellence and has received numerous awards and accolades for its watches over the years. It remains a highly respected and coveted brand among watch collectors and enthusiasts around the world.

1819

1880

1955
Vacheron Constantin introduces the first watch with a precision rate of one second per day
1996
Vacheron Constantin becomes part of the Richemont Group
2016
Vacheron Constantin introduces the Celestia, a highly complicated astronomical watch with 23 different functions.
2025
The brand celebrates its 270th anniversary.

History
ARTICLES
Ventura is renowned for its groundbreaking designs and exceptional craftsmanship. The "watch Flemming Bo Hansen" was selected for the permanent design collection at MoMA and received the prestigious Red Dot "Best of Best" Design Award. Ventura's timepieces, including the SPARC MGS and SPARC Sigma MGS, have consistently earned international accolades for outstanding design. Collaborating with accomplished designers and unrestrained by standardized movements, Ventura has developed a unique design language. The brand's quest for functionality, precision, and quality is rooted in its experience with traditional mechanical chronometers, highlighted by the v-matic collection. To foster innovation, Ventura shifted focus to high-tech electronic timepieces, introducing the world's first Automatic Digital watch, SPARC. Utilizing exclusive materials like Titanox and Durinox ensures product longevity, while the EasySkroll operating system, with its intuitive single scroller, sets a new benchmark for high-value digital timepieces.

Vortic
VORTIC WATCH COMPANY Official
History
ARTICLES
Vortic Watch Company salvages and restores antique American pocket watches and turns them into
                    one-of-a-kind wristwatches. Each watch tells a story. The old saying “they don’t make them like they
                    used to,” is our motto. We quite literally preserve pieces of American history one watch at a
                    time.
Every Vortic watch starts with the heart of an original pocket watch. The dial, the hands, and the movement are painstakingly and meticulously restored by our team of watchmakers. Next, we select the case, crown, and leather design best fitting of that little historical engine. Finally, the metal components are machined from solid blocks of titanium, bronze, or steel in our Fort Collins, Colorado manufacturing facility.
Vortic Watch Company started by asking what we thought was a simple question: “Can you make a watch in America?” Fast forward ten years, and we’re still one of the only American watch manufacturers, and certainly one of the few that can proudly stamp “Made in USA” on the back of every wristwatch.
Our small but dedicated team of watchmakers pours into every piece, ensuring that each Vortic watch is a masterpiece in its own right. We only produce a few hundred watches annually, and although most of our pieces are unique, we also make a limited edition called the Military Edition that you just have to see.
FOUNDED
2013
HEADQUARTERS
Fort Collins, USA

History
ARTICLES
Von Doren is a Norwegian heritage-inspired watch brand specializing in elegant mechanical and quartz wristwatches that reflect both Norwegian storytelling and high-quality Swiss watchmaking. The brand was founded in 2016 by Øyvind VonDoren Asbjørnsen, a filmmaker whose passion for timepieces began when he inherited his great-grandfather’s Swiss watch — an experience that sparked a lifelong fascination with horology and design. Von Doren’s timepieces are designed in Norway and built with Swiss movements, combining clean Scandinavian aesthetics with reliable technical foundations.
The brand’s design philosophy emphasizes timelessness, understated sophistication, and narrative depth, often drawing inspiration from Norwegian nature, culture, film, and industrial heritage. Collections like URÆD GMT — a modern sports watch honoring a real Norwegian Atlantic crossing — and playful lines such as Runde Candy, inspired by the puffins on the island of Runde, reflect this blend of story and style. Von Doren also creates watches inspired by iconic cultural motifs like Il Tempo Gigante from Norway’s beloved film Flåklypa Grand Prix.
2016
Von Doren Watch Company AS was founded by Norwegian filmmaker Øyvind VonDoren Asbjørnsen, inspired by his great-grandfather’s Swiss watch and a lifelong passion for watches.
2017
Von Doren gained early recognition for its *Grandmaster* series, including special editions like the Norway Chess watches commissioned for the Altibox Norway Chess tournament.
2020
Launch and crowdfunding success of the *URÆD Automatic* — a Swiss-made automatic sports watch inspired by Norwegian explorer Ole Brude’s legendary Atlantic voyage, widely supported by the watch community.
2023
Introduction of the *Tandberg* model, paying homage to Norwegian electronics pioneer Vebjørn Tandberg, bridging industrial innovation with wristwatch design.
2025
Expansion of narrative-driven timepieces including the *URÆD GMT*, a dual-time model paying tribute to historic Norwegian adventures, reflecting continued evolution of Von Doren’s design language.
FOUNDED

Wancher
Wander Watch Official
Loading videos...
0/0
History
ARTICLES
Wancher Watch is a Japanese boutique watch brand that blends traditional Japanese craftsmanship with contemporary timepiece design. Founded with a deep appreciation for time and craft, the brand focuses on creating watches that reflect Japan’s artistic heritage — especially through decorative art forms such as urushi lacquer, maki-e, raden, and other traditional techniques that appear in their high-art dial work. This unique fusion of traditional lacquer artistry with modern mechanical watchmaking gives Wancher watches both aesthetic depth and cultural significance.
The design philosophy centers on celebrating cultural identity and craftsmanship while making timepieces that are wearable and meaningful. Wancher’s collections — such as the Dream Watch Urushi Dial series — are crafted with attention to both visual artistry and performance, often featuring premium Japanese automatic movements (e.g., Miyota 9015) and 316L stainless steel cases paired with sapphire crystals. The watches aim to evoke a connection between artistic tradition and modern life, appealing to buyers who value story, culture, and handmade quality in addition to wearable watch functionality.
Wancher’s target audience includes enthusiasts of artistic and culturally inspired timepieces, those drawn to Japanese traditional art forms, and buyers seeking a standout piece that differs from typical Swiss or mainstream microbrand aesthetics. With an emphasis on handcrafted dials and artistic techniques uncommon in most watch brands, Wancher positions itself as a brand that offers unique artistic value paired with practical performance.
2005
Wancher Watch was founded by Taizo Okagaki, originally producing outdoor and performance watches for the Japanese domestic market, laying the groundwork for future artistic and culturally inspired timepieces.
2019
Wancher established its official online presence and expanded internationally, gaining global recognition beyond Japan through its website and social media platforms.
2021
The Dream Watch Collection — featuring Urushi lacquer dials crafted by master artisans — was launched via the Japanese crowdfunding platform Makuake, marking the brand’s shift toward traditional craft-centric watches.
2022
Wancher released the Dream Watch Moonphase Kaguya on Kickstarter, featuring mother-of-pearl raden and Japanese cultural themes, cementing its presence in the international watch market.
2023
The Dream Watch Urushi Dial Collection expanded with multiple traditional techniques including Hibi Nuri, Kinpaku Nuri, and Raden, in collaboration with artisans like Master Yoko Hirota.
FOUNDED
2005
HEADQUARTERS
Bungotakada, Oita Prefecture, Japan

Wancherwatch.com

Watchcraft
History
ARTICLES
Watchcraft is a boutique art-horology brand founded and hand-operated by Eduardo Milieris in his New York City studio. Established in 1994 in Long Island City, NY, it specializes in hand-wrought, limited‑edition timepieces, bracelets, and Apple Watch bands. Each piece is sculpted from solid metals—brass, copper, sterling silver—oxidized, engraved, and then individually hand-painted, making every watch truly unique. The design philosophy centers on retro-modern fusion: embracing industrial, urban aesthetics with evolving metal patinas that age fluidly with wear. The brand melds artistic craftsmanship—welding, sculpting, painting—with functional reliability, powered by robust Citizen quartz movements.

Vulcain
History
ARTICLES
Vulcain is a historic Swiss watchmaker specializing in mechanical wristwatches, celebrated for inventing the first commercial mechanical alarm complication. Known as “The Watch for Presidents,” the brand combines vintage-inspired design—such as mid‑century chronographs and diver models, with modern precision and reliable alarm mechanisms.
Their design philosophy centers on retro‑modern fusion: timeless case shapes (e.g., the 39 mm Skindiver and Monopusher Heritage chronographs) meld with contemporary materials and in‑house or Swiss movements. Their target audience includes horology enthusiasts and collectors who appreciate craftsmanship, mechanical complexity (especially the alarm function), and a sense of history, building loyalty through thoughtful functionality and an evocative legacy.

Whitby
WHITBY WATCH CO. Official
History
ARTICLES
Our journey begins on the shores of Lake Ontario in Whitby, a town rich with history. It was home to Canada's first clock manufacturing company and served as a training ground for World War II spies, doubling as a communications hub for secret messages and code-breaking. It was also the site where the world’s fastest jet plane was tested and ultimately sunk. This rich history inspired us to create watch packages that tell stories steeped in Canadian heritage.
At Whitby Watch Co., we are dedicated to crafting quality watches infused with stories and inspiration from Canadian people, places, and history. Our goal is to forge a horological connection to a nation brimming with adventure. Since our founding in 2018, we take pride in assembling special edition watch lines in Canada, utilizing well-priced movements and local suppliers to create unique timepiece packages.
FOUNDED
2015

Whitby Canada

Watchpeople
History
ARTICLES
WATCHPEOPLE is a Swiss-made, inclusive watch brand created by Ice-Watch, designed for those who value lightness, freedom, and originality. Rather than following traditional market conventions, the brand emphasizes personal values—its tagline articulates that wearing a WATCHPEOPLE timepiece means "regaining power over time, objects and watches," and represents that "freedom is the only luxury". The brand stands out through vibrant, expressive design combined with accessible pricing—starting from approximately €99, which positions it as "Swiss-Made, except the price". Collections like WP1 offer a variety of materials (steel, plastic, or combinations) and bold, colorful versions, while the WP2 models exude a modern, confident aesthetic with refinements such as a 45 mm case and sapphire glass—highlighting both craftsmanship and originality.

Wenger

History
ARTICLES
Wenger is a heritage Swiss brand in watches and travel/business gear, with roots stretching back to 1893 in the Jura region of Switzerland. Originally famed as one of two official makers of the Swiss Army Knife, Wenger has since refocused its identity post‑2005 as a specialist in precision watches and intelligently designed luggage and backpacks—offering Swiss craftsmanship at accessible price points.
Known for its reliable, functional and stylish design language, Wenger marries a retro‑modern aesthetic with practical features—for instance, ergonomic compartments, durable materials, and clean Swiss design influences. Its products include collections like City Classic, Vintage Sport, Seaforce in watches, and well‑engineered business/backpack gear like the Pegasus, Synergy and City Traveler series. The brand targets active urban professionals and travelers who value everyday functionality combined with polished, minimalist styling—a demographic that appreciates Swiss reliability without a luxury‑price.
1893
Founded as Paul Boéchat & Cie knife factory in Courtételle near Delémont in the Swiss Jura region.
1897
Theo Wenger joins as leader and renames the firm Wenger & Cie, later Wenger S.A.
1908
Awarded half of the official Swiss Army knife contract alongside Victorinox.
1997
Wenger begins producing Swiss‑made watches, expanding beyond cutlery.
2005
Acquired by Victorinox after financial difficulties, but retained distinct identity for watches and travel gear.
2013
Victorinox integrates Wenger knife lines into its own Victorinox collection; Wenger focuses solely on watches and travel/business gear.
2016
Brand relaunched at Baselworld, adopting slogan 'A Swiss Company Since 1893' and emphasising value‑driven Swiss design.
2017
Introduction of the Wenger‑branded 'Wenger Red' Swiss Army Knife, produced in Delémont under Victorinox.
FOUNDED
1893
HEADQUARTERS
Canton of Jura, Switzerland

Wenger.ch/global/en

History
ARTICLES
Worden Watch is a design-forward microbrand crafting bold, modern timepieces from a small studio in Guelph, Ontario. Founded by Industrial Designer Tyler Worden, the brand brings an artistic, story driven approach to contemporary watchmaking. Each watch design is based around a single idea; a “theme” that shapes the colors, materials, and tone of the entire drop. The result is a limited edition series of wrist-sized sculptures; playful in spirit, serious in execution. Worden Watch speaks to collectors who value originality, dependability and scarcity in what they wear.

History
ARTICLES
WISE is a Thai watch brand specializing in high-quality timepieces that blend classic aesthetics with modern functionality. Established in 2008, the company has garnered attention for its meticulous craftsmanship and attention to detail. Each watch is designed in Thailand and hand-assembled by skilled watchmakers in Bangkok, ensuring a personalized touch in every piece.
WISE offers a diverse range of collections, including the ADAMASCUS series, known for its durable 904L stainless steel cases and limited-edition models featuring materials like meteorite and 24K gold-filled dials. The HITMAN collection features robust designs with options like full lume dials and FKM rubber straps, catering to both casual and professional tastes. Additionally, the ASURA series introduces Swiss automatic movements, appealing to enthusiasts seeking precision and reliability.

WISE was established in Bangkok, Thailand, focusing on high-quality, hand-assembled timepieces.

2020
Release of the ASURA series, incorporating Swiss automatic movements.
2023

Yema is a renowned French watch brand celebrated for its innovative craftsmanship and timeless designs. Founded in 1948 by Henry Louis Belmont, Yema embodies the spirit of adventure, creating timepieces tailored to explorers, divers, and aviation enthusiasts. With iconic models like the Superman, designed for professional divers, and the Spacegraf, which has ventured into space, Yema blends heritage with cutting-edge technology. Proudly made in France, the brand reflects precision, durability, and a passion for pushing boundaries. Whether conquering the depths of the ocean or soaring to new heights, Yema watches are a testament to French horological artistry.

Zodiac
ZODIAC WATCHES Official
History
ARTICLES
Zodiac is a renowned watch brand known for its innovative designs and precision craftsmanship. Founded in 1882 by Ariste Calame in Switzerland, Zodiac has a rich heritage of creating high-quality timepieces. The brand is particularly famous for its dive watches, including the iconic Sea Wolf, which was one of the first purpose-built diving watches introduced in 1953. Zodiac watches are celebrated for their robust construction, stylish aesthetics, and reliable performance, making them a favorite among watch enthusiasts and collectors. Each Zodiac watch reflects the brand's commitment to combining traditional Swiss watchmaking techniques with contemporary design elements.

Zenith
ZENITH WATCHES Official
History
ARTICLES
Zenith is a luxury watch brand based in Switzerland. The company was founded in 1865 by Georges Favre-Jacot in Le Locle. Zenith is known for its high-quality watches and its use of advanced technology and materials. The brand is particularly well-known for its El Primero movement, which is considered one of the most accurate and reliable chronograph movements in the world. Zenith is also known for its wide range of watch styles, including sport watches, pilot watches, and dress watches, and its use of in-house movements. The brand is also known for its commitment to traditional watchmaking techniques and its collaborations with other luxury brands and personalities. Zenith watches are highly sought-after by collectors and enthusiasts for their precision, reliability, and craftsmanship.
1865

2018
